Description: Red-painted walls and light wooden flooring sets the mood for dishes from the Iberian peninsula. From tapas to dessert, the menu is Spanish.

Name: Diergardts Kühler Grund
Description: Established in 1904, this family-run restaurant is now in its fourth generation, with Philipp Diergardt now at the helm. He is passionate about cooking up traditional, seasonal cuisine that draws on locally sourced ingredients. The extensive menu is extremely appealing, making it difficult to choose. In addition to the fairly priced set menu ("Heimatmenü"), there are also classics such as bouillabaisse or Wiener schnitzel, as well as weekly specials that change with the seasons and dishes for two or more people. Wine connoisseurs will find plenty to enjoy here, and all reasonably priced. The fine selection of high-end German wines and French vintages is of particular note. Then there is the tasteful country house ambience, gorgeous terrace and attentive service.

Name: mural
Description: The location is special in its own right: This restaurant is tucked away inside MUCA (Museum of Urban and Contemporary Art), and this is the inspiration behind the interior design. The open kitchen allows you to watch the chefs prepare their modern and creative four-course set menu – available with meat and fish or in a vegetarian version. Sourced within the region, the ingredients are of outstanding quality. The high-end wine list is wide-ranging and features some excellent natural wines, too. There are also wine or alcohol-free pairings to go with the set menu. Service is friendly and personable. The restaurant is also open for lunch – dishes can be ordered à la carte or as a set menu comprising two to four courses.

Name: DAS GOLDSTEIN BY GOLLNER'S
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: It's quite unexpected to come across such a chic restaurant in a remote wooded area like this. But the journey is absolutely worth making. Set in the former rifle club house about 5km from the city centre, this establishment boasts a tastefully sleek and upscale interior. It serves freshly prepared, predominantly Austrian cuisine, but the menu also features international dishes, some with Asian influences. The walk-in wine cellar and extensive wine list are a draw in themselves.

Name: Pure Wine & Food
Description: The atmosphere here is pleasingly laid-back and trendy – in summer, the lovely terrace in front of the building gives you that holiday feeling. Using the freshest ingredients, the kitchen team serves up pared-back, modern Mediterranean cuisine. Diners choose between the "Chef's Choice" set menu, a vegetarian set menu and à la carte dishes. Given the restaurant's name, it is no surprise that wine plays an important role. The friendly and adept waitstaff will gladly recommend a suitable pairing for any dish. The terrific wine list comprises over 250 (organic) labels – also available to take away. Tip: Book a wine tasting.

Name: POTS
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Relaxed, chic and stylish! The eye-catching features of the Ritz-Carlton's restaurant are its striking decor and large open kitchen. The chefs put a modern spin on traditional German cuisine in dishes such as Königsberger Klopse (meatballs in a white wine sauce) with capers and yellow beetroot. There are also options for sharing. Alternatively, take a punt on the surprise menu. The lunch deal is also popular. Friendly and adept service.

Name: Schloss Monaise
Description: With Hubert Scheid's cuisine, this place is nothing short of a culinary institution in the region. His signature style comprises pleasantly straightforward dishes made from excellent ingredients – served à la carte or as a set menu. The restaurant occupies stylish, high-ceilinged rooms that channel the stately flair of these premises dating back to 1783 – once a summer residence. Or you can dine on the wonderful terrace with a view of the surrounding nature and the Moselle. It's also worth taking a look at the wine list, which features many a big name.

Name: Sansibar
Description: A restaurant with cult status! This smart beach cabin is a much sought-after place to eat. It takes five minutes to reach it on foot through the dunes – or you can use the shuttle service. Fancy a Sansibar classic such as rice pudding with red fruit jelly? Or perhaps you'd prefer something more sophisticated? Superb wine list. No reservations taken at lunchtime. There is a playground for children next to the restaurant.

Name: Gourmetrestaurant "1751"
Description: Located in the heart of the city, in a wine merchant's house dating back over 250 years, this restaurant is considered one of the finest in the region. This delightful historical setting boasts a beautifully restored barrel-vaulted ceiling. The team led by chef Marcel Kube serves up modern cuisine, drawing on seasonal influences and using mostly regional ingredients. They serve an ambitious set menu that while plant-forward also includes one fish and one meat option. Choose between the five- or seven-course format; it is possible to add an extra course. The wine list includes a few rare gems! Weinhaus Uhle hotel has tastefully designed guestrooms. Incidentally, the property has been listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 2024.

Name: Weisser Bock
Description: Pretty details such as wood panelling and historical photos contribute to the cosy atmosphere in this traditional establishment. The kitchen serves up a fine selection of dishes made from regional and international ingredients, from which you can choose a three- to six-course menu. The à la carte menu includes appetising dishes such as "beef tartare, anchovies, brioche, caviar" and "turbot, wild cauliflower, capers". The hotel of the same name has comfortable and tastefully decorated guestrooms. The central location is another trump card.

Name: Die Kurfürstenstube
Description: A classic of Heidelberg's dining scene! In the restaurant of the stylish grand hotel Der Europäische Hof Heidelberg, with its individually designed guestrooms and suites, the mighty coffered ceiling and wall panelling with beautiful inlays preserve a piece of history. The cuisine is based on the classics, and only the very best ingredients are used in the ambitious dishes. Choose à la carte or go for the set menu, available in various lengths. The wine list, specialising in German, French and Italian labels, is also excellent. The highly friendly, professional and attentive service is in keeping with the style of the place. In the summer months, a slightly simpler menu is served in the "Sommerrestaurant", which has a terrace and a pleasant, airy atmosphere.

Name: Magnifico da Umberto
Description: Umberto Scuccia is a well-known figure in Heilbronn. Here on the 12th floor of the WTZ Tower he runs an upmarket, minimalist-style restaurant offering both pleasantly pared-down Italian cuisine with the accent on top-quality produce and a wonderful view of the city.

Name: Christophorus
Description: For car enthusiasts who are fans of quality cuisine: here you can take in views of the Porsche Museum or Porscheplatz as you tuck into Mediterranean and international fare such as pluma bellota Ibérico taco with pineapple and pink peppercorn chutney, or barbecued US prime beef. You can also opt for a six- or nine-course tasting menu. The open kitchen offers some fascinating insights. Impressive wine list comprising over 500 items. A lift takes you straight from the car park to the restaurant on the third floor.

Name: Hugenhof
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Credit cards not accepted,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list
Description: Inside Hugenhof is every bit as attractive as its exterior suggests with its lovely high ceilings, cosy old beams and a roaring open fire in winter. Every evening brings a different 4-course menu, which chef Klaus Ditz will announce at your table. This is accompanied by expert wine selections from his fellow chef and sommelier. The rooms are simple but smart.

Name: Schloss Niederweis
Description: This tasteful restaurant is housed in the converted corn barn of a magnificent Baroque castle dating from 1751. The decor is a fascinating blend of rustic cachet and modern design – the open truss roof is striking and the two historical carriages on the wall are certainly eye-catching! Sample the flavoursome dishes à la carte or as a set menu, washed down by your choice from the terrific wine list that includes rare finds, including by the glass. Charming garden, a banquet hall and a registry office. On a practical note: easy parking.

Name: ZweiSinn Meiers | Bistro
Description: If a really nice bistro in Nuremberg is what you are looking for, then this is the place for you! A pleasant, refined atmosphere, friendly service, and good food await you here. The modern and Mediterranean-inspired à la carte selection is appealing, and there is also a caviar menu. At lunchtime, the reasonably priced daily special is always in demand. Tip: On Wednesday and Thursday evenings, selected dishes from the menu of the fine dining restaurant, which is located in the same building, go into a three-course business menu (sparkling wine included). They also offer fine wines by the glass and top-drawer alcohol-free alternatives.

Name: Steinheuers Landgasthof Poststuben
Description: This attractive alternative to the fine dining restaurant also impresses with sophisticated cuisine made with excellent ingredients. The classic menu incorporates regional influences alongside international flavours. This results in a diverse range of dishes, such as pan-fried veal black pudding on hispi cabbage with fried onions and bacon, or tuna carpaccio with a soy and sesame vinaigrette and tuna tartare on a sushi rice base, as well as fine cuts of local rabbit on spring vegetables with gnocchi, or Zwiebelrostbraten (sirloin steak with crispy fried onions and gravy), Brussels sprouts and Schupfnudeln (finger-shaped potato dumplings). The well-stocked wine list is the same as in the fine dining restaurant. Dine in the cosy interior, or head out to the delightful leafy terrace. For overnight stays, there are double rooms available in the main building, as well as a comfortable guesthouse.

Name: Zum Krug
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Credit cards not accepted,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: This place has a long history that stretches back to 1720, and the Laufer family has now been at the helm for three generations. Behind the gorgeous historical façade, the cosy dining spaces exude traditional charm. Theregional and international cuisine takes its cue from the seasons, and the menu includes dishes such as a Sauerbraten (marinated pot roast) of organic pasture-fed beef with potato dumplings and cranberries. The friendly and professional waitstaff will be happy to recommend fine Rheingau wines from the estate to accompany your meal. Delightful courtyard terrace. Smart guestrooms – some are especially modern.

Name: Kronenschlösschen
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Here, diners can sit beneath a stylishly painted historical ceiling or out on the pretty terrace with adjoining garden. The kitchen draws on traditional French cuisine to propose creative dishes in the form of a four- to six-course menu. Plus the wine list is one of the best in Germany. Alternatively, try the bistro. Guests can also stay overnight in the hotel of the same name in cosy, well-appointed guestrooms.

Name: Weinrestaurant Turmschänke
Description: In this restaurant in the Nicolaiturm, the lovely historical details, such as paintings and original furniture dating from 1912, foster a rustic yet elegant feel. The waitstaff are pleasingly laid-back and down-to-earth. Meticulously prepared seasonal dishes are served as a set menu or à la carte; mouthwatering options from the barbecue are also available. Complete with helpful descriptions, the wine list comprises some 140 items – most are from the region, but international labels also feature. Special wine evenings are also hosted here.

Name: Taverna & Trattoria Palio
Description: Palio offers fresh Italian fare from the open kitchen in a relaxed trattoria-style atmosphere – you simply have to try one of the pasta dishes! The seasonal set menus are also interesting. There’s also a lovely terrace in the shade of some old chestnut trees.

Name: Redüttchen
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: What was once the gardener's cottage at the 18C La Redoute ballroom and concert hall is a feel-good place that has won over more than its fair share of regulars. The restaurant's appeal lies not only in its extremely charming ambience, but also in Matthias Pietsch's innovative take on modern cuisine and the experienced, courteous and cordial service. The pleasant dining area is laid out over two levels, and the terrace to the rear of the building is also a lovely spot.

Name: Wappen-Saal
Description: This imposing castle estate provides a truly unique setting for dining. Before taking a seat in the stately Wappensaal in the Schlosshotel at Burg Schlitz, take a leisurely stroll along the Goetheweg. Experience the historical ambiance of this restaurant as you savour a four- to six-course menu of ambitious classic cuisine beneath an impressive high ceiling. The charming and experienced staff will be happy to help you choose the perfect accompaniment to your meal from the well-curated wine list.

Name: Westphalenhof
Description: This stylish and sophisticated restaurant is somewhat tucked away in a residential area. Here, the Westphal brothers – one is the chef, the other the sommelier – delight diners with delicious food and excellent wine recommendations. The menu features international dishes that take their cue from the seasons, such as confit halibut fillet in nut butter, potato scales, cauliflower cream, dried tomatoes and Sardinian pasta. The walk-in wine cellar accommodates 120 different labels. The restaurant is also open for lunch on Saturdays and Sundays.

Name: XO Seafoodbar
Description: As well as haebel, just across the street, Fabio Haebel runs this trendy, laid-back establishment in the heart of St Pauli. Occupying two floors, the restaurant has an agreeably lively atmosphere. Upstairs there are also a few seats at a counter facing the open kitchen. The food is modern and seasonal, with a focus on seafood specialties, including oysters, monkfish schnitzel and grilled prawns, and of course the famous "Pasta Krabbe". Alternatively, you could opt for "Chef's Choice" – a selection of items picked from the menu by the kitchen team. Tip: Treat yourself to a cocktail at the small bar. In summer, head for the terrace and soak up the buzzy atmosphere.

Name: Seehalde
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Credit cards not accepted,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Run by the Gruler brothers, this restaurant boasts a stunning lakeside location – tables on the terrace are much coveted in summer! The fresh and ambitious cuisine is also a major draw. The menu takes its cue from the region and the seasons with a classic bent. Fish from Lake Constance is also served and is even the focus of a three- or four-course set menu for two people. There is also a set menu based on regional classics. Alternatively, take your pick from the appealing à la carte selection. The well-curated wine list includes some gems, too. Well-drilled front-of house team. Tip: Take some homemade bouillabaisse in a jar with you to enjoy at home. There are well-kept guestrooms as well - most of them look out to the lake.

Name: Meister Lampe
Description: It's a real pleasure to eat here, and there's a good reason for that: chef-patron Daniel Stübler clearly puts his heart and soul into running his cosy little restaurant. He cooks up excellent regional and international dishes, and also does the serving himself – not only is he highly accomplished at what he does, he is also an extremely cordial host and single-handedly creates a fantastic atmosphere! The carefully curated wine list is also remarkable, featuring wines from Baden-Württemberg, Baden and the Palatinate only.

Name: Schlossanger Alp
Description: Set against the backdrop of the Allgäu Alps, this hotel has tasteful, comfortable guestrooms and a lovely, inviting restaurant in which cosy Alpine vibes meet elegant table settings. Affording stunning views, the terrace is a wonderful spot for a meal in summer! The menu is varied: at lunchtime (from 1pm) there is a three-course set menu and à la carte dishes, including "small bites to share"; in the evening the selection includes their "Signature Menu", as well as dishes ranging from braised vegetable ravioli to lobster, and venison loin to Gröstl (pan-fried potatoes). The extensive wine list encompasses a fine selection.

Name: Kinfelts Kitchen & Wine
Description: Located near the Elbphilharmonie concert hall, this restaurant embodies the quintessential HafenCity style. The interior is simple yet chic; the large windows allow natural light to stream into the space and afford compelling views of the Elbe canal. Kirill Kinfelt, who made his reputation at Trüffelschwein, serves up classically rooted contemporary cuisine made from top-notch ingredients, eg king prawn with buttermilk, chilli and Thai basil, or monkfish with fregola sarda, radish and herbs. An outstanding selection of international wines and expert recommendations. For dinner reservations, choose between two seatings.

Name: Witwenball
Description: What makes this deluxe bistro in Hamburg's bustling Schanzenviertel so beloved? A perfect blend of cuisine, service and atmosphere. Once a dance hall, this place now exudes a laid-back yet lively energy, and feels at once trendy and chic with its decorative wine racks – the white marble counter in the middle of the space is a striking feature. The delicious contemporary food incorporates regional, seasonal, and international influences. Order à la carte or compile your own four-course "Your Choice" set menu, with a fish, a meat or vegetarian option for the main course. Wiener schnitzel is also always available on Sundays. The impressive wine list comprises over 500 items, and the staff provide expert guidance. Alternatively, you could have a cocktail. Lovely pavement terrace.

Name: Saittavini
Description: Combining a bistro, wine bar and delicatessen, this friendly restaurant serves Italian food (including a lovely small antipasto counter). They also sell over a 1 000 different wines in the wine shop and a range of Italian specialities in the delicatessen.

Name: Kucher's Weinwirtschaft
Description: The mismatched antique tables and chairs, the pretty decoration and the almost familial atmosphere lend this place a charming feel. The Swabian-style sour kidneys with sautéed potatoes have been on the menu here forever – a classic with its own loyal following. In addition, the menu includes contemporary dishes such as braised Ellscheid lemon chicken with Mediterranean vegetables. Most of the meat used by Florian Kucher and his team to create their seasonal regional fare comes from the Eifel area. Be sure to peruse the wine list – wine is a passion of senior chef Martin Kucher and he will be happy to advise you.

Name: Restaurant 1718
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: At 1718, elegant period architectural elements blend beautifully with modern design. Part of Ketschauer Hof hotel, the restaurant sits across from the fine dining restaurant L.A. Jordan – separating them is a lovely terrace in the peaceful, verdant courtyard. On the menu, French and international cuisine are the order of the day, accompanied by a superb selection of wines from the Palatinate and beyond. Tip: The establishment runs a wine shop around the corner.

Name: Bergfriedel
Description: This establishment has been run by the same family with great dedication for years! The welcoming team serves delicious, fresh food that diners can enjoy as they take in the views over the Bühlertal. They follow a philosophy that is close to nature - and their own chickens in the garden fit in nicely. The wide-ranging menu includes traditional cuisine from Baden, vegetarian/vegan dishes and gourmet specialities. Add to that an extensive wine list. The chic restaurant extension is done out in a modern take on regional style. Pleasant overnight accommodation is available – the rooms and suites ("Natur-Zimmer" and "Natur-Suiten") are done out in locally sourced materials and are contemporary in their design.

Name: Koch und Kellner
Description: A cornerstone of the Nuremberg fine dining scene. The set menu, written up on a large board, comprises nine courses, but for those with a smaller appetite, it can also be shortened. The dishes are crafted from carefully selected ingredients and are quite aptly described as "modernised classics". Besides the chef, owner and waiter Frank Mackert is always on hand with his inimitable Franconian charm. Thanks to his extensive wine expertise, he can confidently guide you through the 500-label list. Clear lines, a warm colour palette and parquet flooring make for a lovely setting. By reservation only: Mon-Sat lunchtime (11.30am-2.30pm).

Name: Stüffel
Description: The riverside location right by the Isekai is every bit as appealing as the contemporary seasonal cuisine with Mediterranean and regional influences, made with choice ingredients. The menu includes, for example, Husum pasture-raised beef tartare, or poached cod with balsamic lentils, as well as classics such as beef fillet and lobster ravioli. The wine list is well chosen – the chef will be happy to guide you. You can eat in the stylish modern bistro or outside on the waterfront. There is also a wine bar in the building.

Name: Husar
Description: Charming dining rooms await behind the painted façade of this inn, which has stood for over 400 years. Here, diners are treated to attentive service as they tuck into classic cuisine. The dishes are packed with flavour and made with good-quality ingredients; attention is paid to seasonality, and a regional produce is given preferential treatment. Started in 1986, the Merget family business continues today, having resumed in 2004 after a brief hiatus.

Name: H'manns
Description: The Hegmanns run this genuinely charming restaurant that is a hit with diners. Only the best produce makes its way into the dishes, which are meticulously prepared to bring out the full flavour of the ingredients. On Fri-Sun, two four- to eight-course seasonal set menus, one of which is vegetarian, are served. On Mon, tapas menu only – to be washed down with something from the promising wine selection. Extremely friendly waitstaff. Tip: The chef's table is very close to the kitchen but sheltered from the thick of the action.

Name: Brauereigasthof Aying
Description: The restaurant is part of the picture-perfect Brauereigasthof Hotel Aying is a family-run establishment steeped in tradition and now run by the seventh generation. Diners are treated to warm hospitality in an upscale yet cosy setting. The terrace to the rear of the building is delightful in summer. The menu features exquisite regional and seasonal dishes, crafted from carefully selected produce of the highest quality – the restaurant has its own herd of Pinzgau beef cattle, as well as a hunter, who provides the game. In addition to the selection of fine wines, there are of course Ayinger beer specialities. Good event facilities. Comfortable and tastefully decorated guestrooms available for overnight stays.

Name: WEINrestaurant Ayler Kupp
Description: While you tuck into the fresh seasonal dishes, which the chefs here cook up with produce from the region, you can take in the vineyards and the lovely garden. There are two set menus, each made up of three to five courses; you can also choose individual dishes à la carte. The wine list includes Riesling from the neighbouring wine estate. They also offer accommodation in inviting, well-kept guestrooms in the hotel.

Name: SCHORN by Lukas Schild
Description: With Lukas Schild as chef-patron, this restaurant in the Bilk neighbourhood boasts a big name in the Düsseldorf fine dining scene. Select ingredients go into ambitious seasonal cooking – the dishes draw on the classics. The set menu comes in a four- to seven-course version, or you can take your pick from the à la carte selection. The team is friendly and knowledgeable – the chef comes to your table and talks you through the dishes. The appealing wine list comprises mostly German and French labels. The restaurant has a charming, intimate feel, with decorative features including an old stove, a wine barrel serving as a digestif table and modern paintings. The terrace is lovely in summer.

Name: Macionga
Description: This attractive, sleek restaurant located close to Ku'damm (Kurfürstendamm) is named after its owner – sommelier and winemaker André Macionga. In keeping with his passion for wine, the wine list is very nicely curated, and the restaurant also has its own wine shop. Chef Christopher Wecker builds on stints in various Tim Raue kitchens, successfully incorporating his own ideas. He rustles up modern dishes with a seasonal bent, such as "scallop, coconut, sweet and sour pumpkin, buttered dashi broth" or "chicken fricassee CW, truffle, black walnut, Madeira jus," not to mention his unique take on a Poire belle Hélène, the classic pear dessert. The portion size means you can try several different dishes – a concept that is entirely in keeping with modern trends and goes down very well with diners.

Name: Das Alte Haus
Description: Enrico Dunkel and his small restaurant have been a well-established part of the city's culinary landscape for years. His cooking style is ambitious, creative and draws on international influences. The set menu can comprise five, six or seven courses. Choose your accompaniment from the extensive selection of fine German wines. There is also a pairing menu. The interior is modern yet cosy. In summer, the pretty terrace beckons.

Name: Strandhaus
Description: A beach house (Strandhaus) right in the heart of Bonn – who would have thought? The delightful maritime decor and laid-back atmosphere transport you to a seaside retreat. Not forgetting the cosy secluded terrace. This is the perfect setting for a Mediterranean-influenced cuisine, with fish at the heart of it all. Using only the finest ingredients, the chef creates flavoursome dishes with a unique twist, available both à la carte and as a tasting menu. As well as fish, there are meat options and a dedicated vegetarian set menu.

Name: Bocca di Bacco
Description: On the outside, a smart historical façade, on the inside an attractively modern and elegant restaurant complete with bar and lounge. Visible through its glass panes, the kitchen turns out Italian cooking. Needless to say, the pasta is home-made. To be paired with one of their great Tuscan wines.

Name: Restaurant zum Bären
Description: The Buggle family has been running this traditional restaurant in the idyllic Lahn Valley since 1827 and chef Joachim Buggle represents the eighth generation to take the helm. He has brought in an impressive concept revolving around culinary classics, serving flavour-intensive, unpretentious dishes. Appetising examples include braised venison shoulder with house-made spaetzle, wild cranberries and glazed pointed cabbage, or grilled calamaretti with lemon risoni, green asparagus and a crustacean foam. Food is served from noon to 9pm Thu-Sun. There is a three-course lunch menu Thu-Sat. On certain dates, they propose a four-course surprise menu. With its beautiful country-style interior, the restaurant has a pleasant, cosy atmosphere; the Weinstube exudes a slightly more rustic feel. And they have another ace up their sleeve in the form of a wonderful terrace in the shade of the lime trees! The front-of-house team provides charming and cordial service… and Landhotel zum Bären is an excellent overnight option.

Name: Heiligenstein
Description: Come here to enjoy classic seasonal cuisine in an inviting, modern atmosphere. High-quality, fresh ingredients are prepared in an agreeably straightforward way that brings out their flavour. Choose between an à la carte menu and a three- or four-course set menu. Then there's the excellent wine list featuring over 400 selections. In summer, tables are laid out on a wonderful terrace. The hotel boasts a peaceful location and lovely cosy guestrooms – and the abundance of greenery and surrounding forest mean it is also a good starting point for hikes.

Name: Weinbistro "George"
Description: This bistro, too, is definitely worth a visit. The second dining option in the Weinhaus Uhle hotel is at once a wine shop and an appealing restaurant and is open all day long. Seated at wooden tables in an attractive space, you enjoy à la carte dishes or a three- or four-course menu. For chef Marcel Kube and his team, regional ingredients are key. Classics include "Die Fischsuppe" (fish soup) and "Der Uhlsche Klops" (meatballs). There is also a good wine selection. In summer, tables are set up on the street out front.

Name: Café GUPI
Description: Set up in what was formerly the gardener's house on a historical country estate, this is the trendy restaurant-bar concept of the Düster and Schneider restaurateur and winemaking families. Christoph Düster's cuisine is made up of what could be described as "modernised classics". The premium quality of the ingredients is evident in dishes such as "pizokel (buckwheat dumplings), Périgord truffle, spinach, hazelnut" or "guinea fowl, sherry, parsley root, Andengold potato". The atmosphere is pleasingly laid-back and casual, not least thanks to the easy-going, attentive and ever-present service under the direction of wine expert Christoph Schneider, who recommends wines from a selection of over 500 labels to accompany the four-course set menu. In case you were wondering, the name of the restaurant is derived from the regional "GUPI" wines ("GUtedel" and "PInot"). In summer, the terrace shaded by the large trees of the lovely Läublin Park is highly inviting. Tip: Booking advisable.

The Stadtgarten Pavilion (1936) has been given a new lease of life with a trendy-chic feel, plus a good dose of glamour! The ingredient-led cuisine blends modern and classic techniques. Food is served throughout the day: The "Business Lunch" is available until 3pm, daily specials until 5pm. In the evening, there is a broader range of options. Diners can opt for a set menu featuring à la carte dishes for both lunch and dinner. Those in the mood for classics such as Wiener schnitzel or beef goulash, or who prefer a pizza, will not be disappointed. The terrace overlooking the small park is lovely. Very good, wide-ranging wine list. For a classic bar experience, check out Pino's Bar, which also has an outdoor lounge.

Name: Drexlers
Description: Since 2007, this bright, modern restaurant right next to Colombipark has been something of an institution in Freiburg. Service is friendly and adept and the atmosphere warm and welcoming. Showcasing regional produce and sustainable practices, the ambitious seasonally inspired cuisine draws on French and Italian influences. The four- to six-course set menu comes in a "classic" or vegetarian version. On Mondays and Thursdays you can also order individual courses à la carte. Great selection of reasonably priced wines – courtesy of their own wine shop. Tip for motorists: Rotteck underground car park is within walking distance.

Name: Genießer Stube - Daniel Raub
Description: Daniel Raub represents the third generation at the helm of this family-run business. At Genießer Stube, one of the restaurants of Romantik Hotel Landhaus Biewald, he serves up classic French-based cuisine with a seasonal focus, eschewing any frills and putting his ingredients centre stage. In the evening there is the set menu ("Genießer Menü") that comes in four or six courses - supplemented with little extras. You can also opt for fine wine pairings. The wine list is also worth a look. In winter, food is served in the cosy dining room with its old wooden beamed ceiling. In summer, you can also dine in the conservatory or on the terrace. Professional service is provided by a charming front-of-house team. The second restaurant, Zur Tränke, is housed in the newly constructed modern hotel building. In addition to comfortable accommodation, overnight guests can look forward to a fantastic breakfast. Tip: Museum Friedland is just round the corner.

Name: Laesâ
Description: Murrhardther Hof, a culinary institution since 1920, has launched an inspired new concept. What sets it apart is that all diners are seated at a long solid-beechwood table – perfect for mingling, but with enough space for this not to be obligatory. From your seat, you can even catch a glimpse of the open kitchen, where Moritz Bilsing and Emilio Löffler run the show. Dishes are clearly and thoughtfully composed – pared down to the essentials, almost to the point of minimalism. A fine example, with harmonious flavours and delicate contrasts, is the rolled turbot fillet: moist and tender, served with a dollop of wonderfully salty fine-beaded caviar, as well as aromatic barley, sweet apricots and a mild, buttery curry sauce. The single set menu comes in four to six courses, which are punctuated by refined little extras. The experience is complemented by the expert and friendly service, with sommelier Nikolas Reidt providing excellent wine recommendations. As an alternative to wine, there are interesting house-made alcohol-free pairings to go with the set menu.

Name: Halbstück
Description: The Knipsers, a well-known Laumersheim winemaking family, opened Halbstück back in 2014 as a Weinstube. Now it is Alsace native Christian Rubert – also known from Karlbacher, a restaurant in Großkarlbach – who is in charge in the kitchen. Influences from his homeland are evident in his dishes, which pair beautifully with the finest wines from Weingut Knisper. The appealing dinner menu includes French classics such as bouillabaisse, and halibut in lobster sauce. Or feel free to choose from the daily specials. At lunchtime, there is a three- to five-course menu. Laura Rubert ensures that guests receive a warm welcome in this cosy atmosphere. Lovely courtyard. Tip: They also offer interesting culinary events and wine tastings (for 10 people or more). In addition, rooms can be booked for weddings and conferences. A holiday flat is available to rent out, too.

Name: Pizarro Fine Dining
Description: This small restaurant is chic, modern and upscale, and can accommodate up to 14 diners. The centrepiece is an impressive open kitchen, where Julio Pizarro runs the show. His creative cooking skilfully incorporates his Peruvian roots and many years' experience on the international fine dining scene. He presents an intriguing fusion of traditional Peruvian cuisine and Japanese influences in the form of a set menu revolving around fish and seafood. Exciting, flavoursome and meticulously crafted, the dishes showcase premium ingredients. To complement your meal, you can choose between wine pairings and alcohol-free alternatives. Charming and laid-back, yet professional service adds to the inviting atmosphere. The terrace overlooking the old half-timbered town hall is a lovely spot for an aperitif. N.B. The menu starts at 7.15pm (please arrive 6.30-7pm).

Name: HimmerRiget
Address: HimmerLand, Lars Larsens Vej 1, Farsø, 9640, Denmark
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: The impressive HimmerLand Resort sits in a rural spot, but has everything you need, from hotel rooms and lodges to a spa, tennis courts, bowling and minigolf. It also boasts the small, elegant HimmerRiget, which overlooks the golf course. Both the décor and the cooking are in a modern vein, with the experienced chef creating a seasonal tasting menu that showcases the best of the local larder. Dishes are intricate and well-presented, with a great understanding of balance and flavour, such as in the quenelle of monkfish with beurre blanc and vendace roe. The accompanying wine list is of the highest order.

Name: Söl
Description: Overlooking the 12th Century Stavanger Cathedral, this cosy restaurant has a friendly touch and offers guests a seasonal set menu. Expect an abundance of vegetables in summer and more meat and fish in winter, but with maximum flavour all year round. The Røros butter and freshly baked sourdough bread are a reason to come alone. They are equally passionate about wine, with one of the biggest natural wine lists in Norway.

Name: Rolfs Kök
Description: A buzzing neighbourhood restaurant in a lively commercial district, run by a passionate chef-owner. The contemporary interior was designed by famous Swedish artists; sit at the counter to watch the chefs in action. Dishes have big flavours, a classic heart, and a wine match each from the impressive list.

Name: Festningen Restaurant
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: It may be a former prison whose name means fortress, but you can’t feel more carefree than when seated under a parasol on Festningen's sheltered terrace looking out over the water to Aker Brygge; the period building is charming too though, with a smart yet cosy feel, and the team will welcome you warmly wherever you sit. The terrace menu comprises brasserie classics such as burgers and moules frites, while the modern restaurant menu offers unfussy, attractively presented dishes like hamachi crudo with marinated cucumber, yuzu vinaigrette and horseradish or grilled chicken supreme with rosemary oil and spinach.

Name: Savoy
Description: This local institution’s refurbishment has restored much of its original charm and its 8th floor setting boasts superb views over Esplanade Park. Sophisticated classic dishes have a Finnish-French slant and a modern edge, with the occasional Russian touch. The wine matches are first class.

Name: Falsled Kro
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list
Description: This charming whitewashed, thatched inn will draw you in with its enticing fireplace and lovely conservatory dining room overlooking the garden. The kitchen takes its cue from the French gastronomy of old, producing rich and indulgent dishes based on quality produce, be it rabbit or langoustine. Many of the ingredients are local, with fruit and vegetables often coming from the estate itself. Watch the chefs in the open kitchen to feel closer to the action.

Name: Muru
Description: You’d be hard pushed to find a restaurant with a greater focus on wine than Muru. This cosy little bistro is home to quirky touches such as a wine bottle chandelier, a bar made from old wine boxes and a raised ‘cellar’ – and that’s not to mention the nearby wine bar they run. Simple, tasty dishes are on-hand to accompany the excellent wine list, and the sommelier’s pairing suggestions are spot-on.

Name: Admiralgade 26
Description: This historic house dates from 1796 and sits in one of the oldest parts of the city. It’s a relaxed place: a mix of wine bar and bistro, with minimalist décor. From oyster mushrooms to actual oysters, the à la carte comprises a selection of vegetable and fish-led dishes. For something a little different, Saturday lunchtimes see a set, Japanese-style 'choushoku' served. Owner Christian is always eager to let you taste some of the 4,000 wines from the cellar.

Name: Levi
Description: Japanese and Italian cuisine join forces at this stylish restaurant near Rosenborg Castle Gardens. Expect to find Italian ingredients mixed into recognisable Japanese dishes and vice versa, such as salsiccia gyozas or spaghettoni with yuzu miso. The ambitious, high quality dishes are served by the friendly, attentive team in a strikingly designed room with high ceilings, bare tables and a large glass front.

Name: Tollbua
Description: Set within the Brattøra waterside development, this large brasserie provides exactly the buzzing atmosphere you would hope for. Banquettes and a central bar fit the bill décor-wise, while visible ducting and terrazzo flooring lend an industrial edge. Choose from a set menu of either four or six courses, where a signature dish of scallops can be added as an extra – the accompaniments depend on the season, but could include the likes of sea buckthorn, XO and celeriac. It’s beautiful Norwegian seafood like this including, when available, skrei cod of course, that will be among the highlights of your meal.

Name: akmē
Description: akmē is much more than just a follow-up to Anaba Sushi – not only does this younger sister occupy Anaba’s former space, but it’s run by two young chefs who used to work on the team. Located in the former industrial harbour district, its youthful, upbeat vibe is a perfect fit for the area, and the energetic staff help to create a memorable experience for its 16 diners. The omakase menu is centred around Japanese-French fusion cuisine – offering the likes of Danish red mullet in bouillabaisse sauce – with the best spot a seat at the counter to observe how the well-conceived dishes are delicately prepared.

Name: Restaurant Anton
Description: Not far from Nyhavn’s picturesque canals sits Restaurant Anton, an impressive 18th-century building that was once an exclusive confectionery store. A restaurant since 1915, it’s now run by the owners behind Marv & Ben, and its appealingly retro dining room still boasts its original murals, along with blue-and-white-tiled tables and Royal Copenhagen tableware. The modern Danish cooking comprises largely of smørrebrød with a twist at lunchtime – such as herring with sour cream, egg, onion compote and tomato – while dinner sees the likes of fried turbot with sunchokes and a gooseberry vinaigrette.

Name: Petri
Description: The name is a diminutive version of ‘petrichor’ – which refers to the lovely scent that accompanies rain after a warm period – and there’s a similarly pleasant cosiness to the restaurant itself, thanks to its charming, intimate atmosphere. The set menu follows the seasons, with produce that’s only available in smaller batches offered as optional extra courses, such as pork from Rosa Skattlådan with fermented berries and rose petals. Dishes are well thought out in their creativity, showcasing a good understanding of balance and flavour, while wine and homemade non-alcoholic pairings complete the package.

Name: Betong
Description: ‘Betong’ translates as ‘concrete’ in English, which explains the bare walls at this buzzy, simply decorated restaurant. Dishes like the Chef's signature ‘Cold Curry’ (available as an added extra to the tasting menu) showcase his creative heart, but the kitchen's classical technique shines through in highlight dishes like butter-poached skrei cod with pak choi. Dining here is a fun experience, in a space bursting with energy and filled with pumping music; but it might not be the best spot for an intimate romantic date.

Name: Brasserie Astoria
Description: The former Astoria Cinema, which dates from the 1870s, has been carefully restored and now boasts a bustling brasserie with all the glamour and opulence of yesteryear. Sit at the counter to watch the chefs at work; quality produce is used to create flavoursome modern brasserie classics with the odd Asian influence.

Name: Brasserie Hansken
Description: A delightfully traditional brasserie, centrally located by the City Hall, with various charming dining areas and a fantastic terrace. Classical cooking follows the seasons and mixes French and Scandic influences; seafood is a speciality.

Name: Hillenberg
Description: Perfectly pitched for busy Humlegårdsgatan is this stylish take on a classic brasserie. It has a smart, elegant feel and a hugely impressive wine cellar. Appealing Swedish classics are immensely satisfying yet exhibit a subtle modern touch and sense of finesse.

Name: Sjømagasinet
Description: Sjømagasinet means ‘Sea Store’, so it’s no surprise to find that seafood leads the way at this vast, three-roomed restaurant beneath an apartment block. When the weather’s right, start with a drink on the superb waterside terrace; when it’s not, make for the chic lounge-bar. There’s plenty of options when it comes to dining here: you can select something from the crab and lobster tank or choose from the shellfish bar for delicious langoustines, a platter of shellfish landed at the local dock or dishes like lobster with chive butter and burdock chips.

Name: Restaurant Sletten
Description: A relaxed former inn in a charming coastal village; most people head to the room with the sea view, although the others with their bold foodie pictures are equally as pleasant. Well-presented modern small plates make up the menu – 2-3 per person is about right. The wine list provides plenty of interest.

Name: Molskroen
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: A table on the charming terrace of this relaxed and stylish seaside inn is the perfect spot to enjoy traditional French cuisine, as well as more modern interpretations of the classics. The chef collaborates with a local organic farmer and the restaurant’s comprehensive sustainability programme includes staff welfare.

Name: Kødbyens Fiskebar
Description: This buzzy, industrial-style restaurant is set – somewhat incongruously – in a former butcher’s shop in the old meatpacking district, and was one of the pioneers in transforming the area into a leisure destination. Menus feature freshly prepared seafood dishes which are based around the latest catch, with oysters being a speciality. The terrace is a popular spot come summer.

Name: Uformel
Description: The more casual sister to formel B, Uformel celebrated its 10-year anniversary in 2024 with an extensive renovation that created a brighter space and a more ‘grown-up’ feel. Its smart open kitchen remains but gone is the cocktail bar and, in its place, is an exclusive chef’s table. Dishes are presented either on a five-course menu or as uniformly priced tasting plates – with three to five per person about the right amount – and see traditional Danish flavours joined by Asian notes. Its central location makes it ideal for a visit at any time of day.

Name: Leijontornet
Description: After a few years as Djuret, this long-established restaurant has returned to its original guise and provides heaps of old-school charm. The dining room is full of character, with armchairs, vaulted brick ceilings and candlelit tables all adding to the appeal. The cooking has heavy French and other European influences, providing bold flavours across a wide selection of generous dishes. Expect plenty of premium produce, from lobster to turbot, and an extensive, similarly luxurious wine list.

Name: Ræst
Description: Pass on the option of a normal table and instead opt for a seat at the shared table in this traditional turf-roofed house with characterful, low-ceilinged dining rooms. Its name means ‘fermented’, and the set menu is indeed centred around fermentation and ageing, with an insight into the heritage of Faroese food and flavours. Dishes are bold, creative and well-balanced.

Name: Hotell Borgholm
Description: The beautiful island of Öland is famous for its wild herbs, and that’s reflected in the cooking at this historic hotel’s restaurant, where the chefs enhance their elaborate dishes with thyme, basil and a host of other flavours from the 100-variety-strong garden. The original, boldly flavoured cooking comes in a tasting menu format, marrying classical techniques with subtle modern ones in dishes such as pan-fried red kingfish with spring chlorophyll, pickled cloudberries, saffron butter sauce and bronze fennel. The accompanying wine list is a real treasure trove for oenophiles.

Name: Boen Gård
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list
Description: Lovingly restored buildings in a beautiful bucolic setting make dining here a memorable experience. The surrounding gardens, meadows and river provide the vegetables, fruits, herbs and fish that are used so respectfully and expertly by the classically trained kitchen; all complemented by an impressive wine cellar. The chef's philosophy of using local ingredients will be explained to you by the excellent service team.

Name: Lieffroy
Description: A beautiful, yellow-painted house in a lovely location overlooking the sea; inside it’s traditionally furnished, with rustic-chic vibes, a romantic atmosphere and formal service. Classic French cooking features local seafood and is accompanied by a well-chosen wine list.

Name: Trattoria La Strega
Description: Run by passionate, charming owners, this lively little trattoria is nestled within a quiet residential area, and serves the kind of authentic Northern Italian food that you would find in a good trattoria in Piemonte or Triveneto. Boldly flavoured dishes are best enjoyed from the 'Menu Degustazione', accompanied by a candlelit table and a glass of wine from the extensive Italian selection.

Name: Sjömagasinet
Address: Klippans Kulturreservat, Adolf Edelsvärds gata 5, Gothenburg, 414 51, Sweden
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: A charming split-level restaurant in an old 1775 East India Company warehouse; sit on the upper floor to take in the lovely harbour view. Cooking offers a pleasing mix of classic and modern dishes, with seafood kept to the fore. The wine list offers a good selection of top burgundies.

Name: Cru
Description: The wine reference in the name is no coincidence – Cru offers up an impressive, largely French wine list that features some particularly fine vintages. In keeping with the Gallic theme, these are joined by a selection of French-inspired dishes where classic flavours come together with Norwegian produce to create the likes of pâté en croûte with cornichons and mustard or chicken vol-au-vent with morels, peas, asparagus and tarragon. The large, high-ceilinged dining room comes in shades of green and grey, and offer views into the open kitchen from its comfy banquettes.

Name: Arakataka
Description: If you’re looking for a relaxed and fun meal out, then Arakataka has you covered courtesy of its cosy atmosphere, friendly service and flexible menus. First, pick your view – beside the huge windows looking out onto the street or opposite the open kitchen – then choose your menu, which comes in a choice of four or six set courses or as smaller snacks. Modern dishes might include halibut tartare with fermented tomatoes or duck breast with cabbage and leeks – and you’ll find the perfect match on the excellent wine list, which offers some impressive verticals from French and Italian producers.

Name: Nokka
Description: A huge anchor and propeller mark out this harbourside warehouse and, inside, three high-ceilinged rooms juxtapose brick with varnished wood. An on-view kitchen allows you to watch wonderful, sustainably sourced ingredients being prepared in a rustic style. There is a strongly Finnish heart to the cooking, which includes classic dishes like freshly made blini topped with crème fraîche, pickled red onions and vendace roe. Friendly, professional service adds to the experience.

Name: Restaurant Saga
Description: A change of name in 2025 from Spontan Vinbar to Restaurant Saga placed a little more emphasis on the food side of things, but its philosophy remains the same. Striking eclectic art adorns the walls of the relaxed and trendy restaurant, while the adjoining wine bar offers an impressive array of bottles to choose from, with real depth to the list and numerous vintages from heavy hitting producers. The ten-course tasting menu offers modern, restrained cooking and original touches – like a deconstructed XO sauce – that enhance the dishes.

Name: Restaurant Palægade
Description: Enter past the pavement terrace with sheepskin-covered chairs and you’ll find yourself in this beautiful brasserie with a striking black & whited tiled floor, unique brass light fittings and oak & walnut chairs modelled on Finn Juhl’s ‘Reading Chair’. During the day, this is the place to come for smørrebrød – both traditional and modern – from warm fried herring with caramelised onion, ramson and apple to red pickled herring with dark berries and crème fraiche. At night, the menu focuses on contemporary Danish cuisine and an impressive wine list, with the hand-crafted dessert trolley a real feature.

Name: Voisine
Description: Homely and cosy, there is a wonderful lack of pretension to this French-inspired bistro. Flavourful dishes from the small open kitchen include classical French desserts like blueberry clafoutis and Paris-Brest. The restaurant has a lived-in feel, with stripped wooden flooring and leather banquettes. The owners have a wine bar next door and it shows in the excellent wine list and shelves stacked full of bottles.

Name: Ošterija Debeluh
Description: Situated in inland Slovenia, just a few kilometres from the border with Croatia, this is one of the most interesting restaurants in the region. Here, highly experienced owner-chef Jure Tomič has created an excellent network of suppliers from all over Slovenia. His meat and fish dishes are prepared with a modern, precise and technical touch that is unfussy and focuses on bringing out the full flavour of his ingredients. Traditional recipes and ingredients are reinterpreted with a personal twist: examples are the plum extract served with duck and the sweet “butter, honey and milk” dish that takes the ingredients of a typical local dessert and creates something new and original. The chef’s specialities include his Cake Debeluh, a beef tartare with meat macerated in a cold nutmeg broth, goose liver and quail egg spiced with coffee. You’ll have no problem finding the right wine to accompany your meal from the extensive wine list.

Name: Danilo
Description: Now run by the third generation of the Čarman family (opened by grandfather Danilo), this restaurant continues to offer cuisine that is much appreciated by its guests, with Nina at the helm in the kitchen and her brother Gašper front of house. The menu includes traditional dishes such as Loška smojka, Loška medla and Loška mešta (old peasant recipes from Škofja Loka – hence the adjective Loška – which are based on semolina and served with turnips, pork or potatoes), as well as a few more creative options that also have a regional flavour. Although meat takes centre stage, a few fish specialities from the Adriatic also feature on the menu.

Name: Galerija okusov
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: The setting for this excellent ‘Gallery of Flavours’ is particularly impressive: a park with an 18th-century Baroque palace, once the castle of Novo Celje, standing in the centre. The welcoming fine dining room and, more importantly, the culinary offerings, always based on local ingredients, prepared and presented with skill and imagination, are definitely more contemporary. The choice is between a concise à la carte menu and a seven-course tasting menu, which can be reduced to five courses. With over 600 labels, the wine cellar is one of the most extraordinary in Slovenia.

Name: Gostilna Repovž
Description: The Repovž family who run this restaurant are continuing a long family tradition that dates back centuries – it’s thought that the property has been in their hands for over 500 years, while there’s been a restaurant here for 300 years! Although it’s impossible to list the different generations that have worked here, one thing’s for sure – the current management has really made its mark. Both the dining room and kitchen staff work hard to ensure that guests enjoy the best regional ingredients and dishes (for example, there’s no fish on the menu), which feature on tasting menus of various lengths, with a careful balance of traditional recipes and more contemporary-style fare. The choice of delicious appetisers showcases an array of local traditional specialities, including locally bred trout, vegetables from the restaurant’s own kitchen garden, rabbit with plum cream, and diced pork with a horseradish mousse. The impressive wine list focuses on Slovenian labels – make sure you try the Laški Riesling from Slovenian Styria, a natural wine, or perhaps the Frelih St. Rupert, a lightly macerated and elegant Sylvaner produced nearby.

Name: Kropka.
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: A cool and casual spot, there's a welcoming vibe to this neighbourhood operation that’s very much in the bistro mould. The cooking mixes a Polish base with influences from over the world – such as the Korean-style chilli sauce that smothers the pierogi – while the generous portions are indicative of the restaurant’s spirit. The interesting and ever-evolving wine list spotlights natural options, with plenty of orange and skin-contact varieties.

Name: OKRestauracja
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Don't be fooled by the name – whilst they undoubtedly have one of the best wine lists in the city, this very much feels like a restaurant. There’s a subtle nautical look to the interiors, chiming with the location overlooking a small marina on the River Oder. The seafood-focused menu has a Mediterranean feel, in dishes like salt-crusted sea bream with a citrus sauce. The in-house wine shop allows you to take home a bottle or two from the globetrotting selection.

Name: Bez Gwiazdek
Description: Located just minutes from The Royal Castle in Warsaw, this cosy restaurant has a real neighbourhood feel. The menu changes monthly, with each version focusing on cooking inspired by one of Poland's 16 regions. The chefs take traditional recipes and modernise them using contemporary techniques and sometimes a creative edge. Service comes from a wonderfully enthusiastic team who show considerable pride in their work. The great wine recommendations favour biodynamic choices from smaller producers.

Name: Fino
Description: The city’s Old Town plays host to this understated restaurant surrounded by attractive period buildings. The elegant interior benefits from modern art for sale on the walls and floral arrangements adding a pop of colour. An overtly seasonal menu means you might be treated to superb, full-flavoured deer when the time is right; but there’s a luxury edge to the offering all year round, with ingredients like Oscietra caviar utilised with skill by the chefs. The friendly service stands out for the enthusiasm and knowledge of the team.

Name: Nolita
Description: A glass-fronted kitchen is the focal point of this smart monochrome restaurant that takes on a multitude of influences. The cooking exhibits flavours from a range of global cuisines, be that Polish, Mediterranean or Southeast Asian. Check the daily specials too, for luxurious produce like whole Alaskan king crab to share. Dishes have assured flavours, often accompanied by vibrant colours, and the excellent bread is a real highlight. Friendly service and spot-on wine recommendations complete the picture.

Name: Dyletanci
Description: Tucked away in a quiet city centre mews, this smart restaurant is the brainchild of an experienced chef and wine importer. This background shines through in an impressive wine list with over 1,500 choices, including a brilliant selection of burgundies and grower champagnes, plus plenty of biodynamic and organic options. The cooking is pared-back and vibrant, utilising ingredients from their own farm and local producers. The friendly team enhance the experience and the lunch menu is a steal.

Name: alewino
Description: Spread across various rooms, this starkly decorated bistro started life as a wine shop. There’s still a range of bottles for sale, along with a wine list that offers plenty of younger and lesser-known producers. The menu allows guests to order a few plates to share, including staples like beef tartare along with dishes that show off seasonal vegetables, be that green asparagus or golden beetroot. Look out for the signpost by the gates when arriving, as the entrance is tucked away off the main street.

Name: Cucina
Address: City Park Hotel & Residence, ul. Wyspiańskiego 26a, Stare Koszary, Poznań, 60-751, Poland
Description: Housed inside a historic building that was once a military barracks, the ever-developing Cucina now forms part of the City Park Hotel. The Mediterranean-influenced menu caters to a range of tastes, from pizzas and pastas to beef tartare and fine roast duck. A large, on-view wine wall is an indicator of the serious list, which features some excellent vintages and is enhanced by charming, knowledgeable service. A covered terrace comes into its own in the summer.

Name: Olivia's Prime
Address: Lot G1-G4, GF, Indochina Mall, 74 Bach Dang Street, Hai Chau Ward, Da Nang, 550000, Vietnam
Description: The family-owned restaurant is known for its quality food and service, as well as its friendly vibe. The menu focuses on beef steaks, including some top notch imported choices such as Japanese Hitachi A5 Wagyu, Aussie Sanchoku MB5 and Black Angus, and USDA Prime. Chargrilled to perfection and flawlessly seasoned, the steaks are tender, juicy and flavourful. Round off your meal with New York style cheesecake.

Name: Le Beaulieu
Address: GF, Sofitel Legend Metropole, 15 Ngo Quyen Street, Hoan Kiem Ward, Hanoi, 100000, Vietnam
Description: Le Beaulieu has been operating at the same spot since 1901. The interior has been entirely refurbished and remodelled with mod cons and a chic décor, while retaining its French DNA courtesy of details such as blue velvet banquettes and crystal chandeliers. Century-old classics like the lobster bisque are served from the guéridon, just like in the good old days. The classic crêpe Suzette is another highlight, beautifully prepared with real orange juice and flambéed to just the right degree at the table. Subtle Vietnamese notes run through the tasting menu. The setting works equally well for a business lunch or a more intimate dinner.

Name: Little Bear
Address: 36 Nguyen Ba Huan Street, An Khanh Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, 70000, Vietnam
Description: This newcomer to HCMC’s bistro scene greets diners with a cosy dining room that offers both counter and table seating. The theatrical open kitchen is where the alchemy takes place. The menu consists of modern dishes inspired by the chef’s childhood memories and Vietnamese food culture, including cheese and noodles made from scratch in-house. The wine list features a wide range of wines by the glass, covering organic, biodynamic and natural tipples.

Name: The Temptation
Address: 125 Ho Nghinh Street, An Hai Ward, Da Nang, 550000, Vietnam
Description: This modern villa with a beautiful courtyard and an opulent dining room is home to an upmarket eatery helmed by Vietnamese chef La Thua An. Brought up in France, he opened The Temptation in 2019, wowing diners with ingenious creations, layers of flavours and often whimsical plating. His signature dessert The Sweet Egg Nest of Han River, for instance, features numerous ingredients encased in a spun-sugar egg-shaped shell in a bamboo cage. An excellent choice for a celebratory meal, the restaurant is in high demand, so reservations are essential.

Name: Lửa
Address: 2 Street No. 11, An Khanh Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, 7000000, Vietnam
Description: The al fresco wine bar, whose tables are set in a courtyard surrounded by lush greenery, is perfect for a drink and nibbles. There are about 100 wine references to choose from with a substantial by-the-glass selection. The wine list changes every month, but the home-brewed sake, a firm fixture, is worth trying. The menu, which features only organic produce from small local farms, combines European dishes with a twist of Japanese elements as both the manager and head chef hail from Japan.

Name: Le Comptoir
Address: 16 Che Lan Vien Street, Ngu Hanh Son Ward, Da Nang, 550000, Vietnam
Description: Run by a chef from Lyon, France, this restaurant is the epitome of Gallic authenticity. The á la carte and set menus roll out bistro classics made with premium local and globally sourced ingredients, cherry-picked by the chef. Expect items like roasted beef bone marrow and hand-chopped Wagyu tartare with Melba toast and smoked aioli. The interesting wine list offers a wide range of boutique wineries. Pop into the deli after your meal.

Name: Etēsia
Address: 14B Lo Su Street, Hoan Kiem Ward, Hanoi, 11007, Vietnam
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Valet parking
Description: An 18-seat kitchen counter is the focal point of this snug buzzy wine bar. Shelves of empty wine bottles adorn the walls, and the wine list showcases over 350 vintages from the Old World. They offer a vast array of wines by the glass and from a Coravin. The neatly presented, modern dishes are Mediterranean at heart with an Asian twist and deliver accurate, well-judged flavours. Don't miss the homemade pasta: the pride and joy of Etēsia.

Name: French Grill​
Address: LF, JW Marriott Hanoi, 8 Do Duc Duc Road, Tu Liem Ward, Hanoi, 100000, Vietnam
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Great view,Interesting wine list
Description: This elegant hotel restaurant boasts a sleek, spacious dining room oozing with special occasion indulgence. Counter seats offer a view of the huge open kitchen and create a connection between the chefs at work and the diners. The contemporary French culinary score is consummately executed with subtle Asian twists, flanked by equally immaculate, spot-on, courteous service. Private dining rooms are available for everything from date nights to business dinners.

Name: La Villa
Address: 14 Ngo Quang Huy Street, An Khanh Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, 700000, Vietnam
Description: The distinctly Gallic menu whisks diners from Ho Chi Minh City to Paris in an instant, with foie gras, meat-filled puff pastry pies, American lobster with champagne and Grand Marnier crème brûlée among the highlights. Decorated with artwork, velvet curtains and a stunning chandelier, La Villa also offers al fresco dining in the garden. Attentive, suitably formal service, a fine wine list and cheese selection. The starched classical vibe of this restaurant will appeal to those who appreciate vintage charm and nostalgia.

Name: Nephele
Address: 125/12 Nguyen Cuu Van Street, Gia Dinh Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, 700000, Vietnam
Description: Located in the alley of a newly renovated building steeped in history, this dinner-only restaurant serves a single tasting menu. The menu, made with ingredients that the chef selects from diverse regions, is perfectly paired with wine or non-alcoholic drinks. The experience begins on the ground floor, moves upstairs to the elegant main dining room, and ends back at the bar with mignardises served to the sound of vinyl records. Each course is distinguished by its sauce, with bold, original flavours that show a clear Vietnamese influence. A standout is the 36-hour fermented sourdough bread, charcoal-baked to a crisp finish and served with honeycomb, truffle butter and miso made from the bread.

Name: The Albion by Kirk Westaway
Address: 23F, Hôtel des Arts Saigon, 76-78 Nguyen Thi Minh Khai Street, Xuan Hoa Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, 070000, Vietnam
Description: Perched on the top floor of Hotel des Arts Saigon, The Albion blends historical charm with modern elegance to pay homage to British heritage. Chef Kirk Westaway's menu reimagines and elevates British classics, such as fish and chips, roast chicken with thyme and Eton mess. The Albion Tomato, with burrata, Dalat heirloom tomatoes and basil-orange sorbet, is a standout. With stunning city views and expert wine pairings, this place showcases contemporary British dining at its finest.

Name: Quince Eatery
Address: 37 Bis Ky Con Street, Ben Thanh Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, 700000, Vietnam
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list
Description: This Colonial-style house with a vibrant decor, bistro-style furniture and soft lighting serves up a modern take on Mediterranean fare. Pick from a range of charcoal-grilled, wood-fired and oven-baked dishes and eat at the copper counter, which has six seats facing the open kitchen. Top choices include wild Hokkaido scallops and smoked marrow cappelletti, aged barbary duck breast and tasty golden berry with burnt sourdough mousse. Cocktails can be ordered from the bar upstairs.

Name: Backstage
Address: GF, Capella Hanoi, 11 Le Phung Hieu Street, Hoan Kiem Ward, Hanoi, 100000, Vietnam
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Your inner celebrity will revel in this opera-themed restaurant, whose open kitchen takes front stage. Signature dishes like Capella Pho and Bun Cha Hanoi respect their northern heritage, whilst featuring modern techniques with European touches that will leave your tastebuds singing an aria of delight. The impressive cocktail list offers some intriguing mixes. The theatrical setting showcases stunning costumes from the world’s greatest operas.

Name: Bar Misera
Description: Tightly-packed tables, a scattering of wine bottles and an appetising lineup of dishes with a seafood slant to share set the scene in this buzzy, modern, almost quirky bistro. Bar Misera is THE place to go for a casual meal with the mates in Antwerp’s hip Nieuw Zuid district. Kick off your meal with a few nibbles from the wide-ranging array of snacks on offer, like egg mayonnaise or sardines, before moving onto larger portions. First-class quality and flawless freshness are paired with acidic tangy notes. Think seabass, seared to perfection in its skin, flanked by sea lavender and a herby butter and then coated in a subtle fresh foamy sauce. Diners choose their own sides. An amusing concept, underpinned by a stellar wine cellar.

Name: Woodend
Address: Maroma, A Belmond Hotel, Carr. Cancún - Tulum Km. 51, Playa del Carmen, 77710, Mexico
Description: Acclaimed Chef Curtis Stone lends his name and refined culinary sensibilities to this seaside retreat, and though he’s unlikely to be present in the flesh, rest assured that you are in good hands regardless. As the first step into the smoke-perfumed room will give away, the cooking here centers around live fire, with a charcoal oven and wood-fired grill lending their aroma to a small menu that leans to bold simplicity. Local seafood and well-marbled steaks from Oklahoma make up the mains, while sides like crunchy potato pavé with jocoque and buttery Parisian gnocchi with endive and poblano are tasty enough to stand on their own. For dessert, a twist on tarte tatin with pineapple, mezcal-tinged caramel, and toasted vanilla ice cream hits just the right mark.

Name: Arbol
Address: Las Ventanas al Paraiso, A Rosewood Resort, Carr. Transpeninsular Km. 19.5, San José del Cabo, 23405, Mexico
Description: Located within Rosewood's Las Ventanas al Paraiso, this beguiling setting hosts diners in an indoor/outdoor space arranged with chunky blond wood tables on a polished stone patio. Lantern-hung branches cast a rosy glow.The large menu is impressively prepared and is founded on Indian specialties. However, the range of offerings is further enhanced by charcoal-grilled meats and seafood, as well as an abundance of Asian specialties. The Punjabi fish masala features the catch of the day, which is gently cooked in a zesty, moderately spiced tomato-based sauce enriched with ground cashews and flecked with herbs and spices. It comes served with spice-tinted basmati pilaf and Indian-style pickled vegetables and is best paired with butter-brushed naan hot off the tandoor.

Name: Hugo
Address: Av. Veracruz 38, Roma Norte, Cuauhtémoc, 06700, Mexico
Description: With its terrace shaded by an awning and likable ambience, Hugo is the sort of place you'll want to return to time and again. While the wine list is indeed extensive, giving it its wine bar classification, it's not just about the bottles here; it's as equally well liked for its bistro-style menu. Quality products shine in dishes with pleasing presentations, while the contemporary cuisine remains faithful to traditional flavors.

Name: Autor
Address: Conrad Tulum Riviera Maya, Carr. Cancun Tulum 307, Tulkal Chemuyil, Tulum, 77774, Mexico
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Autor is quickly writing itself into Riviera Maya's history from its relaxed perch at the luxurious Conrad Tulum Riviera Maya. This kitchen delivers two tasting menus focused on contemporary Mexican cuisine. One is entirely vegetarian, while the other draws on ancient local traditions while simultaneously modernizing and elevating the dishes. Tuna is impeccably presented on a lovely plate with raised edges, and the exquisite pieces, paired with avocado and caviar, as well as fermented daikon, make for an excellent beginning to the meal. Pork belly al pastor is popular in this part of Mexico, but here it's a notch above.The wine list features bottles from Mexico and the United States, as well as Champagnes and European varieties.

Name: Bu’ul
Address: Chablé Maroma, Carr. Federal Cancún - Tulum, Punta Maroma Km. 51, Playa del Carmen, 77710, Mexico
Description: A journey through the jungle, past the private villas that make up the Chablé Maroma, will bring you to this enclave, serving singular cuisine that celebrates native ingredients and flavors. To that effect, sophisticated permutations of corn, beans, and chiles are threaded through the menu, as in a pickled Venison with crispy potato straws and a tableside pour of recado rojo. A main course of luxurious creamy rice — enriched with Ocosingo cheese and accented with nutty, herbaceous pipián verde alongside earthy venison longaniza — offers an unapologetically Mexican answer to risotto. Finish with a wonderfully intense molten cake of Oaxacan chocolate, ideally paired with a sip of mezcal.

Name: María Dolores
Address: Atelier Playa Mujeres, Condominio Playa Mujeres SM-3 M-1 L-13 RTH-4, Isla Mujeres, 77400, Mexico
Description: Expect to be pampered like royalty from the second you step into this luxurious enclave within the sprawling Atelier Playa Mujeres hotel. Apart from hyper-attentive hospitality (including top-notch beverage service), the grand space features a balcony from which diners are serenaded with live music. The menu of contemporary Mexican cuisine, curated by celebrated chef Edgar Nuñez, is similarly ornate, featuring a multitude of à la carte offerings and specials, in addition to a signature tasting menu. The flavorful, original cooking draws upon the breadth of Mexico’s culinary landscape, reimagining familiar regional dishes with a refined modern style, whether it’s a tempura-fried shrimp taco in honor of Sonora, or a complex Oaxacan mole coloradito paired with roasted quail.

Name: Latitud 32
Address: Carr. Guadalupe-El Tigre Km. 7.5, Parcela Km. 118, El Porvenir, 22755, Mexico
Description: Latitud 32 is located outside of Ensenada on the Valle de Guadalupe wine route. This dining room neighbors the El Cielo resort and is set within a working vineyard, commanding a prized view of the valley's landscape. The grounds are stunning, while the restaurant has a rustic elegance with a high ceiling, stone and brick-lined walls and floor-to-ceiling glass.The menu is a fusion of Baja and Yucatan specialties. Highlights include salbute de pato, comprised of fried masa tortillas topped with shredded greens and a generous amount of slow-cooked, shredded duck pibil. Slow-cooked lamb atop mashed potatoes liberally sauced with birria broth shines in borrego tatemado. House wines are poured to enhance the experience, and the menu offers recommendations for each course.

Name: Manta
Address: The Cape Hotel, Carr. Federal 1 Km. 5, Fracc. A, L-21, Cabo San Lucas, 23456, Mexico
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking
Description: Set within the Cape Hotel in a stunning space that frames picture-perfect views of Cabo's famed Lands End, this modern dining room under Enrique Olvera's umbrella isn't just impressing with its good looks. The contemporary Mexican cuisine is punctuated by clear Peruvian and Japanese influences. Offered as a tasting menu and à la carte, expect bold dishes from a kitchen that's unafraid of bringing the heat. Begin with sashimi or a Peruvian ceviche before savoring a refreshing melon salad with diced honeydew and cantaloupe in a leche de tigre fueled by yuzu. Next, a sweet potato with a tamarind and soy crust in an almond mole is at once simple and elegant. From Korean fried chicken and tempura fish tacos to cochinita served in bao buns, Asian accents run from beginning to end on the à la carte menu.

Name: HeJi
Description: Having been five years in the making, this impressively opulent restaurant seems like it might be more at home in Dubai or Macau than Vilnius! The cavernous glass-roofed main dining room is overlooked by two ‘caged’ balconies and a champagne bar – and there’s an intimate, dimly lit cocktail bar serving innovative Asian creations. A wood-framed open kitchen takes centre stage, and captivating and intriguing design details abound. The original menu fuses East and West, with the dim sum a hit and the signature beef dishes cooked over charcoal a real highlight. The wine list features some fine growers and the whole place makes for a glamorous night out.

Name: Somm
Description: The name Somm is an abbreviation of sommelier, giving a strong clue as to where Owner Narimantas Miežys’ passion lies. He is one of the top sommeliers in the Baltic region and, with over 500 bottles making up a truly global selection, this really is an oenophile’s paradise. Set opposite the MO modern art museum, it’s decorated with wine bottles and related memorabilia, and has a pleasingly unfussy, neighbourhood feel. This is a place where the sommeliers choose your wines for you – after discussing your preferences, of course – matching them with simple yet interesting dishes, some of which are prepared in the wood-burning oven

Name: Monte Pacis
Description: Set within a historical property in a large park just outside of the city, Monte Pacis sees Lithuanian cuisine reinterpreted in a modern and visually appealing way. Carefully prepared native, Baltic and international ingredients feature in flavoursome dishes such as lamb sirloin with ricotta tortellini, Swiss chard, shiitake mushrooms, courgette, parsnip cream and pepper sauce. The owner is a passionate wine-lover and a qualified sommelier, so wine is naturally a highlight, with more than 200 bottles individually and lovingly described.

Name: PACAI Restaurant
Description: The owners here claim that this 17th Century baroque property in the heart of the Old Town was once the most magnificent mansion in the Grand Duchy of Lithuania; today, it’s home to a boutique hotel complete with a spa and this stylish restaurant.
