Take his okonomiyaki – Japanese pancake made with spring onion and shiitake, served with sweet-and-sour pickled cucumber, tempura lotus root, Japanese mayonnaise and seaweed. This is a chef who has all it takes to delight diners.

Name: Avize
Address: 956 Brady Ave. NW, Atlanta, GA, 30318, USA
Description: Atlanta's food scene is reaching new heights with Chef Karl Gorline's alpine-accented restaurant, where boho and rustic vibes create the sense of being in a cozy Swiss cabin. Avize features a modern take on traditional alpine cuisine, drawing from the culinary traditions of France, Italy, Germany and Switzerland. Decadent dining begins with an array of starters such as frog legs, venison tartare and foie gras parfait, to a caviar service featuring berliners filled with elderflower crème fraîche. Main dishes include duck, fermented carrot Bolognese and American wagyu; seared scallops sided by charcoaled potato and char-cooked English peas are a highlight, as is the black sesame tiramisu for dessert.

Name: Felicia Suzanne's
Address: 383 S. Main St., Memphis, TN, 38103, USA
Description: Scarlet-red banquettes, a long marble bar and checkered tile cut a striking, seductive figure in downtown Memphis. This is a restaurant whose identity is never in question thanks to veteran Chef Felicia Suzanne, now situated in a swanky new dining room where she sources quality ingredients and honors Southern traditions with class and elegance. Plucky Gulf shrimp set with tasso ham sauce and jalapeño cornbread is a superb signature and a reflection of this kitchen at its very best. Other hits on this finely curated menu include buttermilk fried quail with greens and spiced honey as well as fried green tomatoes with Creole remoulade.

Name: Customshop
Description: Befitting its status as a fixture of the charming Elizabeth neighborhood, Customshop offers a lively, convivial atmosphere. Chef Andrés Kaifer oversees a modern menu that blends together Latin and Italian influences with seasonal North Carolina produce in dishes that are both inventive and approachable. A hamachi crudo with parmesan leche de tigre illustrates the point beautifully with its blend of vibrant acidity and savory depth, and carefully crafted pastas like casarecce with chorizo, local mushrooms and sungold tomato broth put any doubts to rest. Eminently quaffable cocktails and a thoughtful, food-friendly wine list certainly are a bonus, especially when combined with the friendly, helpful service.

Name: Soby's
Address: 207 S. Main St., Greenville, SC, 29601, USA
Description: Soby's is a local institution. Opened in 1997, this restaurant in downtown Greenville has earned a loyal following. The dining room is always buzzing, and the bar is often packed, so plan ahead to reserve a table or pull up a seat at the bar and watch the bartenders at work. The wine list is especially impressive, and the kitchen highlights spot-on Southern cooking. Breaded and fried green tomatoes over a layer of haricots vert are topped with a pimiento fondue sauce and some crispy carrot slices for a dish that speaks with a thick Southern accent and never disappoints.

Name: Bad Idea
Description: Renovating a church sanctuary to house a wine bar and restaurant certainly doesn't sound like a Bad Idea to us. That is the premise behind this striking East Nashville spot, where Alex Burch leads a robust wine program with more than 100 selections housed in a custom wine cellar. Featuring a massive bar anchoring the center of the room and expansive seating all around, the restaurant is primed for groups out for a good time. The kitchen prepares a host of playful dishes like whipped pimento cheese or a riff on chicken nuggets, as well as hearty entrees like charcoal grilled steak and quail stuffed with spinach.

Name: Supperland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: You'll know you're in the right place when you smell the heady scent of smoke as you approach this Plaza Midwood restaurant. A former church, it's undergone an extensive restoration resulting in this stylish space. In case the oversized range fueled by wood didn't give it away, this is Southern fire cooking through and through. Expect prime meat and fish to be cooked over the glowing embers, while imaginative sides are equally worthy of attention. It's all served family style, and sharing is the name of the game here. While you're sharing, order their seasonal sundae with a spoon for all.

Name: Andrew Michael Italian Kitchen
Address: 712 W. Brookhaven Cir., Memphis, TN, 38117, USA
Description: Opened in 2008, the flagship restaurant from Chefs Andy Ticer and Michael Hudman remains one the city’s most enticing efforts. Divided into several small rooms, the cozy house in Brookhaven Circle celebrates Italian cooking with Southern influences. Appetizers and pastas are the strongest features, starting with the signature house breakfast of pork belly, pork rinds, polenta, and a runny egg. For a lighter beginning, swing for the tuna tartare with sundried tomatoes or tender squid stuffed with Barese sausage and set in a sharp puttanesca sauce. Mafaldi coated in a rich bianco veal ragu is as gratifying as potato gnocchi tossed in a garlicky spring pesto.

Name: Cúrate Bar de Tapas
Description: This Spanish-inspired tapas bar lives inside a renovated 1920s bus depot and serves up a menu rooted in tradition—think charcuterie, croquettes, patatas bravas and shareable large plates like mussels and grilled meats. They also have a "jamonarea" with around six different types of cured meats available. Cozy yet refined. this spot in downtown Asheville makes you want to order a bottle of wine and settle in while snacking on a tabla de quesos, a board with a selection of Spanish cheeses, and curate sobrasada, a pimenton-spiced, spreadable, cured sausage sided by goat cheese and honey with a baguette.

Name: Herbsaint
Address: 701 St. Charles Ave., New Orleans, LA, 70130, USA
Description: This restaurant has been weathering storms and going strong for more than two decades. Pull up a chair inside this dining room overlooking St. Charles Street and you'll soon see why. The food is a skilled blend of Cajun and Creole undertones laced with French and Italian inspiration, but there's no need to classify it, just enjoy it. The house-made spaghetti is a perennial favorite but there are so many winning dishes, including a refreshing jumbo lump crab with watermelon and their take on the classic duck confit: Muscovy duck leg confit with dirty rice and citrus gastrique. It's a signature for a reason. Don't miss the comforting finale of vanilla pot de creme with local blackberries and cornmeal madeleines.

Name: Restaurant August
Address: 301 Tchoupitoulas St., New Orleans, LA, 70130, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: At the turn of the 21st century, this historic building transformed into a fine dining haunt that has served the city well ever since. On a corner of Tchoupitoulas Street, glittery chandeliers and wax candles light up a handsome, stately room anchored by white tablecloths, large windows, exposed brick, and dark wood finishes. Indeed, countless birthdays and anniversaries pass through this dining room year after year. A Louisiana native, Chef Corey Thomas steers the ship, highlighting regional ingredients and bringing contemporary touches to Creole cuisine. Think gnocchi with jumbo lump crab or a pithivier stacked with collard greens and chanterelles and finished in a Creole mustard sauce. Friendly servers keep the room from ever feeling too starchy.

Name: Clancy's
Address: 6100 Annunciation St., New Orleans, LA, 70118, USA
Description: Clancy's is a far cry from its former life as a beloved neighborhood po'boy shop from the 1940s. It's still a family affair, owned by former employee Brad Hollingsworth and run by his family, but that's where the similarities end. A man in a tuxedo will greet you upon arrival, welcoming you to an upscale setting complete with uniformed servers and white tablecloths. The kitchen delivers on its promise of refined Creole cuisine, and the menu is seafood focused with plenty of French and Cajun influences. Begin with their signature appetizer of fried oysters with brie, then move on to the chicken and andouille sausage gumbo. Fried, smoked softshell crab with meunière sauce is yet another Clancy's signature that impresses.

Name: Georgia Boy
Address: 1043 Ponce de Leon Ave. NE, Atlanta, GA, 30306, USA
Description: You don't just walk in off the street to Georgia Boy. First, it's secreted away within Southern Belle. Then, if you're one of the diners with reservations, you'll be seated in the "library" for drinks, snacks and a hint of what's to come. Find the chef's favorite book on the shelf and voila!, a secret door opens, revealing an open kitchen. Chef Joey Ward enjoys teasing diners, and while some courses are highly experimental, others are interesting and fun. Milk and cereal is a perfect example of his creative styling, with seared snapper presented alongside a box of cereal containing puffed rice and a tableside pour of hot coconut milk broth.

Name: Staplehouse
Address: 541 Edgewood Ave. SE, Atlanta, GA, 30312, USA
Description: Located in the Old Fourth Ward, Staplehouse is a charming, casual spot. Order, grab a drink if you'd like and then find a seat either inside or in their backyard. It's a limited menu with a few varieties of pizza, several sandwiches and salads listed on a board behind the counter. Small or large charcuterie boards are ideal for snacking with friends but don't overlook the specials, designed around what's seasonal and available. Desserts include treats like cookies and blondies.

Name: Herons
Address: The Umstead Hotel & Spa, 100 Woodland Pond Dr., Cary, NC, 27513, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Need a getaway but have limited time? Herons offers the experience of a country escape without the commute. Set within The Umstead Hotel and Spa on three acres of woodland, this restaurant is a showpiece for their nearby farm's produce. Try several dishes or opt for the chef's surprise five- course menu to relish fresh and vibrant plates crafted with care by Chef Steven Devereaux Greene. His South Carolina roots are best seen in dishes like shrimp and grits, and dessert is impressive, especially the appealing blackberry panna cotta with fresh poached blackberries and crème fraîche.Be sure to consult the extensive, globally minded wine list. The restaurant has even developed several wines in close collaboration with the vineyards.

Name: Di Vino Rosso
Address: 807 Gervais St., Ste. 100, Columbia, SC, 29201, USA
Description: Exposed brick walls and tile floors hint at the building's former incarnation as a Ford dealership but this address has been home to Di Vino Rosso for more than two decades. This longstanding Italian restaurant is a Columbia favorite, known for its focused menu of Italian staples made with local ingredients (they even list their farmers on the back of the menu). Tuck into a bowl of meatballs resting in tomato sauce before a plate of pappardelle with a rich and flavorful wild boat and Italian sausage Bolognese sauce. While the savory dishes are certainly the highlight, end the meal with an espresso and tiramisu for a classic finish. ---

Name: Tall John's
Description: Tall John's is the quintessential neighborhood spot with a classic American menu and an ambience that makes it just right for everyone on any given night. It may be welcoming and laidback, but there is a clear emphasis on quality ingredients here, where house-made sauces are just one hint at the kitchen's skill.The menu leans into the name, with small and medium plates like olives, salads and tartare, and tall plates offerings hearty mains like steak, chicken and duck. Of course, nothing beats a classic order of Caesar salad with house-made croutons and a cheeseburger slathered with house-made dijonnaise and layered with sweet pickles and griddled onions. Shoestring fries on the side clinch the deal.

Name: 1856 - Culinary Residence
Address: The Laurel Hotel and Spa, 205 S. College St., Auburn, AL, 36830, USA
Description: Named for the year Auburn University was founded, this dining room is the primary training ground for students enrolled in the school of hospitality management. Resident chefs guide the kitchen, while sharp-suited servers run a seamless experience in the front. Many ingredients are sourced directly from the rooftop garden, while the wine list is an impressive collection highlighting the very best producers across a wide range of regions. The dinner tasting menu is reasonable in length, as all details are cared for in this expansive, modern dining room where glass walls reach for the sky.

Name: Leo's House of Thirst
Description: Leo's House of Thirst is the sort of place we all want in our neighborhoods. This wine bar feels like your coolest friend's living room, livened up with books, wine bottles and plants. Inside, there’s a small bar with seating, plus a patio outside with tables and umbrellas.Stop in during the day for a cup of coffee and a breakfast sandwich or swing by at night for a glass of wine and a meal. Mornings are met with the likes of avocado toast and a beef short rib hash, while come evening, it's oysters, crudos and tartares, along with heartier options like chicken and pasta.

Name: iggy's
Address: 609 Merritt Ave., Ste. 101, Nashville, TN, 37203, USA
Description: Curious what this spot from brothers Ryan and Matthew Poli is all about? Take one look at the mural outside and you'll soon realize. This Wedgewood-Houston restaurant with a sleek, modern industrial look (and a glass-enclosed pasta room!) is all about Italian food.The concise menu keeps things simple with a few small plates like crispy arancini in warm goat cheese fondue or Village Bakery brioche stuffed with whipped cream cheese before a tempting array of house-made pastas like little shells with Maine peekytoe crab in a sea urchin butter. Soft serve sundaes are nonnegotiable. Lemon huckleberry with cubes of brown butter cake, lemon curd and huckleberry sauce over vanilla ice cream is a sure thing.

Name: The Kingsway
Address: 4201 Magazine St., New Orleans, LA, 70115, USA
Description: Located on a prime corner of Magazine Street in the Garden District, The Kingsway is a surefire looker with its chic, dark blue exterior and sleek, bright dining room armed with wood plank flooring, cream walls and linen-draped tables. Welcome to the home of Chef Ashwin Vilkhu, who draws inspiration from several Asian cuisines and packages them in a succinct, reasonably priced tasting menu. The prix-fixe includes a range of dishes from steamed snapper in a delicate dashi broth to crispy lamb with rice noodles. The beverage program is double trouble. Clever cocktails are flavored with elements such as Thai tea, lychee, black sesame and Asian pear cordial. The wine selection, meanwhile, highlights small producers and back vintages.

Name: Galatoire’s
Address: 209 Bourbon St., New Orleans, LA, 70130, USA
Description: At one of the city's oldest institutions, Friday lunch is a revelrous rite of passage. Pearls and suits file in right as doors open to a gold-accented dining room encased in mirrors and watched over by tuxedoes. Everyone is celebrating something and has the balloons, Champagne, and party hats to prove it. For more than a century, French-Creole classics have run up and down the menu. Go heavy on appetizers, like the Oysters Rockefeller, crabmeat maison with shrimp remoulade, or those superb, puffy soufflé potatoes dipped in a rich béarnaise. For entrees, find more seafood topped with all manner of crab, cream, and butter. There is nothing light, quick, or quiet about this meal. This is about leisure. Take your time. There’s nowhere else you need to be.

Name: The Fearrington House Restaurant
Address: 230 Market St., Fearrington Village, Pittsboro, NC, 27312, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: If you're looking for an escape, few places match this charming country setting just outside of Pittsboro. Fearrington Village, a former dairy farm, has been transformed into a darling village, and at the center of it all is the Fearrington House Restaurant. Inside, the two-story house is an elegant setting that speaks to special occasions with linen-clad tables and intimate spaces. The seasonally changing menu features produce grown here and nearby and showcases a range of cooking styles from creative to classic. The chef's tasting allows for exploration, but don't miss the signature, indulgent chocolate soufflé.

Name: Bottega
Address: 2240 Highland Ave. S., Birmingham, AL, 35205, USA
Description: There is a good reason why reservations are sparse at Frank and Pardis Stitt’s long-standing Bottega. The historic, neo-classical Beaux-Arts building stands out in Birmingham's Southside neighborhood, and eager, sharply dressed guests wait outside for doors to open. Some find seats at the bar; others settle into an elegantly cut dining room fit with dark wood walls and high ceilings. Prepared with confidence, the menu paints broad strokes with its Italian-Mediterranean bent. Think parmesan souffle, wobbly and just set, or sweet potato gnocchi with rock shrimp. Wherever you sit, warm, polished servers ensure a welcoming experience.

Name: Cafe Roze
Description: All-day restaurants are sometimes overlooked, but don't count out East Nashville's Cafe Roze; it's more than meets the eye. Whether you come for coffee or breakfast, swing by for an afternoon glass of wine or stop by on your way home for dinner, this welcoming spot hits all the right notes. The menu leans lighter with vegetable-forward small plates and oysters that seem simple but have unexpected depth. Grilled long beans atop hummus with green nasturtium zhoug is deeply savory and watermelon radish tossed in an urfu vinaigrette with greens is refreshing, while pork schnitzel with brown butter-chili sauce and a zesty slaw offers a fresh take on a classic. For dessert, you'll never go wrong with the creamy chocolate mousse.

Name: Nikos
Address: 905 W. Main St., Unit 21B, Durham, NC, 27701, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Nikos, named for the owner's father, is set within a restored red-brick tobacco storage warehouse dating to the early 1900s. The interior plays up that rustic industrial heritage and blends it with a simple sophistication, sharing a modern tavern appeal. As for the food, expect all of the Greek and Mediterranean classics, including spanakopita and octopus carpaccio to begin. Come with family or friends to tuck into the meze grand platter, add a glass of crisp Greek wine and you might just be convinced you're basking in the sun on a Greek island. Meanwhile, baklava and honey semolina cake round out the meal.

Name: Table
Address: 18 N. Lexington Ave., Asheville, NC, 28801, USA
Description: Husband-and-wife duo Jacob and Alicia Sessoms are behind this simple, seasonal and thoughtful restaurant in downtown Asheville. Table looks and feels welcoming, with plenty of places to sit both indoors and out, and a staff that exudes Southern charm. The New American menu is well-edited, with small plates like roasted vegetables, steak tartare and fresh bread, and larger mains like chicken, steak and house-made pasta (ricotta cavatelli in a rabbit sugo is spot on). The “easy” section includes bar snack standbys like wings, a fried chicken sandwich, and a messy-delicious burger with house-made queso served with shoestring French fries. Flan Parisian is the perfect way to end a meal here.

Name: Brodeto
Address: 2201 Iron Works Dr., Ste. 137, Raleigh, NC, 27604, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Brodeto occupies the ground floor of the former Raleigh Iron Works (RIW), now home to a hub of eateries, retail outlets and offices. The name, a nod to a fish stew found along the Adriatic coast, is the first hint that this restaurant takes its inspiration from the cuisines of Italy and Croatia. From crudo and must-have pasta dishes to wood-fired fish and meat to peka, a Croatian slow-cooked, fire-grilled dish with octopus and potatoes, and of course, the namesake brodeto, this menu is packed with appealing choices. You won't go wrong with any of the selections, but be sure to consult the wine list, where no region of Italy is neglected and special growers and vintages are highlighted.

Name: Omakase Experience by PrimeFish
Address: 2907 Providence Rd., Ste. 101, Charlotte, NC, 28211, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list
Description: Chef Robin Anthony is the driving force behind this ambitious six-seat counter, located just a hop away from its larger, more casual sibling. The price point is undeniably lofty, but a bevy of luxury ingredients—truffles, foie gras, imported fish from Toyosu market—provides ample justification as the offbeat omakase unfolds. This maximalist approach is demonstrated by an indulgent signature dish of toasted brioche topped with wagyu tartare, uni and truffle, and even a hand roll of grilled eel is enriched with a bit of foie gras. When it comes to the nigiri, however, a more understated edomae approach is deployed, allowing the impressive quality of the seafood and finely tuned rice to shine. Thoughtful sake and wine selections are an added bonus.

Name: Helen
Description: Chef Rob McDaniel and his wife Emily built this downtown Birmingham restaurant as an homage to Rob's maternal grandmother who gathered her family for meals that she cooked over her indoor grill. Here at Helen, many of the dishes feature grilled elements.The menu is concise and showcases plates with Southern and international flair. Warm angel biscuits served warm alongside a quenelle of whipped cane syrup butter will soothe your soul. Enjoy a bowl of comfort with braised Peruvian lima beans in a golden broth with coal-roasted rapini and garlic chili oil. Cobia is beautifully seared on the plancha for a simple-but-satisfying dish.

Name: Txanton
Address: 2F, Alegria Alta Building, 2294 Chino Roces Avenue Extension, Makati - Metro Manila, 1232, The Philippines
Description: Born out of a wine and goods import business, this unique place has evolved over a decade into what is reputed as the Philippines' only "jamonería". Diners are seated in rooms lined with shelves of fine European wares that double as display spaces. The extensive, almost book-like menu champions top Spanish produce – jamón ibérico, Rubia Gallega beef, pluma ibérica, caviar and more. Negotiate a couple of the 3000 bottles of wine and share with friends.

Name: Iai
Address: GF, The Montane, 8th Avenue corner 35th Street, Bonifacio Global City, Taguig - Metro Manila, 1630, The Philippines
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list
Description: Chef Bruce Ricketts has designed a deeply personal restaurant where Japanese precision meets Filipino soul. Unfolding at the sweeping acacia counter, the omakase experience blends traditional sushi with inventive touches such as foie gras parfait with pineapple. In a thoughtfully composed menu, which includes home-smoked tuna, it's the classics that shine brightest. The curated wine pairings round off a refined journey that reflects the chef's passions.

Name: Old Manila
Address: GF, The Peninsula Manila, Corner of Ayala and Makati Avenues, Makati - Metro Manila, The Philippines
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Pass through the lobby of The Peninsula to enter an elegant Art Deco setting for a timeless fine dining experience. At Old Manila, French classics with bold touches meet aged steaks and refined seafood, all presented with care. The gracious hospitality and beautifully composed plates are in keeping with the restaurant's long-standing reputation, making this a fitting choice for special occasions and a showcase of Manila's enduring culinary class.

Name: RAUSCH
Address: Reuterweg 57, Frankfurt on the Main, 60323, Germany
Description: Jochim Busch has established RAUSCH on the premises that used to house Gustav, the restaurant in which he made a name for himself with his MICHELIN-starred cuisine. Here, he serves up accessible modern food. The bold dishes are notable for their contrasting flavours, always judiciously pared back, and the ingredients are of excellent quality. This approach is evident, for instance, in the super-fresh and tender raw marinated white prawns accompanied by an aromatic buttermilk emulsion enriched with crisp salted cucumber brunoise and the citrus aromas of grand fir tree. The chef serves up a four- or five-course set menu, with two additional dishes to choose from. From some tables in the chic and minimalist space, the open kitchen is visible. Service is laid-back and attentive, with staff always on hand and attentive yet pleasantly unobtrusive. The well-stocked wine list with a penchant for German and French picks is excellent.

Name: Credence
Description: It may be ranch-inspired but Credence is firmly planted in Houston. Large and airy, the dining room is anchored by an open kitchen with a live fire.Credence is Chef/owner Levi Goode’s first brick-and-mortar, and it perfectly showcases his background as a fifth-generation Texan and classically trained chef. From fresh seafood towers and smoked tuna dip to hearty, live-fire options like whole duck and ribeye, there is plenty to like on this menu. Deviled crab meat tossed in a smoky tomato butter is even better when accompanied by monkey bread to soak up every last trace. Look to the flame to guide your entree choice, with a prime dry-aged New York strip a terrific option. Brown butter Bundt cake with a dollop of chantilly cream is sized for a crowd but you won't want to share.

Name: Monarch
Address: Thompson Dallas, by Hyatt, 1401 Elm St., Dallas, TX, 75201, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking
Description: Monarch is a destination for celebratory dining, set high above the city with sweeping views from its perch inside The National. The expansive room and polished setting encourage a dress-up occasion. The menu spans Italian-leaning pastas, wood-fired meats and premium seafood. Handmade pastas are generously portioned and reliably prepared, while dishes from the hearth remain a focal point. Seafood and steaks round out the offering, and the extensive wine list features a broad, international selection.

Name: Stillwell's
Address: Hôtel Swexan, 2575 Mckinnon St., Dallas, TX, 75201, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: It's nestled inside the Hôtel Swexan, a tongue-in-cheek play on Swiss hospitality meets Texas, but there's no mistaking the roots of this upscale steakhouse: it's named for the legendary lady rancher, Hallie Stillwell. Ride the elevator to the seventh floor to enter this clubby den, then sip a cocktail as you peruse their extensive menu with all of the classics. Kick things off with a shrimp cocktail, caviar, or treat yourself to the bountiful seafood tower, but of course, steaks take center stage. There are many selections but their bespoke in-house HWD beef—a superb Akaushi beef from their own breeding program just north of the Red River—is the one to beat. All the while, elegant white jacketed staff keep things moving smoothly.

Name: Stock & Barrel
Address: 316 W. Davis St., Dallas, TX, 75208, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Set in the charming Bishop Arts area, Jon Stevens's Stock & Barrel is the perfect neighborhood spot. With an industrial chic design, open kitchen, and convivial bar counter, this restaurant is consistently packed with locals, all here for the inviting ambience. Whether you sit at the bar or in a booth, the friendly staff will ensure an enjoyable time. Expect American and Texas favorites that have been given a twist, as in deviled eggs with pickled rhubarb, meatloaf made with wagyu beef, or a bread pudding from croissants. Flavors are big and bold. Don't miss their caramelized Brussels sprouts charred with chili and cilantro. The wine list features a nice selection of by-the-glass offerings.

Name: The Marigold Club
Description: This sleek spot in Montrose mixes Mayfair elegance with Houston’s vibrant energy. Elegant yet approachable, it's a stylish space for dressing up for dinner or sharing oysters and caviar at the bar. A player piano adds to the ambience.Chef Austin Waiter puts a British twist and a fresh spin on a French-inspired menu. From seafood towers and miniature gougères piped with chicken liver mousse to duck Wellington, the offerings lean decadent. Veal cuscinetto, a bone-in chop stuffed with prosciutto and Comté, is topped with vermouth figs and set over a mostarda sauce for an especially refined dish, while braised leeks in sauce gribiche make for a worthy side. For dessert, the baba-misu is an intriguing riff on two classics.

Name: Bar Bludorn
Description: How many beignets is too many? In what feels like an easygoing tavern, these light pillows land on the table warm, filled with ricotta cheese, and topped with thin slivers of Benton’s ham. One could nearly make a meal out of them. Such is the appeal of this confident neighborhood spot from the ever-busy Chef Aaron Bludorn. Wrapped in dark wood lit under keen spotlights, the dining room has clear views of a gleaming open kitchen. Just as service is both approachable and attentive, the menu too deals in hearty, comfort fare that’s short on fuss and long on flavor. From pasta to steaks, everything is designed for sharing, especially the golden-fried half chicken. To finish, desserts like warm devil’s food cake with salted caramel are indulgent affairs.

Name: Agnes and Sherman
Address: 250 W. 19th St., Ste. A, Houston, TX, 77008, USA
Description: Agnes and Sherman is a fun spot in the Houston Heights neighborhood from Chef Nick Wong and Lisa Lee. Rooted in their third-culture upbringing and love for nostalgic flavors, it's an Asian American-inspired diner with a casual and cool vibe. It's just right for meeting a friend, dining solo or taking a date. As for the dishes, think comfort food meets playful takes. Favorites like shrimp toast and savory scallion waffle with sambal honey butter kick things off. Then, chilled noodles tossed with vegetables and yuba are a tempting selection, as is their take on egg foo young, with a crawfish-studded omelet blanketed in a gumbo-style gravy.

Name: Signature Restaurant
Address: 16401 La Cantera Pkwy, San Antonio, TX, 78256, USA
Description: There is a warm and inviting appeal about Signature Restaurant, set within La Cantera Resort & Spa. Seafood dominates the menu, though there are plenty of other choices, including venison, pork, and beef. If the large menu and comprehensive wine list have you stumped, the attentive staff will keep you on track. Begin with an appetizer such as the Spanish octopus, grilled to a tender and crispy finish, served over an avocado puree and sided by confit potatoes, Spanish chorizo, and a salsa verde. Next, consider the venison, deftly cooked and accompanied by a cherry jus, baby carrots, and potato pave. End the meal on a high note with their warm torta di ricotta with candied almonds, blueberries, and yuzu buttermilk ice cream.

Name: Pappas Bros. Steakhouse
Description: Even after more than two decades of service, this Houston institution is as sharp as ever. The menu is focused, offering just a limited number of appetizers and salads to keep all eyes and appetites on the prime, in-house dry-aged steaks. Seasoned and seared properly, the cuts are complemented by generous, finely tuned sides like creamed spinach with ham or oversized onion rings (ask for extra crispy). Part of the genius here is the service: Veteran waiters and friendly sommeliers are effortlessly hospitable regardless of whether you order 1947 Petrus. Altogether, with white tablecloths, glossy wood paneling, and a wine list that has few equals, this restaurant captures a kind of magic that is rarely seen these days but unquestionably appreciated.

Name: Late August
Address: 4201 Main St., Ste. 120, Houston, TX, 77002, USA
Description: Once a Sears department store and now a community tech hub, The Ion is a fitting home for this vibrant restaurant. Cool shades of teal, couches colored burnt orange and plenty of light streaming in through the windows make for an airy stage for this eclectic menu by chefs Chris Williams and Sergio Hidalgo. The cooking is unique, offering a blend of American, Mexican and African flavors driven by the seasons. Order from the small menu or opt for the appropriately named "surprise tasting" with items like tuna aguachile, seafood consommé and an elegant duck confit pozole. Like the food, the service is honest and welcoming, and much of the produce comes from Fort Bend County, where the group’s philanthropic arm runs a 10-acre farm.

Name: Georgie
Description: This swanky space, decked out in sunny tones of peach and carrot with a retro-meets-modern vibe, certainly looks the part for an elegant night out, especially given the attentive team of uniformed servers. The cooking combines a reverence for top-quality ingredients with a basis in classic technique along with a hint of playfulness. Take, for example, crispy, creamy potato “churros,” served with a fluffy, creamy caramelized onion dip (and an optional addition of caviar), or a roasted chicken paired with grilled chicories and sauce vin jaune. For dessert, an indulgent chocolate “candy bar” layered with peanut and caramel mousse is pleasingly nostalgic. The prices may be nothing to sneeze at, but guests are certain to be well taken care of—and well fed.

Name: Apt 115
Description: Appt 115 is an intimate wine bar with a cozy ambience. It's the sort of place you'll want to stop in for a glass or two of wine paired with a charcuterie board or perhaps a parade of dishes from the full menu. Texas Ruby Red grapefruit with toasted coconut and fermented chili presents an intriguing surprise before a crispy rice cake topped with langoustine and sweet tamarind. A single Hokkaido scallop with red curry followed by duck cassoulet and Texas wagyu are among the bites on the multicourse tasting menu. Food may be the focus, but wine is taken just as seriously. The list is a passionate curation balancing old and new world finds with many small producers.

Name: Yan Garden by Chef Fei
Address: No. 12, Caochang 9th Alley, Dongcheng, Beijing, Chinese Mainland
Description: Tucked away in an alley, this restaurant converted from a historical siheyuan has a restored courtyard where customers can dine under a century-old toon tree. A Chaoshan native, Chef Fei sources the best ingredients from his hometown to turn them into refined culinary gems. The fish maw sautéed tableside with dried shrimps, crabmeat, chopped chillies, dried radish and XO sauce boasts layers of flavours. Try the boneless marinated goose web too.

Name: Rive Gauche
Address: 2F, The Puxuan Hotel, 1 Wangfujing Street, Dongcheng, Beijing, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: In this chic modern place inspired by Paris's Left Bank, Gallic techniques and recipes hold no secrets for the seasoned head chef from Hong Kong. Chinese influences are also apparent in the fermented tofu-flavoured butter served with the bread, and the longjing tea panna cotta. His flexible prix-fixe lets diners choose four or five items from the à la carte menu, including signatures such as foie gras mousseline in pistachio tart shell, and scampi ravioli.

Name: La Chansonnière
Address: Room B-103, 1F, Iiig Work, Courtyard 2, Jiangtai West Road, Chaoyang, Beijing, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Valet parking
Description: Formally trained as an engineer, the chef-owner opened this place in 2019 to pursue his passion for the culinary arts. The space, dominated by warm whites and neutrals, feels sleek and romantic. The menu offers much flexibility – French classics such as dry-aged Australian M9 filet mignon with mashed potato, poulet en vessie and loup en croûte rub shoulders with seasonal dishes and sharing plates. Pre-order three days ahead, when booking a table.

Name: Xiang Shang Xiang (Jinhe East Road)
Address: L301A-L302A, 3F, CP Center, 20 Jinhe East Road, Chaoyang, Beijing, Chinese Mainland
Description: The chain now runs a few establishments in Beijing and Chengdu, but the story started in 2021 at this very first outlet. A veteran with 20-plus years of experience under his belt, Chef Peng revamps the menu every now and then to keep it fresh. He presents a good-value set menu featuring 24 different chilli peppers – heaven for spicy food lovers. The butler, tea master and sommelier go the extra mile to deliver thoughtful, meticulous service.

Name: Mio
Address: 3F, Four Seasons Hotel, 48 Liangmaqiao Road, Chaoyang, Beijing, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: In the lavish, richly textured space, strings of glass beads draped from the mirrored ceiling form a dazzling sculpture. The Milanese chef pays tribute to the culinary traditions of Northern Italy, updating them with a sense for playful drama, meticulous plating and modern techniques. Reimagined classics of Piedmont and Lombardy such as vitello tonnato, tagliolini con ragù di agnello, and agnolotti del plin are all immensely satisfying.

Name: Country Kitchen
Address: 3F, Rosewood Hotel, Jing Guang Centre, Hujialou, 1 Chaoyangmenwai Street, Chaoyang, Beijing, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Reclaimed bricks, vintage ceramics and wood stoves set the rustic chic scene. Despite the panoramic views, your attention may be drawn to the rows of roast ducks in a display oven. The must-try chestnut-fed birds are prized for their unique nutty sweetness and crispy, wafer-thin skin. Well versed in both Beijing and Shandong cooking, the local chef presents a menu rich in distinctive Northern charm from rustic hand-cut noodles to exquisite seafood.

Name: Ray's Grill
Address: Level 63, Conrad Etihad Towers Hotel, West Corniche, Abu Dhabi
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Thanks to the floor-to-ceiling windows and its perch on the 63rd floor, great views are a given at this smart steakhouse in the Conrad Etihad Towers Hotel. Prime cuts from Argentinian and Canadian cattle are offered alongside Australian Wagyu and the steaks are cooked on a chargrill; succulence and great flavours are guaranteed.

Name: Zuma
Address: Abu Dhabi Global Market, The Galleria Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Gliding down the softly lit staircase allows everyone to feel like they’re about to arrive at the best party in town – this really is Fun Central, with the music to prove it. As with all other Zuma branches around the globe, the menu covers a multitude of Japanese bases, with seafood from the robata grill being the standout, although the signature corn-fed baby chicken rightly has many admirers too.

Name: La Petite Maison (LPM)
Address: L1, The Galleria, Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: The history of La Petite Maison (LPM) began in London in 2007, with further outposts opening around the world in the years since. Here in The Galleria, customers can judge it for themselves, with cuisine prepared with French-Mediterranean touches and served by a well-trained team in elegant surroundings. Dishes like thinly sliced octopus or salt-baked sea bass will put you in a sunny mood; and be sure to leave room for desserts.

Name: Butcher & Still
Address: Four Seasons Al Maryah Island Hotel, 2 Global Market Street, Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Those who like a classic steakhouse will find this one at the luxurious Four Seasons ticks all the boxes. Leather banquettes? Check. Prime American beef? Check. Great cocktails? Check. Start with the generously proportioned crab cake before diving into a steak – there are plenty on offer, from ribeye to porterhouse. To finish, there’s New York cheesecake of course, but the more adventurous should take on the challenge of the Tribune Tower chocolate cake.

Name: TAURO
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: This über-smart steak restaurant is secreted away on the ground floor of the Zunda Towers skyscraper and comes with cowhides mounted to look like bulls, hence the name. Forgo starters and enjoy a generous plate of cured meats and salamis from their farm, then move on to a superb steak from Latvia, Spain, California, Japan or Australia. Accompany this with a side – the crispy potatoes with thyme are a must – and one of their freshly made sauces in myriad flavours. The 300+ bin wine list is beautifully curated, with some of the North American classics creating the perfect pairings.

Name: LOWINE
Description: Naturally, wine leads the way at this snug bar located on the very edge of the city. Inside it has a rustic look and a laid-back feel, and on warmer days you can sit outside in the enclosed rear courtyard. The appealing, keenly priced wine list keeps natural wines to the fore and changes weekly, providing plenty of interest for the regulars. Similarly, the seasonally changing menu also lists something for everyone, from the traditional to the modern, with an extensive brunch menu offered earlier in the day and the likes of salmon with ponzu sauce followed by Latvian meatballs in the evening.

Name: Whitehouse
Address: Vermane Park, Terbatas iela 2, Riga, 1050, Latvia
Description: Whitehouse's dining terrace is one of the most sought after in the city thanks to its views over the colourful flower displays of Vērmanes Garden, but its signature cocktails ensure that the place really comes alive too. It’s a sizeable spot, with a long, narrow room, stylish décor and even a living wall. Menus blend influences from across the globe with a range of fermentation techniques, and there’s a touch of theatre to the presentation. The seasonal tasting menus reflect the locality – as do the smaller local producers on their wine list – and desserts are a particular highlight.

Name: BABO
Description: BABO is currently something of a lesser-known gem. Set up by a pair of experienced restaurateurs, it’s split between two rooms and a smart terrace. One of the owners hails from Georgia and it’s this that informs the menu. Delicious breads are cooked in the pizza oven and the charcoal grill adds a lovely flavour to the various meats and fish. Order two dishes each and share – maybe beef and pork khinkali (dumplings), salmon in bazhe (walnut) sauce or chicken tabaka with salt and lemon. The superb wine list showcases a unique mix of classic and modern Georgian wines, which they import directly.

Name: 36.Line Grill Restaurant
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Credit cards not accepted,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Beachside locations don’t come much better than this, as you can walk straight in off the sand. Those after some respite from the sun can stay inside, while those wanting to feel the sea breeze should head for the glass-fronted terrace. The accessible menu covers all options, from lighter sushi and tartares to dumplings and hearty grilled fish and meat dishes. The tiramisu laced with the famous Black Balsam – their national drink – is a must-try, and the extensive selection of wines and cocktails is perfect for watching the sun go down.

Name: Riviera
Description: This long-standing brasserie has stood the test of time, and it's easy to see why. The room has a fresh, unfussy elegance and a warm Mediterranean style – and the menu is the perfect match. Good quality ingredients feature in an appealing range of classic Mediterranean dishes, with everything from oysters and tartares to fish and steaks of offer; the vitello tonnato is worth a mention, as is the tiramisu. Everything is generously sized, with well-defined flavours and textures, and the sizeable wine list (1,000+) offers some great choices from around the globe. In summer, the terrace is the place to be.

Name: Villa Más
Address: Passeig de Sant Pol 95, Sant Feliu de Guíxols, 17220, Spain
Description: This restaurant occupies an impressive setting in an old Modernist-style villa overlooking Sant Pol beach, as well as boasting a pleasant terrace that gets very busy in the evening. The traditional, maritime-inspired à la carte features top-notch fresh fish straight from the auction (sea bream, John Dory, grouper, sea bass etc), a few rice dishes and fideuàs. The extensive wine list has a particular penchant for wines from Burgundy.

Name: Haberbüni
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: The atmosphere here is one of a kind! Housed in the airy hayloft of a 19C farmhouse with rustic beams, this place has a special feel to it. The tasteful interior is complemented by a secluded garden terrace with trees and shrubs. The food is fresh and made with high-quality ingredients. In the evening, there is a set menu and an à la carte selection. The latter is also available at lunchtime, supplemented by daily specials and a "Business Lunch" option. Excellent wine list featuring plenty of rarities and magnums.

Name: Ciani Lugano
Description: Located between the Palazzo dei Congressi and the Ciani Garden, this modern and cosmopolitan venue is a true gem. Renowned regional chef Dario Ranza focuses on Mediterranean cuisine, prepared with skill and precision to guarantee delicious flavors. Among his specialties: Lostallo salmon “Caini style,” a variety of risottos, sole meunière, and salt-crusted sea bass, filleted directly at the table. The selection of exclusive wines and Champagnes is truly remarkable.

Name: Tredecim by Monika Jans und Thomas Huber
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list
Description: Thomas Huber, representing the 13th generation of his family, and Monika Jans run the long-established Krone with great dedication. She is responsible for the cordial and professional service, and he takes care of things in the kitchen, cooking up seasonal, traditionally influenced dishes. Tableside preparation features prominently, particularly for larger cuts of meat or whole fish for two or more diners. They will also prepare a creatively inspired set menu if you order in advance, and a children's menu is available as well. Anticipation builds from the moment you arrive: freshly baked bread and plaited brioche loaves are displayed in the entrance area, and the magnificent cheese trolley is a feast for the eyes. In addition to the extensive wine list, there is a remarkable sake selection. The wood-panelled interior exudes charm and contributes to the delightful atmosphere, with its rustic dining area and many lovely details such as floral decorations and crown-shaped napkin holders. Should you wish to stay overnight, there are attractive, individually decorated guestrooms.

Name: De la Gare - La Table des Suter
Address: Avenue de la Gare 13, Lucens, 1522, Switzerland
Description: This elegant family-run institution opposite Lucens railway station always hits the spot thanks to its buzzy, friendly vibe and professional service. The carefully crafted repertory is rolled out in polished lunch and dinner menus. Some of its high-flying signature dishes include “Belles Demoiselles” (langoustines cooked in several ways with a Thai sauce), “Patte Noire de Bourgogne” poularde (young chicken) stuffed with truffles and a citrus fruit and chocolate extravaganza. Top-notch produce is enhanced by the know-how of its seasoned kitchen team. Savvy selection of Swiss wines, also available in magnums and half bottles. Pleasant guestrooms for overnight stays. An elegant interior and slick polished service for a gourmet experience – what’s not to like?

Name: Restaurant Chastè
Description: In their delightful little hotel, the Pazeller family offers not only attractive, comfortable guestrooms but also sophisticated cuisine. The restaurant combines two concepts: "Ustaria Chastè" with an upscale evening menu and a simpler lunchtime menu, and "Bocca Fina Chastè" with an evening fine dining set menu. The interior is tasteful and cosy, the service friendly and attentive.

Name: Paradiso
Description: In good weather, you can take in breathtaking views up here at 2 181m above sea level. This down-to-earth Mountain Club restaurant appeals to diners of all ages. You can look forward to good food and a great wine selection, as well as a relaxed atmosphere, partly thanks to the music and DJs. Note: there is an entry fee for non-members. Accessible by chairlift and a short walk (10min).

Name: Schotte-Sepp-Stube / Flickflauder
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Whether you choose to dine in the cosy Schotte-Sepp-Stube, the bright Veranda or the contemporary, minimalist-style Flickflauder, you’re guaranteed good-quality, seasonally inspired cuisine and friendly service at this restaurant, which hosts an “Appenzell evening” with a buffet every Wednesday. The à la carte is available to non-resident guests from 8pm onwards.

Name: Spettacolo
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: In the Lenkerhof spa resort's elegant restaurant, hotel patrons who opt into the "Gourmet Package" can look forward to an appealing half-board set menu, while others, including external guests are treated to a high-quality and modern take on classic cuisine that also draws on international influences. Pick four to six dishes from a selection of 15 to create your own menu. The excellent wine list spans sizes from half bottles to magnums. Friendly and knowledgeable waitstaff. The best view of the mountains and valley can be enjoyed from the light and airy conservatory – and, of course, from the terrace.

Name: La Brasserie du Royal
Description: Hosted in the Royal Savoy hotel, a favourite haunt of the Spanish monarchy, the Brasserie shines the spotlight on an enticing cast: timeless elegance, polished cuisine and spot-on service. The first-rate “Menu du Marché” and “Menu de la Brasserie” feature four courses that exemplify the establishment’s high-flying culinary hallmark: crab meat, shellfish mayonnaise, Granny Smith apples, celery and lovage; seafood, aioli and crunchy fennel; juicy, melt-in-the-mouth Swiss steak, velvety mashed potato; mille-feuille laced in Madagascan vanilla. The duo of sommeliers knows their glazed wine cellar inside out. The terrace is a hit for alfresco dining come the fine weather.

Name: Hirschen
Description: Classic, seasonal and regional food is the name of the game here. Guests can enjoy fine dining and vegetarian options in the Zeitgeist restaurant, as well as house classics, including the signature dish: chateaubriand. Tip: The chocolate cake with coffee is sensational! Alternatively, you can order burgers, pasta etc in the cosy Hirschenstube. Popular terrace for summer meals, plus comfortable guestrooms.

Name: La Coupole - Matsuhisa
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list,Wheelchair access
Description: "Irasshaimase" – this is the exclamation with which diners are greeted here. Housed in the building that was once home to Europe's first indoor tennis courts, Nobuyuki Matsuhisa's restaurant buzzes with infectious vibrancy. The wide-ranging menu features a fusion of Japanese and Peruvian cuisines – everything is prepared in the open kitchen (there is also a sushi bar). The black cod miso is definitely worth trying.

Name: Da Enzo
Address: Via Ai Grotti, Ponte Brolla, 6652, Switzerland
Description: This Ticino-style stone house boasts a sun-blessed, verdant setting and a beautiful garden-terrace. Take a seat at one of its typical granite tables, either crowned by stone vaulting or shaded by trees, where you can savour exquisite Mediterranean on several tasting menus (including vegetarian) and an à la carte that includes daily specials. Other features include a well-stocked wine cellar and two romantic rustic-style guestrooms. Open for over three decades, Dan Enzo is now run by the second generation of the family.

Name: Gildo's Ristorante
Description: If you’re in the mood for some authentic, upmarket Italian food, you should try the line-caught sea bass, cime di rapa, garlic, pine nuts and peperoncino at this restaurant. Expect excellent service, plus a stylish Alpine ambience.

Name: Moosegg
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Up here, at an altitude of around 1 000m, there is much to get excited about: the phenomenal view of the Emmental and the Alps – either from the modern, elegant restaurant with its large window front or the magnificent terrace – is complemented by good food with a regional and seasonal focus. For a cheaper but equally indulgent alternative, head to Beiz. The terrific wine selection boasts around 900 different options. Chic guestrooms are available in the adjoining boutique hotel.

Name: Rössli
Description: Rössli is a pleasant restaurant decorated along clean, modern lines in warm colours. Enjoy the smart place settings and friendly service led by the lady of the house and Ueli Kellenberger’s flavoursome, contemporary cuisine. The mouthwatering offerings include open ravioli with spinach, egg, parmesan and beurre noisette.

Name: Le Bistrot des Ours
Address: Route du Pas de l'Ours 41, Crans-Montana, 3963, Switzerland
Description: A sloth of hungry bears would be right at home in the rustic, alpine vibe of this bistro which commands a fantastic view over the valley and the surrounding peaks. The accent is on ambitious French-Mediterranean cuisine that will find favour with discerning omnivores, together with an insightful array of Valais wines. All of which is courtesy of chef Franck Reynaud, who is also at the helm of the Michelin-starred L'Ours. We warrant it will set your tastebuds tingling!

Name: Auberge de la Couronne
Description: Housed in a tastefully renovated traditional edifice, the Auberge de la Couronne morphs from a simple bistro at lunchtime into a fine dining gourmet establishment in the evenings. Generosity and indulgence are the order of the day in dishes such as crisp golden roasted sweetbread served with fresh crayfish and crunchy glazed mini vegetables. The front-of-house team is on hand to help you find the perfect food-wine pairing as you take a seat in the handsome vaulted dining room.

Name: Le Restaurant / Le Relais
Description: If you are looking for excellence and elegance, you are in the right place at Le Restaurant or Le Relais of the venerable Badrutt's Palace! In an atmosphere of luxury, the attentive front-of-house team provides classic service. The flavourful cuisine is made from exclusive ingredients and the menu also features a number of dishes that are prepared at your table, e.g. flambées, tartar etc. Whatever you opt for, there is a first-class selection of wines from the best vineyards to accompany your meal. Tip: You can also have breakfast here - accompanied by views of the lake and mountains.

Name: La Grappe d'Or
Description: A restaurant where superb flavours and a friendly atmosphere go hand in hand. Chef Theotim Bioret is strongly inspired by northern Italy, from Venice to Ventimiglia: Piedmont-style agnolotti del plin, sage and a parmesan jus; calf's liver saltimbocca, spinach and polenta. In addition to the à la carte menu, the chef serves a more affordable weekly dish. Meticulous plating, precise cooking and delicious sauces combine to create a faultless dining experience.

Name: La Terrasse Brasserie
Description: Befitting the elegance that characterises the Victoria Jungfrau Grand Hotel & Spa, which is steeped in tradition, a classically refined atmosphere awaits you here. The location against the impressive backdrop of the mountains is a real draw in itself. In summer, make a beeline for the terrace on the promenade! The menu includes a broad assortment of Arab, Asian, Mediterranean and Swiss dishes – flavoursome food made from high-quality ingredients.

Name: Pfändler's Gasthof zum Bären
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Steeped in over 200 years of history, this building makes for a really delightful setting: Bärenstube and Orangerie are full of charm and attention to detail. The garden is also attractive – a truly idyllic spot! The seasonal food has regional and Mediterranean influences. You will feel right at home in the pretty guestrooms.

Name: Marée
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: The location high above Vaduz is exceptional, commanding sweeping views over the town and surrounding mountains. Park-Hotel Sonnenhof draws acclaim not only for its tasteful, classic-style guestrooms, but also for Hubertus Real's cooking. Attentive and adept waitstaff serve dishes made from select ingredients, including house classics such as "spicy beef tartare, French fries allumettes", "mini bouillabaisse – fish, shellfish, sauce rouille, croutons" or "slow-cooked ray wing, capers, brown garlic butter, mashed potatoes". The extensive wine list is presented on a tablet. Dine in the elegant interior or, in summer, out on the magnificent terrace with its vantage point – the "Adlernest" (eagle's nest) – from which you can take in the stunning view.

Name: Izumi
FacilitiesAndServices: Interesting wine list,Notable sake list,Terrace,Valet parking
Description: The atmosphere is truly international: top-notch ingredients go into a fusion cuisine of Japanese and other Asian influences. Perhaps you will be tempted by the Wagyu beef gyoza with a spicy ponzu sauce, or the izumi rolls? The lively atmosphere and chic, modern look of this restaurant on the seventh floor of the Four Seasons Hotel are just as much of a draw as the food. Don't miss the rooftop terrace, one floor up, with its breath-taking view over Geneva, the mountains and the lake. Make a reservation without delay!

Name: The Omnia
Description: A lift whisks you up here from the pedestrian area – to some breathtaking views! In the restaurant of the eponymous hotel, the focus lies squarely on vegetarian cuisine. Dishes such as baked cauliflower with wild mushrooms, cedar nuts and mountain thyme can be complemented by meat/fish specialties. Those in the mood for a culinary adventure can opt for the five-course "Chef's Choice" set menu (with meat/fish or vegetarian), with optional wine pairings. The wine list boasts a fantastic selection of labels from Switzerland and France – especially Burgundy and Bordeaux. The interior is modern and upscale, featuring clean lines and large granite-topped tables. Tip: Enjoy an aperitif by the fireplace.

Name: Balm - La Pistache
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: At the elegant restaurant of Hotel Balm both the service and Beat Stofer's cuisine have a classical feel. He cooks unpretentious fare using excellent ingredients. Choose between the three- to five-course "Amuse Bouche Menu" or order from the à la carte selection. Highlights include classics such as "Chateaubriand in two courses" (served for two or more) or Swiss Black Angus simmered beef terrine with apple, mustard and horseradish. There is also a well-curated wine list. In good weather, the terrace is a fabulous spot from which to enjoy the mountain views. This place is always busy, so be sure to book well in advance! Inviting guestrooms.

Name: La Bagatelle
Description: Here you can look forward to classic French cuisine made from top-notch ingredients, including an appealing selection of dishes for two. The cosy ambience created by warm wood, soft lighting and a touch of elegance is inviting. Plus they have a terrace with a stunning view. The service is charming, and there is a terrific wine list – half bottles are available, too. For an overnight stay: Hotel Le Grand Chalet has cosy rustic-style guestrooms.

Name: Groven
Description: A small restaurant with a focus on simple local dishes. If you stop here for lunch on the terrace, you’ll find a different menu every day inspired by whatever ingredients are in season. There’s also a tempting selection of cheeses and game such as venison and wild boar during the hunting season, plus an interesting wine list.

Name: Du Théâtre
Description: A stone’s throw from the centre, within the Crochetan theatre precinct, this elegant restaurant sports an airy interior, an open kitchen and a small conservatory. The Italian-born chef conjures up a creative, deftly crafted score that slaloms between regional classics and Mediterranean favourites, but always with seasonal produce. The crisp balanced dishes showcase the occasional inspired imaginative spin, like his wondrous “Risotto of red Sicilian shrimps with blueberry powder and celery”. The talented sommelier is a font of wine knowledge and the shaded terrace is bliss in fine weather.

Name: Ristorante Seven Lugano
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated on the rooftop of the casino and accessible via a glass-sided lift, this restaurant boasts fine views of the lake. In summer, the popular seats on the terrace need to be booked well in advance, while the lounge bar on the ground floor is perfect for a post-dinner cocktail. The cuisine here is varied and wide-ranging, both on the set menus and the à la carte, plus there’s an excellent choice of wines featuring labels from around the world.

Name: Adler
Description: A little off the beaten track, this inn is delightfully cosy with its wood-panelled interior. The chef-patron, who hails from South Tyrol, serves up classic cuisine with a modern twist, making a point of working with regional produce. There is a four-course set menu, which can be extended with additional dishes up to the nine-course "Full Story". A vegetarian version is also available. Alternatively, you can dine à la carte. The attentive front-of-house team includes the chef's significant other, a trained sommelier – the wine cellar boasts over 800 labels. There are a few attractive guestrooms available for overnight stays.

Name: L'Artichaut
Description: Only the street separates this appealing restaurant from the River Arve. You will feel completely at home here, thanks to both the pleasant bistro atmosphere, including decorative paintings and posters of vintage advertisements, and the French cuisine reinterpreted with seasonal influences. Excellent wine list.

Name: Le Vallon
Description: This stylishly appointed classical inn with a pretty pink façade and green shutters welcomes you with open arms. The tastefully luxurious interior is decked in bouquets of fresh flowers and floral wallpaper. In his open kitchen, David Thomas concocts traditional fare with an extremely subtle sprinkling of modern touches (Lyon-style frog’s legs, sweetbread à la meunière). Friendly, attentive service and a splendid wine list (among which a noteworthy selection of reds, also available in magnums). In fine weather, alfresco dining on the terrace and in the lush green garden is heavenly. A country chic interior with overtones of romanticism – perfect for a date night!

Name: Auberge du Lion d'Or
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: In a chic setting with a romantic view of the Jura mountains and Lake Geneva, you can enjoy modern, creative fare with a weakness for vegetables, illustrated by flame-licked roast watermelon paired with a marigold stock. The enticing lineup is paired with a fine wine list that boasts a few Swiss and French gems. The swish, luminous private room upstairs is the epitome of contemporary good taste.

Name: GLACIER
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: What a great location right in front of the Eiger North Face! The fine dining restaurant of the chic boutique hotel (also called Glacier) makes the most of its surroundings with a floor-to-ceiling window front – and a wonderful terrace with a magical view. The dishes are based on select regional ingredients: you can either opt for the set menu or go à la carte, and sharing is also possible. The set menu is accompanied by harmonious wine pairings from the 320-year-old wine cellar, which is home to some 500 labels, over 200 of which are available by the glass. The enthusiastic sommelier provides astute recommendations.

Name: Lindenhofkeller
Description: This establishment bills its cuisine as a sophisticated version of home cooking. But soul food is also a very apt description of what is served in this gorgeous traditional restaurant in a desirable Old Town location. Sebastian Rösch delivers genuinely feel-good fare through a symbiosis of his Franconian heritage and Swiss influences. Shining examples include sausage salad with Gruyère and slices of radish, or a classic braised beef roulade served with parsley root, spiced gingerbread and bone marrow jus. The chef strikes a balance between tradition and modernity, always showcasing high-quality ingredients. Diners can go à la carte or opt for the "Schmankerl Menü" (available with or without meat); the "Brotzeit" platter makes for an appealing starter. The modern decor and relaxed ambience are reminiscent of a chic, trendy gastropub; the courtyard terrace is also pretty. Extremely friendly waitstaff. Real passion has gone into curating the wine list – a fine selection of well-established names but also lesser-known or smaller winegrowers. They are also open for lunch on Wed (shorter menu). Tip for motorists: Parkhaus Urania is just a short (if steep) walk away.

Name: Café des Banques
Description: In the heart of Geneva, this chic bistro is the lair of Yoann Caloué who draws upon seasonal ingredients and high-flying technique to craft contemporary French cuisine. The classy yet cosily chummy setting provides the perfect foil to dishes of the likes of carpaccio of scallops enhanced by a sea urchin mayonnaise or free-range chicken served with a truffle-flavoured gravy. Wine buffs will be enchanted by the cellar, which stars mostly French wineries, with the odd international gem. The desserts are also meticulously crafted, illustrated by two signatures: soufflé à la chartreuse and lemon tart with a limoncello sabayon. This smart venue manages to combine fine dining with a friendly buzzy vibe.

Name: Kronenstübli
Description: Premium materials, tasteful fabrics and attractive Swiss stone pine panelling make for a cosy yet elegant atmosphere – just the right backdrop for the sophisticated cuisine served here, which combines classic French culinary tradition with Italian influences. Opt for one of two set menus: the "Degustations Menu" or the "Geniesser Menu". Alternatively, dishes are also served à la carte. Tip: Try the canard à la presse – pressed duck – (prepared for two or more diners, served in two courses). Wednesday special: Chateaubriand for two or more diners. The restaurant is housed in the long-established and stylishly luxurious Grand Hotel Kronenhof.

Name: La Cène
Description: This establishment draws the eye with its refined decor as much as it appeals with its two mouth-watering set menus: the weekly lunch menu ("Inspiration de la Semaine" – three to five courses) and the evening menu ("Éclosion des Sens" – three to seven courses), which is changed every month. Diners enjoy modern seasonal cuisine with a dash of creative flair, which keeps them coming back for more. A food and wine pairing event is also organised one evening a month.

Name: Langosteria
Description: This is a place to see and be seen on the ski slopes overlooking St Moritz. The terrace affords magnificent views, making it a delightful spot in which to savour freshly prepared Italian cuisine. Langoustine, lobster and fish, truffle pizza and pasta are the specialities of this restaurant, which also has locations in Milan, Paris and Paraggi. If you're not skiing, the best way to get here is by taxi.

Name: Alter Torkel - Huus vum Bündner Wii
Description: Even from the outside, Alte Torkel, which is located right on the road through the vineyards, is liable to turn heads! The old stone house at a remove from even its closest neighbours has maintained its regional charm while incorporating modern touches; the wine theme is omnipresent. In keeping with its "Fine Wineing" motto, the menu is characterised by a regularly changing selection of wines by the glass. A contemporary influence makes itself felt in the seasonal, regional cuisine. The hosts are cordial and dedicated. In summer, the terrace with a view of the valley is a real boon!

Name: Amaru by Claudia Canessa
Description: A taste of Peru in the heart of the Upper Engadine. At the restaurant housed in the elegant Kulm Hotel St. Moritz, the charming, attentive and professional front-of-house team welcomes you in a tastefully decorated and cheerful space with a handsome vaulted ceiling. In the kitchen, Claudia Canessa and her team bring Peruvian cuisine in all its diversity to the plate, cooking up modern-inspired dishes using excellent ingredients. Intriguing options include "El Choclo y sus Gambas" (grilled corn cake, red prawns, pickled onions, ocopa) and "Tuna Chicano" (aka ceviche).

Name: Carlton
Description: This tasteful, elegant restaurant is located in a building that was originally erected in 1913 to house a department store and later converted into the Carlton Elite hotel. The cuisine is classic and sophisticated and includes an appealing à la carte selection of dishes, such as beef tartare, Zürcher Kalbs-Geschnetzeltes ("Zurich-style creamy veal ragout"), cheese ravioli and crêpes Suzette. Additionally, there is a quick lunch option. On Saturdays, the popular brunch draws a crowd – diners can also opt for the regular menu or the lunch set menu. The impressive wine list features premium rare picks served by the glass. The dedicated and professional front-of-house team, who deliver exceptionally friendly and attentive service, deserve a special mention for helping to create a wonderfully welcoming atmosphere.

Name: parkhuus
Description: The restaurant at the luxurious Park Hyatt business hotel is spacious and high-ceilinged, with an open kitchen, upscale furnishings and sophisticated modern lighting; the glassed-in wine library is well worth seeing! The outstanding wine list features plenty of international labels and an interesting selection of natural and organic wines. The chefs serve up modern dishes that are available as a set menu or à la carte. If you're undecided, the grilled dishes won't disappoint – think top-quality tomahawk steak or a Chateaubriand for two, both irresistibly appetising. In season, opt for the delicious truffle gnocchi.

Name: Donatz
Description: Cornelia and René A Donatz run their business with great dedication. In an elegant rustic-style decor, they serve good fresh cuisine, including classic veal dishes, Angus beef "bestsellers", vegetarian fare and fish such as Graubünden salmon from Lostallo. They stock some 550 wines (20 available by the glass) – a passion of the owner. Donatz Wine Bar proposes a smaller menu. Hotel Donatz has appealingly modern and comfortable guestrooms.

Name: Belle Vue
Description: To make the most of the simply stunning mountain views, opt for a table near the large glass-planed front – or on the delightful terrace! Complementing the wonderful scenery, the interior of the Bellevue Parkhotel & Spa restaurant boasts a sleek design that provides the backdrop for the fine dining menu: classic dishes such as roast duck breast with potato galettes are served in the evening, and a simpler à la carte selection is available at lunchtime. Choose an accompaniment from the exquisite wine list.

Name: Silex
Description: This restaurant is located in a lively residential area. Plenty of Swiss produce goes into the modern seasonal dishes, which are created in the partially visible kitchen. The restaurant is just as committed to sustainability as it is to wine – selected by connoisseurs, the distinctive wine list is testament to a true passion for the grape. British-born chef George Tomlin, previously sous-chef at London's The Clove Club, expresses his creative streak in dishes such as grilled slices of elm mushroom with roasted black onions and a creamy buttermilk and roasted yeast espuma. You can opt to go à la carte or take the five-course tasting menu (also vegetarian). The vibe is deliberately kept simple, with a decor boasting clean lines, and there is a nice terrace outside, in front of this corner plot. The service is friendly and laid back. Just the sort of restaurant you would want in your neighbourhood!

Name: PRISMA Expérience
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Credit cards not accepted,Great view,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list,Terrace
Description: Housed in the exclusive Park Hotel Vitznau, which dates back to 1903, this summer-only restaurant whisks you away on a culinary voyage. Japanese fusion cuisine is the name of the game here. There is an omakase menu (for two or more diners) and an à la carte selection including sushi, sashimi, Wagyu dishes and ramen. The vibe is casually elegant. The location overlooking the lake is a draw in itself, and it goes without saying that tables on the terrace are highly coveted.

Name: dr Egge - das restaurant
Description: Located in Muttenz's old town, this compact restaurant delights diners with its stylish and charming decor, warm hospitality and Italian-inspired cuisine. They propose a seasonal set menu made using regional produce wherever possible; the "Piedmontese Autumn Menu", featuring Alba white truffles, is also a popular choice. Astute wine recommendations come courtesy of the maître d'.

Name: Spices Kitchen & Terrace
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: The manifold dining options available at Bürgenstock Hotel & Alpine Spa include this modern restaurant, which is the perfect choice for fans of Asian cuisine. Premium ingredients go into the Japanese, Chinese, Thai and Indian dishes prepared in an open kitchen. Tip: "Signature Peking duck" (by pre-order only). From the idyllic location overlooking the lake, at over 800m above sea level, the views are sensational – and the terrace is wonderful! Should you prefer to focus your attention on the action in the kitchen, book a table by the kitchen pass.

Name: Oliveiras
Description: The Oliveira family ensures a warm welcome in a harmonious, intentionally informal setting in which guests feel instantly at ease. Service is attentive, and the Mediterranean cuisine is based on top-quality ingredients, with fresh seafood a particular highlight. Be sure not to miss the zabaione with port wine and vanilla ice cream – available by pre-order only. Alongside an extensive à la carte menu, there is also a selection of daily specials and seasonal dishes. The food is complemented by an outstanding range of wines from the owner's Portuguese homeland, including fine ports, also available by the glass. A small boutique offers all kinds of delicacies to take home or give as gifts, such as wine, olives or fleur de sel from the Algarve. The annual summer party, featuring a selection of tapas, is especially popular.

Name: Hide & Seek
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Ayurvedic philosophy inspires a special section of the menu, where Mediterranean cooking styles and ingredients merge with chef Francesco Leone’s colorful and joyful vision. His cuisine, not without international influences, finds its ideal setting in this refined restaurant inside the “Giardino” hotel. In the warmer months, dining on the magnificent terrace overlooking the garden with its pond and floating flowers is unmissable: a corner of priceless tranquility and beauty.

Name: Gui
Address: 776 Eighth Ave., New York, NY, 10036, USA
Description: Dining in the heart of Times Square isn't always a good bet, but Gui Steakhouse changes the narrative. A natural choice for pre- or post-theater dining, it's also worth a trip without show tickets to experience their traditional steakhouse classics with Korean influences in a sleek setting. The menu includes raw bar items (Alaskan king crab leg) before shifting the focus to steaks with Japanese wagyu and USDA Prime meat. With a beautiful sear and deep grill marks, the aged steaks are finished simply with a sprinkling of Maldon salt. While steaks are indeed the raison d'être, the menu also features traditional American dishes like flounder and grilled chicken alongside Asian-inspired plates like dan dan noodles and wagyu fried rice.

Name: Lei
Address: 15-17 Doyers St., New York, NY, 10013, USA
Description: In the beating heart of Chinatown on Doyers Street, Annie Shi’s little wine bar is a gift to the city. Rare wines are everywhere. They line the edges of the room, and they fill countless boxes stacked to the ceiling. People are everywhere, too, sitting elbow to elbow and even spilling out onto tables in the alley. This talented team doesn’t waste a single square inch of space – and that includes the kitchen, which turns out smart, thoughtful modern Chinese dishes. Chilled celtuce with shallots and red wine vinegar is an essential starter. Other hits include fried whiting with seaweed, scallops with lily buds and ginger, and hand-rolled noodles with braised lamb.

Name: Muku
Address: 412 Greenwich St., New York, NY, 10013, USA
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list
Description: This intimate counter is a tight squeeze, but that’s hardly a deterrent for the guests who come for Chef Manabu Asanuma’s inspired kaiseki. From raw and grilled to simmered, steamed and fried, the menu is rooted heavily in the seasons, pulling ingredients from surrounding areas as well as prized seafood and meat from Japan. Soba noodles made with buckwheat from the chef's hometown are constant fixtures that read personal and sincere.
