Highlights on the menu include the new raw fish options, which have an entire tasting menu to themselves. The well-stocked wine cellar is home to around 500 labels which offer good value for money.

Name: Osteria del 36
Description: The oldest osteria in the town centre, this restaurant has delighted locals and visitors in two simple yet convivial dining rooms since 1880. The cuisine here is regional, with many dishes freshly prepared, from the excellent pasta to the delicious ice cream. Specialities include duck breast in a raspberry sauce with parmesan cheese flan.

Name: Boccadoro
Description: Situated on the outskirts of Padova, this classically elegant restaurant is very popular with locals – and for good reason! The menu includes skilfully prepared dishes influenced by the traditions of the Veneto (from bigoli pasta to dried cod and liver Venetian-style) as well as a selection of grilled options.

Name: Hosteria Giusti
Description: Hidden away in the back room of the historic Giusti delicatessen, which has been famous for over four centuries, is a small dining room with just four tables. Simple and pleasantly retro, this is th perfct place to soak up the authentic spirit of Modena’s culinary tradition. The menu is a sincere tribute to Emilian cuisine: it begins with charcuterie, mostly from Mora Romagnola pigs reared in the wild, accompanied by the ever-present gnocco fritto and fragrant pasta fritters.This is followed by fresh pasta, the star of iconic dishes such as tortellini in capon broth and tagliolini with cheese and walnuts, before moving on to slow-cooked meats, including the outstanding beef stew with mashed potatoes and the traditional recipe for fried cotechino with Sorbara zabaione, a precious tribute from the Morandi family. The desserts are simple yet delicious and include panna cotta with saba (grape syrup), a perfect blend of tradition and simplicity.All that’s left is to wish you “buon appetito”.

Name: Pomiroeu
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: This restaurant, one of the finest in the region, is run by its owner Giancarlo Morelli and celebrated its 30th anniversary in 2023. Situated in the centre of Seregno, the restaurant is intimate and welcoming with walls adorned with beautiful works of contemporary art thanks to the restaurant’s partnership with a local art gallery. The cuisine here is modern yet unfussy and the chef is equally at ease with meat, fish and vegetable dishes. One of the few restaurants around Monza to still offer a cheese course!

Name: Mondschein
Description: Surrounded by the lush greenery of the Dolomite valley of Sappada, Mondschein is one of the area's most refined and sought-after gourmet retreats. The Kratter family offers a warm welcome, wrapping guests in the authentic atmosphere of the Dolomites. The kitchen is filled with the aromas of the forest and local flavours, combined with the freshness of seasonal ingredients: don't miss the tender and juicy venison fillet, enhanced by a mountain pine sauce and the spicy sweetness of Williams pears with saffron. The wine list, which has been updated with new labels, invites you to make new discoveries thanks to KAVEAU, the spectacular wine cellar that's perfect for unforgettable tastings.

Name: Selvatico
Address: via Silvio Pellico 19, Rivanazzano Terme, 27055, Italy
Description: Opened in 1912 and now run by the fourth generation of the same family, this is one of the best restaurants in the Oltrepò Pavese area. If you’re looking to sample the gastronomic delights of this region, you’ll find a whole host of delicious specialities on offer, from charcuterie and stews to boiled meats and excellent fresh pasta. Attractively furnished guestrooms with the same nostalgic feel as the restaurant are also available.

Name: Antica Locanda dell'Orco
Description: Fans of Piedmontese cuisine will find one of its best exponents in this restaurant located at the end of a beautiful porticoed street in the historic centre. Savour some of Italy’s most exciting regional cooking, such as snails, bagna caoda, agnolotti pasta, a few (mainly freshwater) fish dishes, braised and boiled meats, “finanziera” stew and some extraordinary desserts – all prepared in magnificent style.

Name: Ai Cadelach
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Enjoy local cuisine in a rustic - style dining room or alongside the swimming pool in summer. The menu focuses on meat dishes (especially grilled meat), such as traditional Fiorentina steak and Alpago lamb. Wine lovers will appreciate the excellent wine cellar kept by Ezio, one of the restaurant owners.

Name: Hidalgo
Description: Although fish dishes also feature on the menu, Hidalgo has long enjoyed a reputation for its excellent meat options thanks to its different cuts of steak and beef sourced from all around the world and cooked mainly (but not exclusively) on the barbecue grill. In a separate room(Aomi, a kind of restaurant within the restaurant), guests can choose from a menu dedicated uniquely (from antipasti to main courses) to Japanese Wagyubeef.

Name: Perbacco
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: A rustic restaurant offering an authentic taste of the Cilento area, both in its setting amid lush Mediterranean vegetation and in its regional cuisine. Fish and seafood dominate the antipasti selection, while the main courses focus more on meat and vegetable dishes. Good choice of regional wines, plus the option of a few pleasant guestrooms.

Name: Giardinetto
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Set on the shores of the lake within the elegant hotel of the same name – now approaching its centenary after careful renovations – this restaurant boasts splendid views that extend as far as the Island of San Giulio. The cuisine is Mediterranean (try the beef bavette with red and yellow pepper sauce), with the occasional imaginative flourish (such as Carnaroli risotto with basil, black lemon, and purple shrimp). For an aperitif or after-dinner drink, a stop at the Roof Top Bar is recommended. In addition to cocktails, the wine cellar is also well stocked.

Name: Parma Rotta
Description: Here, where the Parma river once broke its banks, you’ll find an attractive trattoria with pleasant dining rooms and excellent cuisine cooked over a wood and herb fire, one of the oldest culinary techniques in the world. The main courses feature meat only, with an extensive number of dishes on the menu. Among the home-made desserts, we recommend the fior di latte ice-cream, which is served with a choice of delicious sauces.

Name: Lillotatini
Description: Situated in the heart of a small medieval castle-hamlet, this traditional-style restaurant with an attractive terrace on the historic square provides the setting for local specialities such as freshwater fish and truffles in season. There’s also a wine boutique on the same piazza (reached as soon as you come into the historic centre), which sells typical local produce and a good selection of wine. The latter also features on the restaurant’s wine list, with the family on hand to offer recommendations.

Name: Al Fogher
Address: strada statale 117 bis, Piazza Armerina, 94015, Italy
Description: This small, rustic-style restaurant just outside the centre of Piazza Armerina serves personalised cuisine in which local ingredients take pride of place. Influences from elsewhere in Italy are also evident, with dishes such as artichoke pansotti (stuffed pasta) with local tuma cheese and black truffles on the menu.

Name: I Sette Consoli
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: In this simple restaurant boasting an attractive outdoor summer garden and splendid views of the Duomo, chef Anna Rita focuses on carefully selected ingredients from the region and beyond. Highlights include soft egg, pan-fried courgettes and spring onions, and pecorino crumble, as well as soft veal rump, Jerusalem artichoke sauce and home-made pickled vegetables. The wine and spirits selection is particularly appealing – as an example, the Orvieto Classico Superiore Doc “Vendemmia Tardiva” is the perfect pairing for your dessert.

Name: L'Archivolto - Osteria Nostrale
Description: Situated on Ovada’s main square, this restaurant has the pleasant friendly ambience of an attractive trattoria, while its menu takes guests on a delicious culinary journey through the Piedmont region, including local specialities such as home-made tajarin pasta, braised beef in Ovada wine with mashed potato, and the local bunet dessert (finanziera stew also makes an occasional appearance). The restaurant is also a wine bar/boutique offering a selection of wines, including different Barolo labels (presented area by area) and aperitifs served both at lunchtime and dinner.

Name: Boccon DiVino
Address: via Traversa dei Monti 201, località Colombaio Tozzi, Montalcino, 53024, Italy
Description: In a farmhouse on the edges of town, this restaurant has a rustic dining area with modern tables and chairs and a beautiful summer terrace with a view. Authentic local dishes.

Name: Trattoria di Coronate
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Housed in a splendid 16C farmhouse just a few minutes from the beautiful Morimondo abbey, this restaurant has an elegant and friendly ambience. The wooden ceiling and furniture in the dining room gives it a warm and welcoming feel, while the cuisine, deeply rooted in the region, is reinterpreted with a contemporary twist. The delicious options on the menu include highlights such as quail breast with a thigh-meat “lollipop” and ravioli stuffed with braised beef.

Name: Alla Corte Lombarda
Description: Housed in a restored hay barn on the edge of the village, this charming rustic restaurant offers traditional cuisine with a modern twist, a good selection of wine and an excellent choice of beer. Every week, the menu features five records which played a major role in the history of music or which represent an important moment in time for the restaurant owner.

Name: Il Giardino "Da Felicin"
Description: A bastion of the most authentic Piedmontese tradition, the restaurant is located in a refined period building, enhanced by a panoramic terrace and a romantic garden that invites guests to dine al fresco in the summer months. The dining rooms reflect the same vocation for traditional, rich and welcoming elegance, which reaches its fullest expression in the dishes: impeccable interpretations of the region’s finest cuisine. The restaurant’s signature dish is the famous Tajarin di Felicin, handmade tagliatelle cut into very fine strands and served with a delicate Fassona beef sauce, a true declaration of love for local gastronomy. The property also comprises a scattered hotel, with rooms located both in the main building and in other parts of the village, including the charming Palazzo Boeri, which has a small spa accessible by reservation. The owners know the area intimately and are delighted to accompany guests on guided tours of the Langhe, organised by themselves or by local experts, making the stay a complete experience encompassing cuisine, hospitality and local culture.

Name: Josè - Tenuta Villa Guerra
Address: via Nazionale 414, Torre del Greco, 80059, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: 2025 has seen a change at the helm in the kitchen at this restaurant, in line with the new approach introduced by its enthusiastic owners. The full focus now is on the flavours of Campania, served in lighter versions and reinterpretations of traditional recipes. This is the case with the legendary Santa Rosa dessert, which has been transformed into a millefeuille made with the same ingredients as the original dish, namely crispy puff pastry, custard and sour cherries in syrup. Situated in one of the extraordinary villas on Herculaneum’s “Golden Mile”, the restaurant boasts an elegant dining room within this 18C building, as well as a room overlooking the villa gardens where pizzas are served from mid spring to late summer. Wine-enthusiasts will be pleased to hear that the wine cellar continues to offer an extensive selection of superb labels.

Name: Alpes
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: A warm and welcoming setting, where hospitality stands out for its authenticity and attention to detail. The kitchen, under the direction of chef Matthias Kirchler, presents creative, refined dishes designed for those who love to experiment and be surprised. The gastronomic offering has been recently refreshed and now unfolds in two distinct journeys: “Alpes 1988” and “Alpes – Locanda” for culinary experiences that narrate the territory with contemporary flair.

Name: Acaja
Description: Central, elegant and modern, the cuisine of Acaja winds along two lines: the traditional regional one, with ravioli del plin, cardi gobbi, Piedmont veal and hazelnuts to mention just a few that cannot be missed, but also various fish dishes that are truly remarkable, with a touch of contemporaneity, starting with excellent raw materials.

Name: La Corte
Address: via San Pancrazio 41, Palazzolo sull'Oglio, 25036, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated in a tranquil street that leads to the centre of Palazzolo sull’Oglio, this stylish restaurant in a 16C palazzo boasts classic elegance and authentic cuisine which is delicious and full of flavour. Here, Aldo and his wife offer guests a warm and informal welcome, always with a smile on their lips.

Name: Da Miky
Address: via Fegina 104, Monterosso al Mare, 19016, Italy
Description: Situated right on the seafront, Miky is one of the best restaurants for fish (including the famous Monterosso anchovies) in the region, serving cuisine which is more and more exciting and individual every year. There’s also a small shop selling local produce.

Name: Fattoria delle Torri
Description: A slice of paradise in Modica’s historic center: delightful terrace service among the lemon trees and a professional legacy carried on by chef-patron Peppe Barone's daughters – Carla in the dining room and Francesca in the kitchen – who, with youthful energy, champion a highly original gastronomic offering that balances innovation with tradition. They draw freely from the island’s culinary history, enriching it with exotic touches often characterized by gently spiced flavors. The wine program is equally impressive: guests can confidently rely on their recommendations to discover something special from an excellent wine list.

Name: Rovello
Description: Although it has a new venue, this restaurant has retained its attractive friendly feel in a dining room that acts as a backdrop for substantial dishes that are full of flavour. The interesting classic menu is supplemented by a selection of daily specials, with meat, fish and vegetables (some grilled) prepared in front of guests seated at the counter. There are also traditional tables available and the lively atmosphere remains that of a friendly, welcoming bistro. Interesting wine list, including a selection of wines by the glass.

Name: La Cantina di Manuela
Description: The dining room in this young, dynamic restaurant is surrounded by bottles of wine. Elaborate dishes feature on the menu, with antipasti available in the evening. At lunchtime these are replaced by various salads aimed at a business clientele in a hurry. Milanese-style cutlets are the house speciality.

Name: Darmagi
Description: Small villa in secluded position with warm, homely atmosphere, especially in the nice room with fireplace. The range of cuisine is rich in tradition.

Name: Oberwirt
Description: The various Stube-style dining rooms in the Oberwirt hotel restaurant have a typical Tyrolean ambience, while the cuisine is decidedly traditional with its focus on seasonal and regional ingredients, although a few fish options also always feature on the menu. Wine-enthusiasts will be delighted with the excellent wine selection.

Name: Da Guido
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: The story of this restaurant began in 1967, when Guido and Giovanna Fasan, both of whom still work front of house, met in Germany. They then moved back to Italy, where they opened Da Guido in the early 1990s. White dominates in the elegant, contemporary-style dining room, while the cuisine features delicious seafood dishes with a focus on grilled specialities. In summer, there’s a welcoming bar in the garden, which boasts a romantic atmosphere. Together with other restaurant-owners from Venice, the Fasan family also have a kitchen garden on the Venetian island of Sant’Erasmo, where they focus on sustainable growing methods.

Name: Taverna dello Spuntino
Description: This Roman trattoria has a particularly striking interior. From its old tufa passageways converted into a wine cellar in the basement, to its charming dining rooms with brick - vaulted arches where hams, flasks of wine, fruit and antipasti all form an attractive display.

Name: Cacciani
Description: Run by the Cacciani family for over 100 years, this is one of the most famous restaurants in the Castelli Romani region. Demonstrating a real passion for regional traditions and eschewing modern trends, the menu continues to offer a tempting selection of delicious classic dishes from Lazio, including a couple of fish and seafood options. The focaccia, which is served with the bread, is superb and well worth a mention. The dining room evokes the long history of the restaurant, while the panoramic terrace is a firm favourite with guests.

Name: Villa Fiordaliso
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Villa Fiordaliso is one of the beautiful early 20C villas that line the lake front. Surrounded by verdant grounds and extending out into the lake on a pontoon, the restaurant serves creative, modern cuisine. The chef is equally at ease with fish and meat dishes, while the extensive wine list suggests that you’ll have no problem finding your favourite wine here.

Name: Cibrèo
Address: via A. Del Verrocchio 8r, Florence, 50122, Italy
Description: A historic city institution and essential stop for anyone arriving in Florence, this establishment welcomes guests into an elegant dining room reminiscent of a private salon, where cordial, graciously measured service frames a cuisine of bold flavors. The menu features dishes that have become true house classics, capable of narrating the territory through recipes that time has rendered iconic: pork liver with lemon, tortellini timbale, squab stuffed with mostarda, rabbit roulade with sweet-and-sour borettane onions, and, to close, a cheese tart accompanied by marmalade.

Name: Osteria della Chiocciola
Description: Situated in the historic centre just a stone’s throw from the elegant Via Roma, this restaurant is decorated with paintings by renowned modern artists such as Pignatelli, Andy Warhol, and Valerio Berruti. The ground floor is home to a colourful wine bar, while the first-floor restaurant serves traditional cuisine made from local ingredients with a natural focus on Piedmont. There is also a fish alternative for non-meat-eaters.

Name: Osteria del Teatro
Description: Enjoy traditional cuisine served in an elegant 16C dining room with a fireplace or in more informal trattoria-style rooms, all of which are decorated with the same theatrical theme. The recipes here focus on local traditions and seasonal ingredients, resulting in colourful, well-balanced and beautifully presented dishes that are full of flavour. There are just a few tables on the small terrace near the entrance overlooking the pedestrianised street, so if you want to sit outdoors make sure you book well in advance.

Name: Capuccina
Address: via Novara 19/b, località Capuccina, Cureggio, 28060, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: A new chef brings fresh energy and ideas to this excellent restaurant housed within a 16C farmhouse that is surrounded by countryside and which has been restored in elegant style. The chef focuses on top-quality ingredients (including vegetables from his own kitchen garden) and contemporary recipes with a classic flavour, as well as personalised recipes created with just a few ingredients for which he uses a mix of classic methods (“express” cooking) and modern techniques (such as whipping siphons) in order to achieve comforting, classic flavours. With the exception of a few champagnes, the wine selection showcases the region, especially the areas closest to Upper Piedmont, such as Ghemme, Gattinara, Boca and Sizzano – plus, of course, the wines produced on the restaurant’s own estate!

Name: La Casa degli Spiriti
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: The Chignola family has been welcoming guests in this beautiful, restored farmhouse – a luxurious residence with one of the best views of Lake Garda – for almost 30 years. The ground-floor restaurant occupies a veranda surrounded by greenery facing the lake, where young chef Filippo (following in his father’s footsteps), together with his brother, sommelier Lorenzo, offers his guests an enjoyable dining experience with its roots in the cuisine of the region. The first-floor terrace is home to the Osteria Spiritino, which is more informal in both its cuisine and atmosphere. The remarkable wine cellar is housed in a room adorned with 18C frescoes and boasts more than 1 300 labels, with a particular focus on champagnes.

Name: Al Vedel
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: A temple for the production of culatello ham, which features in the dishes served and is also matured on the premises for 16, 26 and 38 months (the maturing rooms are well worth a visit), Al Vedel also serves other regional specialities, as well as more imaginative fare. The menu features dishes such as gran lesso(boiled beef and chicken), delicious Bourguignon-style snails, tortel dols (tortelli pasta with a hint of sweetness), and specialities enhanced by the addition of Parmesan cheese and red butter. The wine list, which will delight Lambrusco enthusiasts, boasts a selection of French wines including newer wine-producing regions, while the cheese trolley offers a selection of regional and Italian cheeses, plus a few options from further afield such as the famous Brillat-Savarin with its natural, bloomy rind.

Name: Osteria dell'Oca Bianca
Description: In the heart of a small town, right opposite the church, this typical village osteria run by a friendly family serves traditional cuisine from the Piedmont, including goose dishes. Highlights here include the rustic, welcoming ambience, an excellent wine list, a wine cellar that can be visited, and a charming glass-fronted veranda that is used throughout the year. There are also three beautiful guestrooms reserved exclusively for guests dining at the restaurant.

Name: Maré
Description: An unexpected gourmet restaurant in a typical private beach club on the Romagna coast, with a dining room that opens on to the beach with its long line of beach umbrellas. The dishes here are carefully prepared and occasionally creative, and all of good quality. We particularly recommend the monfettini (fresh egg pasta) mantecati with cuttlefish, clams, mantis shrimp and ink, which looks like a risotto and is absolutely delicious. The restaurant also boasts a good wine list with an excellent choice of champagnes.

Name: Antinè
Description: Manuel Bouchard’s cuisine impressed our inspectors thanks to striking features such as his minimalist approach and the use of just a few well-chosen ingredients. Traditional recipes, such as plin pasta and Fassona beef, feature on the menu alongside more alternative, imaginative dishes (including fresh- and saltwater fish). This elegant restaurant is located on the first floor of a building in the old town, just a stone’s throw from the medieval tower. A warm welcome and an excellent wine list complete the picture.

Name: Il Giardino delle Esperidi
Description: The ideal starting point for an exploration of the area. In summer one dines on the terrace located in the heart of the historic centre while drinks and cigars may be enjoyed in the lounge bar.

Name: Anticofurlo
Cuisine: Cuisine from the Marches, Country cooking
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated in the Gola del Furlo nature reserve, this restaurant is run by the talented and enthusiastic Alberto and Roberta, whose focus is on typical Marche specialities such as fresh pasta, cured hams, fine cheese and regional dishes full of intense flavours. The picturesque grotto carved into the rock is perfect for an aperitif, while the inn also offers charming guestrooms.

Name: Il Carpaccio
Description: This traditional family restaurant has been recently renovated to include an attractive veranda - style dining room overlooking the valley. Typical dishes from Calabria, fish specialities by prior arrangement, and a good wine list all add to the appeal.

Name: Ventuno.1
Description: Major refurbishment at this renowned restaurant has given it the feel of an informal, welcoming contemporary bistro. Don’t miss the “Napule” tasting menu, which offers just four courses that include anchovies, cavatelli pasta and Neapolitan meatballs in a nod to the owner’s origins. Over time, the wine list has been extended and improved, with wines now stored in a new cellar. Some of the ingredients used in the dishes can also be purchased to take home.

Name: Hostaria dai Musi
Description: Offering a limited à la carte that includes fish dishes (including raw options) which vary according to market availability, this restaurant serves what it describes as “typically modern cuisine”. The breaded and fried anchovies with a typical saor sauce and lemon gel are particularly good, plus the menu features a selection of typical regional specialities. The pleasant setting is enhanced by the backdrop of the large central square in a pedestrianised area on the edge of the old town. The drinks menu includes an excellent selection of wines, plus a good choice of local beers.

Name: L'Argaj
Description: In the Piedmontese dialect, “Argaj” translates as “satisfaction” and never has a restaurant name seemed more appropriate! In a simple, colourful and welcoming dining room with windows looking out at the hills, all the attention is on cuisine which is skilfully balanced between classic local dishes and more creative options. Choose between the à la carte and tasting menus, all accompanied by an excellent array of wines.

Name: Trattoria del Bivio
Description: Situated in the Alta Langa region, this old restored farmhouse is now home to an elegant restaurant with a contemporary rural feel. The cuisine has strong regional roots, although the menu also offers a few fishes dishes. Excellent food, as well as attractive guestrooms and a warm, family welcome.

Name: I Salotti
Address: Località Querce al Pino, SS 146, Chiusi, 53043, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: In summer, the outdoor spaces at this restaurant, such as the garden gazebo, are the perfect setting for a candlelit dinner in total harmony with nature, while in winter, the elegant dining room with just a few tables (book well in advance) really comes into its own. Creative cuisine prepared using produce from the restaurant’s own farm, plus a long-established wine cellar that has been extended over the years so that it now boasts over two thousand different labels from the region and elsewhere in Italy, many of them of the highest quality.

Name: Oste Scuro - Finsterwirt
Description: Run by the capable Mayr family, this long-established restaurant has occupied a narrow alleyway in the centre of Bressanone (Brixen) since the late 19C. The dining rooms are decorated in historic style, plus there’s a charming terrace for alfresco dining in summer. Dishes are presented on different tasting menus with the option of choosing traditional or Mediterranean dishes à la carte style – we highly recommend the soup made with Sylvaner wine from the Isarco valley, as well as the Tirtl (fried pie) stuffed with ricotta, spinach and cinnamon. Guestrooms are available in the nearby and recently renovated Adler Historic Guesthouse, which is run by the same owners.

Name: Gardenia
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Even if you’re not a local, you’ll feel at home in this old property which offers a glimpse of the Piedmont of old. In this 19C building, with a typical balcony running along the inner courtyard, the atmosphere continues into the dining room and, of course, via the dishes on the menu. Here, regional cuisine is celebrated to the full, so you’ll find lots of meat (although some fish options are also available), accompanied by a carefully selected choice of vegetables, some grown in the restaurant’s own garden.

Name: Enoclub
Description: Under the arcades of a piazza named after Michele Ferrero, this restaurant has a simple, modern ambience with walls covered with wine bottles, as well as friendly, efficient female staff. The beautifully prepared Piedmontese cuisine is showcased on an à la carte and a tasting menu, accompanied by an impressive selection of quality wine.

Name: Il Labirinto
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated on the outskirts of Brescia, this long-established restaurant has an elegant retro atmosphere. The cuisine is varied with a balance of fish and meat dishes, as well as some excellent home-made salumi.

Name: Già Sotto l'Arco
Description: A historic gourmet restaurant, with an equally historic setting on the first floor of a Baroque palazzo fronting an elegant piazza. A fine staircase leads up to the simple yet stylish dining room, which is skilfully run by a father and his offspring. In the kitchen, his wife Teresa prepares creative, regional cuisine, which is showcased on a surprise menu of four, six or eight courses and which features locally sourced meat and fish. If you’d like to book the delightful table on the small terrace for just two fortunate guests, make sure you do so well in advance!

Name: La Locanda del Cardinale
Description: Situated right in the centre of Assisi, La Locanda del Cardinale occupies an old palazzo that is home to the perfectly preserved remains of a Roman villa dating back to 70 BC in a fascinating setting full of history. The menu features simple recipes such as crispy organic egg with asparagus and marjoram, as well as more elaborate, modern dishes including quail and Danubio brioche with barbecued artichoke. It’s not surprising that this delightful restaurant also boasts a superb wine cellar with an array of carefully chosen labels that will delight wine-enthusiasts.

Name: Il Focarile
Address: via Pontina al km 46,5, Aprilia, 04011, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: The sumptuous entrance gives an elegant tone to a large bright dining room with well spaced out tables; a touch of Tuscany with a cuisine rich in tradition and also imagination. Now offers new, elegant rooms.

Name: Loewengrube
Description: This building has been home to a restaurant since the 16C, making it one of the oldest in Bolzano. Today, it houses an elegant restaurant that serves a combination of traditional Alpine cuisine and classic Italian dishes with a contemporary twist, such as carbonara tortelloni filled with parmesan cream and crispy bacon. The attractive wine cellar boasts a selection of approximately 1 000 wines from the region and elsewhere in Italy, as well as a choice of labels from further afield.

Name: La Fermata
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated in the countryside around Alessandria, this 18C farmhouse has a modern, minimalist decor which serves as a backdrop for traditional Piedmontese cuisine with a clever modern twist. The chef offers just the right amount of creativity, with a focus on meat dishes and satisfying, memorable flavours that appeal to the emotions as well as the palate. Adding to the appeal, the elegant and exclusive ambience is the result of careful refurbishment, which has transformed the dining room into a triumph of art and design.

Name: Scatto
Description: From Turin’s beautiful Piazza San Carlo, you head through an inner courtyard to this restaurant’s modern dining room with its open-view kitchen. Whether you opt for the à la carte or tasting menu, you’ll find a balance of traditional Piedmontese and more creative dishes, all of remarkable quality, in keeping with the superb wine list.

Name: Enotavola Pino
Description: The entrance to this restaurant through a wine-bar can be misleading, as you are eventually led to simpler – yet still attractive – dining rooms. In this wine-themed setting, enjoy delicious fish and seafood dishes with the occasional creative twist, accompanied by a particularly extensive selection of wines by the glass. A highly recommended address for fish lovers, situated right in the historic centre of Padova.

Name: Il Presidente
Description: This restaurant situated in an alleyway in the historic centre just a few metres from Lucera’s cathedral was once home to the stables of an old palazzo. Nowadays, the dining room, which is crowned by a stone-vaulted ceiling and decorated in shades of black and gold, is divided into small sections. In the middle room, two glass cabinets display the wine selection, which includes over 400 different labels. The Mediterranean cuisine served here is modern and colourful, with vegetables often accompanying the fish and seafood (red tuna, swordfish, prawns, scampi and a delicious seafood focaccia). The ingredients are more important than the technique, resulting in dishes that are full of flavour!

Name: Osteria della Tana
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Occupying the same red house as chef Alessandro Dal Degan’s gourmet restaurant, the Osteria is where this chef turns his attention to more traditional dishes (meat and fish alike), which are often typical of the Veneto. There’s one highly recommended exception, however, namely the carbonara dell’Osteria, prepared with top-quality ingredients from the region. Also popular are the baccalà alla vicentina (dried cod) and the tripe, both of which feature on a menu with a wide selection, including renowned dishes from the chef’s Michelin-starred restaurant. Full-bodied flavours that will delight the palate.

Name: Belé
Description: The name says it all – in Milanese dialect, “belé” means beautiful and the word is an apt description of this restaurant. Belé is charmingly lit by an old-fashioned chandelier on which the pieces of crystal create a play of light resembling an intricate and shifting lace-like pattern. The dining rooms are decorated in attractive contemporary colours, with comfortable armchairs and acoustics perfect for conversation, while the cuisine is Italian with a modern twist.

Name: Regio Patio
Address: via San Francesco d'Assisi 23, Garda, 37016, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated just a stone’s throw from the lakefront, this elegant gourmet restaurant in the Regina Adelaide hotel boasts an attractive English-style garden, as well as a beautiful outdoor patio and stylish dining room. The two tasting menus served here (one of which is vegetarian) showcase the traditions of Lake Garda and its surrounding region with a creative twist using vegetables from the property’s own kitchen garden and a focus on seasonal ingredients. There’s also an extensive wine list.

Name: Degli Angeli
Address: località Madonna degli Angeli, Magliano Sabina, 02046, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: The family that runs this excellent restaurant with views of the surrounding countryside has been doing so with passion and experience for over a century. The menu here focuses on the culinary traditions of Lazio, while the wine and extra-virgin olive oil are also sourced locally. The restaurant is part of a hotel and includes a shop (La Bottega delle Delizie) selling home-made produce.

Name: La Sosta di Ottone III
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Ask for directions to this restaurant when you make your booking, as you’ll need to leave your vehicle in a private car park at the entrance to the village and then make your way uphill on foot. Situated in one of Liguria’s most typical and picturesque inland villages with views of the sea in the distance, the restaurant offers outdoor dining under a wisteria in fine weather. The menu features a small choice of regional dishes, with the same focus on Liguria evident on the wine list.

Name: Ristorante 1500
Address: via Pavicolo 43, funivia per raggiungere il ristorante in via Villa 3, San Vigilio, 39011, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: If you’re not a guest at the Vigilius Mountain Resort hotel where this restaurant is situated, leave your car in Lana and take the short cable-car ride to Monte San Vigilio where you’ll find the hotel at an altitude of 1 500m (remember to check the time of the last cable-car back down the mountain!). Decorated with a profusion of wood, the dining room has the same modern feel as the creative mountain cuisine prepared by the new chef who started here in 2023.

Name: Vino Buono
Address: via Castello 20, Grumello del Monte, 24064, Italy
Description: Housed in an old 17C hay barn near the Duomo belltower, this interesting wine bar offers around 400 different labels to accompany an array of simple yet well-prepared traditional dishes (including a few fish options). Highlights include the cured hams, cheeses and the excellent starters.

Name: Osteria dai Mazzeri
Description: Once the town hall of Follina, this building dating from 1704 is now home to a restaurant run by two brothers who serve delicious regional and seasonal cuisine here (in winter, don’t miss the spit-roasted chicken, veal or pork cooked according to local tradition). A beautiful mulberry tree, the old symbol of the village that was once involved in the silk industry, graces the outdoor dining space.

Name: Crotto Valtellina
Address: via Fiume 11, località Valle, Malnate, 21046, Italy
Description: For over fifty years, the Valbuzzi family has passionately maintained this enchanting crotto, now in its third generation. The atmosphere is elegant and refined while staying true to its origins: wooden tables, period furnishings, and floral touches. The summer veranda is particularly stunning, overlooking a lush garden and a sandstone wall. The menu balances traditional dishes – the pizzoccheri are unmissable – with more contemporary creations, such as the surprising saltimbocca of larded quail with fennel, escarole gel, pickled apricot, and zucchini flan.

Name: Vesta Versilia
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Within Twiga beach establishment, in a setting where glamour reigns supreme, multifunctional and equipped with a fully-fledged cocktail bar, the cuisine favours classic recipes and cooking methods, but equally dedicates substantial attention to raw seafood and pizza. From our last visit, the seafood salad—boasting excellent varied citronette-dressed products—and the roast turbot with lemon potato cream and asparagus stand particularly memorable.

Name: Buca di Sant'Antonio
Description: Just a stone’s throw from Piazza San Michele, in the heart of the beautiful historic centre of Lucca, the Buca di Sant'Antonio dates back over 200 years and is one of the city’s oldest restaurants. Here, the old copper pots and pans that hang from the ceiling add to the warm, romantic atmosphere, while the cuisine unsurprisingly showcases traditional Tuscan specialities including meat dishes and home-made pasta.

Name: La Tavola
Description: Situated in the Il Porticciolo hotel, this restaurant will appeal to anyone who enjoys a lakeside setting. Leave your car in the car park, then take the lift down to the dining room with its windows overlooking the water and views of the opposite shore of Lake Maggiore. The lake also takes pride of place in some of the creative, elaborate and personalised dishes on the two tasting menus created by Riccardo Bassetti, many of which feature freshwater fish.

Name: Cavalluccio Marino
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Imaginative cuisine in a long - established family restaurant, which is constantly updated by each generation. Situated away from the town centre, yet easily accessible, the restaurant boasts an enviable seafront location. Excellent selection of local and international wines.

Name: Filippino
Description: Opened in 1910, this historic restaurant with an attractive shaded outdoor pergola serves an extensive array of delicious local fish prepared in traditional recipes. Enjoy a selection of typical Sicilian specialities prepared by the Bernardi family, including the highly recommended “Nonno Filippino” fish soup.

Name: Botero
Description: Close to the city centre and within walking distance of the Cathedral, the restaurant is located inside a beautiful historic building. The cuisine is fragrant, and the menu alternates between seafood offerings – depending on market availability – and a few more traditional recipes such as hand-pinched Cremasque sweet tortelli. The jovial welcome, streamlined service and interesting wine list (with a few "natural" offerings for enthusiasts) explain why there is a large turnout for both lunch and dinner. Better make reservations!

Name: L'Antico Giardino
Description: A menu steeped in Ferrarese tradition, served in a welcoming and comfortable setting, energised by the friendly charm of the owner, who offers valuable advice on the best wine and food pairings. The menu features flavours that seduce the senses: passatelli drenched in a velvety cheese fondue, enriched with morel mushrooms and the intense aroma of autumn black truffle ; soft and juicy larded pheasant breast accompanied by a Castelmagno custard that caresses the palate; and to finish, a soft cheesecake with double white chocolate and pistachio mousse, topped with a blackberry sauce. And, season permitting, the king of the table: the white truffle, ready to enhance every dish.

Name: Entrà
Address: via Salde Entrà 60, località Entrà, Finale Emilia, 41034, Italy
Description: Siblings Antonio and Elvira uphold a family tradition which, since the 1960s, has been part of a much longer history, that of the simple wine shop which occupied these premises immediately after the Great War. For generations, the Previdi family has been a benchmark for authentic, fragrant and delicious Emilian cuisine, prepared — as they like to recall — “without revisiting it, whenever possible”.Fresh, homemade pasta is a real highlight, starting with the legendary pumpkin tortelli – made famous in part by the restaurant’s proximity to the province of Mantua – served with a savoury meat sauce. The signature dish, however, is roast guinea fowl, served on a bed of seasonal salad and accompanied by tender roast potatoes, in the truest family tradition.The wine selection is also of the highest standard, comprising around 400 labels and reflecting a carefully considered choice that complements the dishes on the menu.

Name: Carlo Magno
Description: This restaurant housed in a country house dating from the 19C offers elegant period dining rooms and a menu featuring Mediterranean-style meat and fish dishes.

Name: Alle Codole
Address: via XX Agosto 27, Canale d'Agordo, 32020, Italy
Description: ‘Codole’ is the nickname of the family that runs this restaurant and whose ancestors once worked in the copper mines in the area. Today, the family has exchanged mining for cooking, offering guests specialities such as ricotta and black truffle ravioli with a lamb ragù, and loin of venison in a porcini mushroom crust.

Name: Al Carroponte
Description: This restaurant is renowned in Bergamo for its good choice of varied cuisine, as well as its lively, buzzing atmosphere. The excellent, tasty cuisine is typically Italian and contemporary in style, and includes luxury ingredients such as lobster and caviar, while the “classic” à la carte is enhanced by a selection of cured hams, cheeses and finger food. But the Carroponte’s beating heart is owner Oscar Mazzoleni’s passion for the world of wine, with an incredible selection of over 2 000 labels that puts many more famous Michelin-starred restaurants in the shade.

Name: Frosio
Description: Housed in a 18C villa which was once a family inn, this restaurant is run by the dynamic and professional Frosio family who have over 30 years’ experience in the restaurant business. There’s nothing improvised about Frosio, where Camillo guarantees impeccable service front of house and Paolo is at the helm in the kitchen, creating carefully prepared dishes with a classic-modern feel and an equal emphasis on meat and fish, often made from traditionally “gourmet” ingredients such as scampi, lobster, foie gras, pigeon and caviar. The wine list continues to offer an excellent selection, with one section dedicated to Italy and another to the rest of the world. Decorated with austere elegance, the wine cellar in the 13C tower is home to more than 1,000 different labels. Attractive outdoor space for summer dining.

Name: Crotasc
Address: via Don Primo Lucchinetti 63, Mese, 23020, Italy
Description: Here one can choose between a smaller, typically mountain style dining room with stone paving and open fireplace and a larger room in contemporary rustic style. In summer one can dine on the terrace.

Name: La Nicchia
Description: This tastefully decorated restaurant is housed in a late - 18C building, which is marked on an old map from the Napoleonic era. Here the best local ingredients are selected to create regional dishes with the occasional contemporary twist.

Name: Wine & Dine
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list
Description: In the center of Canazei, this restaurant within the Croce Bianca hotel occupies wood-paneled rooms that foster a relaxed, informal atmosphere, with a few charming alcoves for more intimate seating. Meat reigns supreme, notably a dedicated selection of grilled main courses. A good assortment of wines by the glass.

Name: Antica Osteria dei Camelì
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Within a sixteenth-century farmhouse of intact charm, where historic rooftops now coexist with modern solar panels in a clear gesture of sustainability, this establishment represents a successful encounter between classic elegance and contemporary design. The kitchen follows the same philosophy: tradition and innovation converse naturally in dishes that place great emphasis on nutritional balance and wholesome pairings, thanks in part to ingredients sourced from trusted local farmers. In the dining room, service maintains the professionalism and courtesy it has always been known for, affirming an approach that has lost neither rigor nor warmth over time.

Name: Paolo Teverini
Address: via del Popolo 2, Bagno di Romagna, 47021, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated in the charming village of Bagno di Romagna and surrounded by beautiful rolling hills, Teverini is a culinary name full of history in this area. The cuisine here demonstrates different traditions – the local region is an obvious influence (especially its mushrooms), but fish and seafood also feature along with plenty of vegetables.

Name: La Fiaschetteria
Description: Enjoy excellent reinterpretations of Emilian classics in this large 18C farmhouse. It is lit with modern designer - style chandeliers and warmed by a large fireplace. The three splendid guestrooms combine elegance with typical regional decor.

Name: Bistrot La Perla
Address: strada Col Alt 105, Corvara in Badia, 39033, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Credit cards not accepted,Interesting wine list
Description: The venue unfolds around a large wooden bar, where the bartenders are constantly at work making excellent cocktails that complement an impressive wine list. We are in the bistro of the famous La Perla hotel: a tribute to mountain life, expressed through charming and cheerful décor that creates a lively and welcoming atmosphere. The menu invites you to indulge in mouth-watering dishes inspired by the classic flavours of Italian tradition, while live music plays in the background.The venue is open practically all day: a café in the morning, a bistro at lunchtime, a more informal menu from 2 pm to 4 pm, and then it’s time for aperitifs, with cocktails and snacks, before moving on to dinner.

Name: Carne & Spirito
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: On the outskirts of the city, meat lovers will find their paradise: the kitchen selects some of the finest breeds from around the world (almost exclusively beef), offered in a variety of cuts and cooked over a Josper charcoal oven: an authentic delight. Cured meats also feature among the starters, alongside fresh pasta and excellent desserts. The wine selection is equally noteworthy, as is the selection by the glass.

Name: San Marco
Description: This long-established restaurant in the Asti region has been open for over 70 years and is still renowned for its regional cuisine served in a classic-style dining room. Dishes include cardoons with Raschera cheese fondue, the famous agnolottini del plin (a type of ravioli), and the Piedmontese bunet which is part of a trio of desserts that includes hazelnut tart and zabaglione with Canelli muscat wine.

Name: Trattoria del Cimino dal 1895
Description: Situated on the hill leading to Palazzo Farnese, this restaurant occupies the oldest building in the village, which dates back to 1370. Since 1940, the Calistri family have been delighting their guests with delicious specialities from Lazio, including excellent cured hams, fresh pasta (the amatriciana is particularly renowned), grilled meats and an impressive selection of cheeses and desserts. Excellent wines and spirits complete the picture.

Name: L'Angolo d'Abruzzo
Description: For almost 40 years, the Angolo d’Abruzzo restaurant has been home to the Centofanti family, who act as excellent ambassadors for the region by offering the best local, seasonal ingredients (for this reason, there’s no fish on the menu) in a host of traditional, regional recipes. Specialities such as cured meats, local ham, bruschetta and fresh home-made pasta (including “chitarra” spaghetti) take centre stage, while main courses include grilled dishes (porcini mushrooms in season, cuts of beef and veal at any time of year) and stews (mutton etc). When it comes to dessert, make sure you try the crème caramel with Navelli saffron. A misleading sign saying “sagrestia” (sacristy) actually indicates the well-stocked wine cellar.

Name: Zest
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Garden or park,Interesting wine list
Description: Situated within the luxury Grand Hotel La Favorita in the centre of Sorrento, this restaurant has the traditional, opulent style of a grand hotel, while the outdoor terrace boasts the romantic charm that is typical of Sorrento. In the kitchen, the chef creates top-quality cuisine influenced by Campania and enhanced by his own creative touches, resulting in dishes such as marinated tuna with tonka beans, lemon gel and ginger mousse. There’s also a vegetarian tasting menu. A recently opened restaurant that is growing in popularity.

Name: Lalibera
Description: In the heart of Alba, just a stone's throw from the cathedral, this refined and elegant restaurant offers guests a warm and simple welcome. The aromas of Langhe cuisine combine with the chefs' creativity to deliver dishes that reflect the region's flavours with taste and harmony. The wine list, lovingly curated by Flavia, offers a wide variety of labels, with Piedmontese wines taking centre stage.

Name: Il Ristorante - Niko Romito
Description: Situated on the fifth floor of the Hotel Bulgari with views of the Mausoleum of Augustus, this restaurant boasts a spacious terrace and an elegant, welcoming dining room with mahogany walls adorned with works of art. To reach the dining room, you pass through the lively bar, where we recommend starting your evening with an aperitif. Once at your table, the menu offers a concrete expression of three-Michelin-star chef Niko Romito’s culinary approach which focuses on lighter, elegant versions of traditional Italian dishes in a sequence of courses that take guests on a culinary tour around Italy. Spaghetti in tomato sauce, artichoke soup, saffron risotto and Piedmontese “bonet” dessert all feature on the menu.

Name: Ora d'Aria
Description: Behind the Uffizi, a restaurant that stands out for its originality: not so much for its impressively large open kitchen as for its deliberate desire to encourage direct interaction between guests and the kitchen team. The restaurant’s name reflects its proximity to the historic Florentine prison of Le Murate and its intention to offer customers a moment of genuine relaxation.A small but charming dining room adorned with a ceiling frescoed with gold leaf, houses Marco Stabile’s delightful restaurant, where he serves creative cuisine that is equally accomplished in both meat and fish dishes, all based on top-quality ingredients.

Name: Locanda del Falco
Address: Castello di Rivalta 4, Rivalta Trebbia, 29010, Italy
Description: In the heart of a medieval village, an inn of authentic charm welcomes guests with the captivating aromas of Piacenza cuisine and imaginative creations that surprise the palate, accompanied by local wines and distinguished labels from beyond the region. Heritage dishes speak of time-honored flavors, revisited with modern touches, while the inspector’s favorite remains the grilled cardoncelli mushrooms: intense aroma and pure taste. In the dining room, Sabrina radiates energy and smiles, presenting each dish with infectious passion. On winter evenings, crackling fireplaces warm the atmosphere; in summer, a centuries-old wisteria drapes the courtyard tables in fragrant, romantic shade.

Name: La Gallina
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Villa Sparina is an elegant rural resort named after its winery. The Orangerie hosts guests, whereas fine dining unfolds at La Gallina restaurant: amid rustic and elegant settings, contemporary cuisine is presented, drawing ample inspiration from Piedmont, as well as nearby Liguria (the dessert "Good Morning Genoa" playfully mimics breakfast with cappuccino and focaccia), though Campania references, the chef's homeland, never falter. Personalised wine selection: exclusively Champagne, abundant Piedmont choices, with particular emphasis on Barbaresco and Barolo.

Name: Posillipo
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Boasting a superb location above Gabicce Monte, this excellent restaurant run by the third generation of the same family offers breathtaking views of the Romagna coast. Posillipo’s superb fish dishes have made the restaurant a must in the region, with options ranging from raw fish to traditional pasta specialities and delicious main courses (fish fillets, catch of the day, fried options etc). There’s also a tempting dessert trolley and an excellent wine list.

Name: Da Poli alla Stazione
Address: viale della Repubblica 10, Castelnovo di Sotto, 42024, Italy
Description: In the centre of a town known for its carnival and many allegorical floats, this family-run restaurant housed in an old railway station dating from the early 20C serves traditional regional dishes. The house speciality is charcoal-grilled cuisine, with an equal emphasis on meat and fish options.

Name: Locanda del Feudo
Address: via Trasversale 2, Castelvetro di Modena, 41014, Italy
Description: Situated in the historic upper section of this picturesque village, this romantic restaurant serves imaginative and colourful cuisine including some excellent local cured hams. There are also six elegant, recently renovated suites with a historic feel, plus a small, charming outdoor space overlooking the main street.

Name: Rosso di Sera
Address: via Pietro Nenni 2, Castelletto sopra Ticino, 28053, Italy
Description: Situated closed to the motorway exit, this restaurant’s rustic exterior belies its young, colourful and contemporary interior. The dishes are as modern as the decor, yet they have their roots in strong local traditions. The owner has a real passion for French and sparkling wines, including around 160 different champagnes and a wide selection of Burgundies, plus a good choice of wines by the glass, craft beers and cocktails.

Name: Casa D'Angelo
Address: via San Nicola 5, Fara Filiorum Petri, 66010, Italy
Description: Housed in what was once an old family home, this elegant restaurant serves delicious regional cuisine. The menu includes dishes that have been skilfully and imaginatively enhanced by the chef, such as warm pheasant terrine with apple, alongside more traditional fare, from cured ham to charcoal-grilled local lamb and potato gnocchi with a duck sauce. The restaurant’s own extra-virgin olive oil often adds further flavour to the dishes. The 4-course tasting menu deserves a special mention for its excellent value for money.

Name: Antico Ristorante Pagnanelli
Description: In business since 1882, yet constantly evolving, this restaurant boasts a superb location overlooking the lake. The menu features meat and fish dishes, enhanced by wild herbs. The extensive wine selection is presented on two wine lists. Don’t miss the famous wine cellars built into the rock, plus the small wine museum.

Name: Pinocchio
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: This reliable restaurant is well run by a highly professional and experienced family. In the classic-style dining room, where the decor eschews fashionable trends, enjoy typical Piedmontese cuisine lightened by the occasional modern twist. The restaurant’s contemporary version of “paniscia” is a good example – a type of risotto with beans, salami and vegetables, in which the latter, instead of being cooked directly with the rice, are added on top of the mixture in the form of vegetable cream, giving the dish an attractive splash of colour. The menu always features a few fish-based recipes, including both salt- and freshwater species.

Name: Magiargè Osteria Contemporanea
Description: The dining rooms in this lively, typical restaurant in the upper, historic part of Bordighera appear to be almost dug out of the rock. Here, a local chef with plenty of experience behind him creates predominantly Ligurian cuisine (and not just fish and seafood), accompanied by an excellent and extensive wine list, including labels from outside Italy and a good selection of champagnes. Attentive service completes the picture here.

Name: Nido del Picchio
Description: This restaurant with a low-key, hushed atmosphere has the feel of a tastefully furnished private home, with an open fire to warm cold winter evenings while a cool, welcoming outdoor terrace becomes the ideal spot for al fresco dining in summer. The dining experience comprises four tasting menus and an extensive à la carte menu, offering a generous variety of dishes designed to delight every palate. The recipes range naturally between meat, fish and vegetarian options, offering a wide and well-balanced array of flavours. The desserts are all well worth trying and our favourites include Tribute to Gauguin, a tonka bean blancmange with passion fruit, mango, papaya and coconut ice cream for an exotic and refined finish. The chef’s wife, Lucy, manages the dining room service with the utmost courtesy and professionalism, contributing to a thoroughly enjoyable overall experience. The wine cellar is home to more than 700 of finest best Italian and international wines, with a curious and interesting emphasis on labels from cooler climes.

Name: Da Giovanni
Description: The 18C ceramic stove, bright, colourful decor (both period and vintage), long family tradition and excellent cuisine all contribute to making this restaurant which opened half a century ago a bastion of fine cuisine in Piacenza and the wider Po valley. Meat and fish both feature on the menu, although it is the latter that has become renowned as the restaurant’s speciality over the years. The excellent service is friendly and informal yet highly professional, while the extensive wine list also includes a good selection of wines by the glass.

Name: Locanda La Posta
Description: A traditional setting reflecting the style of the early 20th century, with photos and documents telling the restaurant’s story, and numerous small dining rooms characterised by wooden ceilings. The restaurant’s traditional-style cuisine features excellent home-made pastas and a typical boiled meat trolley (“carrello dei bolliti”). One of the signature dishes here is rabbit stew served with a generous portion of Carmagnola peppers. In the summer, guests can sit in a welcoming outdoor area adorned with green plants and flowers.

Name: Scola
Description: Situated in the heart of the Ligurian hinterland, this country house is a real gem, surrounded by a garden that resembles a painting with an English-style lawn that ripples in the wind. Since 1926, the Scola family, now in the fourth generation, have offered guests a friendly welcome here, serving cuisine that is a delight for the tastebuds. In the warm dining room, where the wooden floor and exposed beams hint at the building’s history, guests enjoy traditional local dishes that have been reinterpreted with a modern twist, such as spaghetti with grapefruit, purple prawns and sea lettuce – an explosion of flavours!

Name: Osteria Casale Ferrovia
Address: via Stazione 1, sulla SP 34, Carovigno, 72012, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Housed in what was once the 20C dwelling of an old olive mill, this restaurant boasts elegant decor, including a few antique family pieces and Art Deco - style tables. The female owner - chef prepares light and modern dishes with a regional flavour, while the wine list also showcases the best of Puglia. As the name suggests, the railway lies to the rear of the property, although there’s very little noise and no disturbance at all.

Name: Da Tonino
Description: The chef at this restaurant serves decidedly contemporary cuisine with its roots in traditional recipes. His creative flair results in modern dishes in which each detail is carefully curated, with bold combinations that come as a surprise yet always feature a perfect balance of flavours and textures. The restaurant, situated in a quiet residential area away from the hustle and bustle of the small town, is the perfect choice for anyone looking for a relaxed and yet elegant dining experience.

Name: Baita Fraina
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Warm little bar rooms, reminiscent of the German wine bars or "Stube", a summer terrace and well - prepared regional dishes: for those who want to escape the mundane life of Cortina for the silence of an isolated "baita" [mountain hut]. Delightful rooms, colourfully furnished in warm tones so you can better enjoy the silence and scents of the mountains.

Name: Sa Cardiga e Su Schironi
Address: strada statale 195 rotonda per Capoterra, Capoterra, 09012, Italy
Description: Since 1967, this small restaurant has been delighting locals and visitors alike with its vast array of fish and seafood dishes, including grilled and skewered specialities. The fresh fish display cabinet at the restaurant entrance is one of the highlights here, as is the extensive selection of antipasti which includes tuna and mullet bottarga, “agliata alla bosana” (blue shark with tomatoes in a light sweet and sour sauce) and “burrida alla cagliaritana” (with dogfish). Other dishes include fresh and pasta with various seafood sauces, fried fish, soup, lobster, barbecued fish and even eel.

Name: I 5 Campanili
Address: via Luigi Maino 18, Busto Arsizio, 21052, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Contemporary cooking with an emphasis on Mediterranean fare in tune with the seasons is to the fore in this elegant restaurant occupying a 20C building with an attractive veranda to the rear. Fish takes pride of place on the menu, with a particular focus on raw dishes and shellfish, but the splendid desserts are also worthy of note, such as cherries in sour cherry juice with fior di latte ice cream.

Name: Marsupino 1901
Description: "Once the village’s old bakery and now a leading restaurant, Trattoria Marsupino 1901 embodies the story of a family now in its fourth generation. The cuisine remains true to its roots, solid and authentic, yet capable of reinterpreting traditional flavours with a modern and balanced touch. A prime example is the Cisrà, a traditional soup made with chickpeas and pork ribs, made surprisingly light here while retaining all its delicious intensity.In the dining room, Luca’s professionalism and affability, combined with his unbounded passion for wine, complete the experience with a warm and expert service. His careful selection of wines and his ability to tell their stories make the dining experience a harmonious journey through tradition, terroir and good company. "

Name: Vitis
Description: A modern wine bar in the centre of Bressanone (Brixen), situated in the same building as the delightful family restaurant, the Oste Scuro. Here, small armchairs, benches and stools are dotted among low square tables and higher tables, all surrounded by walls completely lined with wine bottles. The contemporary cuisine features tempting, generous dishes such as cod with cauliflower, cavolo nero and beurre blanc. Accommodation is available just two minutes’ away in the historic Adler Historic Guesthouse run by the same owners.

Name: Elephant
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated in the famous Elephant hotel (with which it shares a name), this restaurant has a unique, historic ambience thanks to its decor of antique wood panelling. The cuisine here is regional with a modern twist, featuring dishes such as char with cream of celery and green apple on a bed of fragrant elderflowers. The interesting wine list boasts a selection of almost a thousand different labels.

Name: Castello Malvezzi
Address: via Colle San Giuseppe 1, Brescia, 25133, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: This elegant 16C hunting lodge offers a modern menu of fish dishes and regional specialities. Particular attention is paid to the excellent Calvisano caviar, with a dedicated menu. There’s an attractive outdoor dining area facing the gardens with views of the city of Brescia.

Name: Momento al 25
Description: A late 19th century building houses this simple but welcoming restaurant, which moves its service outdoors during the summer months, creating a pleasant and informal atmosphere. The menu alternates traditional local specialities with Italian dishes, featuring a significant selection of seafood options. Raw seafood is always on the menu: served plain or in slightly more elaborate versions, it is one of the most popular choices among diners.

Name: Quintilio
Description: For over a century, and from generation to generation, Quintilio has acted as a junction between two beautiful regions, Liguria and Piedmont – and not just in a geographical sense. These regions take pride of place on the wine list (overseen by the friendly and talented owner Lorena) as well as on the menu, which is created daily with dishes made from top-quality local ingredients full of flavour. Regional specialities such as plin, Ligurian-style rabbit tortelli, risottos, vitello tonnato (veal) and brain are all skilfully reinterpreted with a modern twist by owner-chef Luca Bazzano.

Name: Dispensa Franciacorta
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: The decor at this restaurant features bottles of wine on the walls (there are around 700 labels on the wine list, with an unsurprising focus on sparkling wines from the local Franciacorta region), while the menu celebrates some of Italy’s best produce, as well as ingredients from further afield, with a focus on Mediterranean flavours. There’s also an excellent cheese selection which will delight cheese-lovers.

Name: Ca' Apollonio Gourmet
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list
Description: At the foot of Monte Grappa, surrounded by vineyards, meadows, and trees, once you pass through the main gate and travel down a 1000-foot-long avenue, you’ll reach the restaurant's entrance, where you’ll be welcomed by a luxurious and elegant setting. There are two menus to choose from at the time of booking: Familiarity, which is tied to "home” flavors, and Conjunction, which focuses more on the sea and the garden. The wine cellar boasts around 800 labels from both small and large producers, offering a selection that will satisfy even the most discerning palates.

Name: Nello
Address: Via IV Novembre 66, San Casciano in Val di Pesa, 50026, Italy
Location: San Casciano in Val di Pesa, Italy
Description: Michele, in the kitchen, and pastry chef Chiara, who also works front of house, have renovated this historic local address and transformed it into a restaurant offering a modern interpretation of Tuscan cuisine. On the varied and intriguing menu, you’ll find dishes such as the intense and highly technical ravioli filled with Parmesan cream (made with milk from Reggiana “red cows”) with chanterelle mushrooms and onions. The concise wine list focuses on nearby Chiantis but also includes bottles from elsewhere.

Name: KELINA Fine Dine
Address: Strada Lech de Boà s.n., Corvara in Badia, 39033, Italy
Description: From Corvara, take the ultra-modern Boè cable-car to this restaurant (accessible also on skis or by foot) which is one of the dining options at the Piz Boè Alpine Lounge (for access, turn right as you exit the cable-car). Open at lunchtime only, KELINA serves unexpectedly gourmet cuisine in a minimalist-style dining room which allows the stunning views of Sassongher, Santa Croce and La Varella to take centre stage through the restaurant’s large floor-to-ceiling windows. The dishes are always modern and creative, combining Alpine ingredients with fresh fish – the red prawns with scampi mayonnaise and sea asparagus sorbet is a good example.

Name: Pentole e Provette
Description: This restaurant run by Luca Trabalzini and his partner Rossella is a real gem in the small town of Fasano. Their story, which began in Australia, adds a fascinating touch to this gastronomic project. The atmosphere here is warm and lively, making this the perfect setting in which to enjoy the chef’s innovative cuisine, which he showcases on three distinct menus dedicated to meat, fish and vegetarian dishes respectively. Luca’s cuisine is modern and elegant, with particular attention paid to flavours and textures so that each and every dish offers a fully rounded sensorial experience.

Name: Locanda Sensi
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: A country retreat that captivates with simplicity and passion: modern, comfortable rooms open onto a landscape that embraces the hills of Piacenza, while outdoor dining offers glimpses of light and nature. The menu invites guests to enjoy a tasting menu in which the grill releases intense aromas, or à la carte dishes that range from land to sea to garden, in a balance of scents and colours. Two cosy suites, a holistic wellness centre scented with natural essences and, in fine weather, a Zen garden that invites you to relax, complete the experience.

Name: John Restaurant
Address: via Santa Maria la Nova 35, Afragola, 80021, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Follow the signs to Casamadre (the specialist food shop next door) to get to this gourmet restaurant, where the chef surprises guests with modern cuisine that is full of character. The dishes served here are inspired by classic recipes which are reinterpreted with creativity and a respect for local traditions, resulting in highly distinctive flavours. Each course reflects the region’s history, with a common thread running through the menu to create an authentic culinary experience. To accompany your meal, the wines are presented on a practical tablet which makes it easy to explore the different labels available. Among the different options, the excellent Costa D'Amalfi “Selva delle Monache” 2022 is particularly striking thanks to its fresh, earthy aromas.

Name: Cocun Cellar Restaurant
Address: strada Prè de Vì 31, San Cassiano, 39036, Italy
Description: In the cellars of the Ciasa Salares hotel, you dine surrounded by bottles – after all, cocun in Ladin means cork, and not by chance the wine selection is vast. The chef creates dishes with ingredients and influences from every corner of the world and makes frequent use of the grill.

Name: La Foresteria
Address: Contrada Passo di Gurra SP 79 km 91, Menfi, 92013, Italy
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: Planeta’s elegance is reflected in this property nestled among Sicilian vineyards. The rooms bear the signature of the celebrated winery, while the restaurant offers modern Sicilian cuisine, with an à la carte menu and two tasting journeys. Don’t miss “Casa Planeta”: five iconic courses inspired by the monsù, the chefs of ancient noble residences.

Name: Enoteca Meucci
Description: Occupying a restored farmhouse at the foot of Cortona, this restaurant has two different dining areas: a wine bar on the ground floor where you can enjoy excellent aperitifs or buy local food produce, and a restaurant on the first floor that serves a traditional yet constantly evolving menu with a focus on seasonal ingredients. There’s also a beautiful outdoor space for summer dining.

Name: Palazzo Utini
Description: A historical palazzo in the town center has been given new life after its purchase by a local entrepreneurial family, who also bestowed their name upon it. Just steps from the heart of Italy’s famed food valley, it now serves as a refined hospitality venue: the ground floor hosts a bar and bistro, while upstairs are elegant spaces devoted to gourmet cuisine. Tasting menus are available, with dishes also offered à la carte, alongside an extensive and well-curated wine list. The upper floors feature intimate, stylish guest rooms, designed in the same reassuring style that runs throughout the building.

Name: I 5 Sensi
Description: The whole family (mother and four children) is involved in the running of this restaurant, both front of house and in the kitchen. If you’re looking for top-quality cuisine which focuses on local ingredients yet is also creative and innovative, then this is the place for you! Classic-style dining room with the occasional modern decorative touch, plus a well-stocked wine cellar offering a selection of 700 labels.

Name: Alla Locanda
Description: A colourful, contemporary-style restaurant within the Locanda degli Artisti Art Hotel, a new address that is striking for its large art collection displayed throughout its various rooms. The cuisine here focuses on seasonal ingredients which are used to create personalised and imaginative dishes showcased on tasting menus and an à la carte. Interesting wine list, with a special emphasis on labels from the Trentino-Alto Adige region.

Name: Musciora
Description: The father-and-son team (chef and sommelier respectively) at this excellent restaurant not far from the town centre put heart and soul into their work, offering guests three tasting menus: one dedicated to fish and seafood; another to inland ingredients and meat; and the third, entitled “Completo”, simply offering a combination of the first two menus. The recipes are inspired by the island’s produce, which is prepared simply and with real skill. Our inspector particularly enjoyed the raw mullet tartare encased in a “fake” tomato made from tomato and raspberry jelly. The wine list also showcases Sardinia, with a focus on small-scale wineries, although bottles from elsewhere (including natural wines) also feature.

Name: Innocenti Wine Experiences
Description: Innocenti Wine is a venue with a dual identity: on one side, a wine shop boasting an ever-evolving collection of over 1,200 labels spanning Italy’s finest wine regions and international terroirs; on the other, a restaurant where contemporary cuisine finds expression through three tasting menus or à la carte selections. A place designed for those who approach wine as exploration and the table as its natural complement.

Name: Rear Restaurant
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: This restaurant with a modern, elegant dining room in which an entire wall is taken up by the open-view kitchen serves varied cuisine. In addition to more elaborate dishes, there’s also a choice of street food such as fried dishes, sandwiches, Spanish prosciutto and tacos, as well as raw fish and seafood, classic and more creative options, and various fish and meat main courses, many of which are cooked over the barbecue grill. The superb wine list includes a fine array of champagnes.

Name: Villa Pignano
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace
Description: A veritable Garden of Eden just 15km from Volterra, this 300ha estate is home to a restaurant with a strong zero-mile philosophy.
