In this setting, which is particularly romantic at night when candlelit, the cuisine showcases regional ingredients and combines Brazilian, French and Italian influences through dishes such as the gnocchi au gratin with beef ragout or the delicious suckling pig cooked at low temperature and accompanied by manioc purée, caramelised onion and basil pesto.

Name: Polux
Address: No. 5, Xintiandi Beili, Lane 181, Taicang Road, Huangpu, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Don’t be perplexed if certain items on the menu seem familiar – this all-day dining bistro is from Paul Pairet of Ultraviolet and Mr & Mrs Bund fame. Some dishes echo the signatures from those establishments, albeit rendered in simpler forms. Onion soup is recommended – rich and well-balanced, with a nice contrast of textures. The classic Parisian décor with an industrial twist works perfectly. Reservations are recommended.

Name: Tempura Mizuki
Address: B1F, The Ritz-Carlton, Kyoto, 543 Hokodencho, Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto, 604-0902, Japan
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list,Wheelchair access
Description: Ingredients are presented before frying begins: vegetables from Takagamine and Shūgakuin, seafood from Awaji and Shizuoka. Safflower oil is used to achieve a light tempura coating. While some items, such as shrimp and conger eel, are fried on their own, other items are ingeniously paired, such as sesame tofu with sea urchin, wagyu with perilla leaf, and egg yolk with caviar. Gracious hospitality accompanies.

Name: TEMPURA HANAGATAMI
Address: 5F, The Ritz-Carlton, Osaka, 2-5-25 Umeda, Kita-ku, Osaka, 530-0001, Japan
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Boxed sea urchins, foods from both land and sea, and tempura to start. The tempura, fried in blended oil, is light and airy. The secret is in the batter: to reduce gluten, wheat flour is chilled to below freezing before frying. One inventive dish is tempura of sea urchin and nori seaweed. Combinations are delightful, including eel grilled without seasoning then brushed with wasabi, and Orient clams dressed with pepper flowers.

Name: TOKI
Address: 1F, Hotel The Mitsui Kyoto, 284 Nijo Aburanokojicho, Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto, 604-0051, Japan
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Water from Kyoto’s Fushimi district supports TOKI’s contemporary cuisine. In accordance with its concept, that ‘fond’ equals ‘dashi’, the restaurant harmonises elements from France and Japan. A wide range of dashi are used: vegetable, fish, meat. The chef brings out the flavour of each ingredient, such as pairing duck meat with duck dashi. Western and Japanese ingredients are combined into new harmonies, including salmon with sweet, white Kyoto-style Saikyo miso and foie gras with sake lees.

Name: La Kanro
Description: Presentation sparkles with colour here as the chef skilfully weaves in flowers and herbs and decorates his plates with sauces of vegetable and spices. Combo platters of appetisers, garnished with flowers, beguile the senses. Trying out a departure from normal French practice, meat dishes are stripped of garnishes. Prix fixe menus change monthly and the chef repeatedly updates his menus.

Name: Utsubohommachi Gaku
Description: The chef cultivated his free-ranging creativity at a Naniwa kappo, his genial customer service at a bistro. His creative flair is on full display in his combinations of ingredients. Pairings such as sweetfish with peach, barracuda with persimmon, and Spanish mackerel with apple marry fish of the season with the sweetness, tartness and flavour of fruit. Great care is taken to offer a wide selection of organic wines that have affinity for each offering. The chef’s philosophy is that originality must be firmly grounded in the basics, and his cuisine attests to it.

Name: LES SOUVENIRS
Description: The chef layers Japanese elements over French cuisine. Insisting on only the finest Japan-grown ingredients in season, he uses kombu dashi and soy sauce as subtle accents of flavour. The chef values knowing the people behind his produce, and deepens his ties with them, while crockery and cutlery are specially commissioned from artisans. The restaurant name is French for ‘memories’, reflecting the chef’s desire to make it a place that recalls the happiness of chatting contently around a dining table.

Name: MOTOÏ
Description: Housed in a remodeled Taisho-era residence, with a dining room looking out on a courtyard, MOTOÏ is suffused with the atmosphere of Old Japan. Kyoto native Motoi Maeda boasts careers in both Chinese and French cuisine, so he assembles prix fixe menus applying Kyoto customs to Chinese and French techniques. Sichuan-style tofu mousse is a chilled hors d’oeuvre. During seasonal festivals, Motoi cheerfully mixes elements of traditional events into his dishes.

Name: PONTE VECCHIO
Description: Daisuke Yamane’s culinary goal is ‘optimal cuisine’. Cooking and composition are kept to a minimum, so that beauty flows from function. Each ingredient serves a purpose. The chef rejects needless decoration, knowing too much can be as bad as too little. Disparate elements judiciously balanced create harmony—for example, the luxury of caviar meeting the familiarity of potato.

Name: LOUISE
Description: The Cameroon-born chef co-stars with the hospitable sommelier. What prompted a chef who pursued gastronomy in Paris to set up shop in Osaka. Simply put, a fascination with the foodstuffs of Japan. Layered ingredients, effectively seasoned with herbs and spices, bring out a distinctive character. The counter, where you can chat with staff and enjoy the delicious aromas wafting from the kitchen, offers the best seats in the house.

Name: Hwaro
Description: Chef Sungchul Shim is no stranger to the New York restaurant scene, but this 22-seat counter located inside Gui Steakhouse is certainly one to watch. Begin with a cocktail before riding the elevator to discover this hidden-in-plain-sight spot. The space is stunning, though all eyes are drawn to the center where the chefs prepare a multicourse menu fusing Korean flavors with French techniques. The meal begins with a bowl of luscious brown butter miso soup before wild amberjack crudo with a tableside pour of buttermilk koji sauce. The shrimp toast sandwich reveals perfectly cooked abalone nestled at its center, though the white soy custard with Osetra caviar for spooning over top is reason alone for its guest favorite status. A sesame ice cream popsicle is a delightful finale.

Name: Le Chêne
Address: 76 Carmine St., New York, NY, 10014, USA
Description: With tables huddled close, the ceiling low and the room packed, everyone looks when a pithivier emerges from the kitchen. Ornately carved, perfectly bronzed and flaky, and layered with precision, this French throwback tells you everything you need to know about Alexia and Ronan Duchêne’s West Village bijou. Rich, robust cooking celebrates French excellence of yesteryear with foie gras terrines and vin jaune sauces prepared with substance and astuteness. A wonderful, curved bar in the front room welcomes walk-ins. Wine lovers will not want for choice, as the serious list is deep and carefully curated.

Name: Cove
Address: 285 W. Houston St., New York, NY, 10014, USA
Description: Chef Flynn McGarry’s ambitious restaurant in Hudson Square means business. Dressed top to bottom in sleek wood and boasting ceilings that reach for the sky, the spacious dining room is equal parts warm and luxurious. The open kitchen is constantly in motion to prepare an à la carte menu as well as a formal tasting that’s rooted in Californian sensibilities and tied to the seasons, especially as many ingredients are sourced from the team’s farm in Long Island. From grilled black cod with mushroom and leek rice to cheese pumpkin schnitzel to huckleberry with cherry blossom semifreddo, the cooking boasts a kind of playful elegance that doesn’t feel forced and never wants for flavor.

Name: La Villa
Address: Seven Villas, 1 Bapanling Road, Xihu, Hangzhou, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Valet parking
Description: In a resort, this monastery-turned restaurant imparts a serene vibe. The all-wood architecture is elegantly enhanced by floral arrangements, white table linen and leather chairs. Only open to the public for dinner, it serves a seasonal-centric menu, finessed with European techniques and artistic plating. Some dishes pay tribute to the chef’s mother’s cooking. Bookings advisable due to limited seating. Ask about wine or tea pairings.

Name: 1515 West Chophouse
Address: 4F, Jing An Shangri-La Hotel, 1218 Middle Yan'an Road, Jingan, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Premium cuts of beef displayed in a dry-aging room greet diners entering this classy steakhouse depicted by dark wood, leather and black and white floor tiles. The open kitchen puts on quite a show with steaks sizzling on the flaming grill, served with a sauce of your choice, like a tangy, spicy chimichurri that cuts through the rich meat flavours. Be it warm chocolate fondant or New York cheesecake, round off your meal with a comforting dessert.

Name: Arva
Address: 6161 Yuanjiang Road, Minhang, Shanghai, 201111, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: The lush green, airy room is home to a farm-to-table dining concept. The passionate chef adds a modern spin to country recipes from all over Italy. Veg grown on Arva’s own farm is paired with Italian produce and seafood from all over China in a culinary lineup packed in familiar flavours. Fresh handmade pasta stands out with silky texture and wheat aromas. Arva is nestled in a remote luxury resort, so driving there is the best option.

Name: Plum Garden
Address: 2F, Jinling Hotel, 2 Hanzhong Road, Gulou, Nanjing, Chinese Mainland
Description: This plum blossom-themed restaurant has been serving Huaiyang classics to food-loving locals and tourists since 1983. If the extensive menu proves hard to navigate, seek advice from the knowledgeable servers. The signature brine-poached duck is infused with the aromas of the aged marinade before being deboned and sliced. It is a rare treat to see painstakingly made fried eel meat braised with pork belly and pigeon eggs on a menu.

Name: Jean Georges
Address: 4F, Bund 3, 3 Zhongshan Dong Yi Road, Huangpu, Shanghai, 200002, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Those who are familiar with Jean-Georges Vongerichten’s New York flagship will recognise some of the menu items here at his Shanghai outpost, such as toasted egg yolk with caviar, and tuna tartare with avocado. The cooking is light and fragrant, with plenty of choice on offer. The comfortable room comes with views and an open kitchen; it’s warmly lit and run by a well-organised and professional team.

Name: The Pine
Address: 333 Changle Road, Xuhui, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
Description: Both the manicured garden and exquisite interior of this historical British-style mansion are charming, with many classical and modern art pieces on display. The young chef shows solid skills in traditional European cooking and gives it a new spin, with Chinese seasoning and produce. Chargrilled Australian Wagyu steak is served with braised beef sinew and a mildly spicy sauce; the soupy rice with pickles features coral grouper fillet.

Name: Hakkasan
Address: 5F, Bund 18, 18 Zhongshan Dong Yi Road, Huangpu, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Though its name means Hakka people, this chic restaurant on the Bund serves modern Cantonese fare with Western influences, but not necessarily of Hakka origin. The must-try Peking duck with caviar boasts interesting layers of flavours. Top-notch ingredients are used for the hand-made dim sum; and the dim sum set lunch offers great value. Don’t miss the cocktails featuring local liquors. The sweeping river views are a beauty to behold.

Name: 28 Hubin Road
Address: 1F, Grand Hyatt, 28 Hubin Road, Shangcheng, Hangzhou, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Perched on the shore of West Lake, this hotel restaurant is led by a local chef with over 20 years of experience under his belt. Traditional Zhejiang recipes are given a new spin – to great effect. The braised pork belly pagoda comes in two portion sizes: a mini version served with pumpkin flatbread and a regular one with chestnut pancakes. The wine list features a plethora of Chinese labels from Hebei, Shandong, Shanxi, Xinjiang and Ningxia.

Name: Summer Palace
Address: 3F, Jing An Shangri-La Hotel, 1218 Middle Yan'an Road, Jingan, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: This smart and sumptuously decorated Cantonese restaurant is divided into three distinct areas, each serving a different speciality. You'll find dim sum in the Pantry, claypot dishes in the Lantern and sophisticated Cantonese classics in the Imperial. Every facet of Cantonese cuisine is covered, alongside seasonal offerings such as hairy crabmeat and roe for a Shanghai twist. The private rooms are impressive, as is the wine list.

Name: Shanghai Tavern
Address: 1F, Shanghai Edition, 199 East Nanjing Road, Huangpu, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: It may be located within the contemporary backdrop of the Edition Hotel but the Shanghai Tavern celebrates a bygone era; the elegant space shows off its original 20C heritage and there are potted palms, waiters in white jackets, a champagne chariot and even a dessert trolley. The menu covers all bases from clam Rockefeller to steak from the Josper grill and even mac & cheese. Keen service and a soundtrack all add to its appeal.

Name: Jade on 36
Address: 36F, Grand Tower, Pudong Shangri-La Hotel, 33 Fucheng Road, Pudong, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Even when this longstanding restaurant gets a makeover, nothing is really able to compete with the stunning views of Huangpu and the river. With an experienced French chef at the helm, the menu ushers in some sophisticated creations; they may be deeply rooted in traditions, but they are also jazzed up by modern touches and subtle Mediterranean influences. Blue lobster can be ordered in three servings and the lunch menu offers good value.

Name: YongFoo Élite
Address: 200 Yongfu Road, Xuhui, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
Description: This circa 1930 building was the British Consulate before it underwent a three-year makeover. Its historic character is paired with a menu of traditional Shanghainese dishes, including many heritage recipes rarely found elsewhere. When in season, their decadent dishes of hairy crab roe and crabmeat picked fresh on the day are simply unmissable. No meal is complete without a starch course drenched in Shanghai flair, like xiao long bao or noodles tossed in scallion oil.

Name: Mercato
Address: 6F, Bund 3, 3 Zhongshan Dong Yi Road, Huangpu, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Counter dining,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: From Jean-Georges Vongerichten comes this lively Italian restaurant with Mediterranean twists. The interior sees an eclectic mix of natural materials matched with urban savviness. On the menu, family-style dishes come in sizes large enough for sharing. Signatures such as wood oven-roasted whole Icelandic halibut, and porcini-crusted salmon with warm leek vinaigrette and herbs, are well worth trying. Also ask about the chef’s specials.

Name: Mr & Mrs Bund
Address: 6F, Bund 18, 18 Zhongshan Dong Yi Road, Huangpu, Shanghai, 200002, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Counter dining,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: On the city's dining scene for over 10 years, Chef Pairet's brasserie never ages. Lovely service, great music, a terrific wine list and bags of French joie de vivre make dining here a real experience. In the modern space – inside a former bank dating from 1922 – diners feast on chilled seafood platters, and charbroiled steaks and seafood. Original creations such as lemon & lemon tart with a sorbet-filled whole lemon also stand out.

Name: Nuits
Address: 68 Tongren Road, Jingan, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
Description: The bistro may not have a big shopfront, but its bright orange sign and full-height windows more than compensate in terms of impact. Named after Côte de Nuits in Burgundy, it serves a nice selection from that wine region. Head to the cellar to talk to the sommelier. The menu is mostly classic French and the signature beef tartare is laced with lightly cooked geoduck and oyster liquor, reflecting the bounty of sea and land.

Name: Blaise
Description: Housed in the historic Maternidade Filomena Matarazzo building, this restaurant draws its inspiration from Blaise Cendrars — the Franco-Swiss writer and passionate lover of Brazil. The interior evokes a cabin, reminiscent of the one in which the writer is said to have lived, decorated with volcanic crystal stones set amongst wood, in a rustic atmosphere warmed by the soft glow of lampshades that light the space. Here, chef Fernando Bouzan presents a French-inspired cuisine crafted exclusively from Brazilian ingredients, with a firm emphasis on seasonal produce and regional provenance. Our suggestion? The flavourful Fish Crudo served with cashew nuts, lírio do brejo — a native Brazilian marsh flower — and shiokara, a cured seafood condiment; the succulent Black Angus short rib with arracacha, mustard leaf and a natural jus; and, to finish, a Jabuticaba sorbet — made from the distinctive Brazilian berry that grows straight from the tree trunk — accompanied by a compote, jelly and pearls of the same fruit nestled in a delicate crème diplomate. The full offering is available either as a tasting menu called Experiência, or à la carte!

Name: Fasano
Address: Rua Vitório Fasano 88, São Paulo, 01414-020, Brazil
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: The grand piano at the entrance and the lounge with its high ceiling and skylight take us right back to the New York of the 1940s. A symbol of quality, glamour and fine dining, the Fasano is renowned across Brazil for its classic Italian cuisine, albeit with a modern touch. The menu, designed by chef Luca Gozzani, showcases an extensive array of copious and surprising dishes on which pasta plays a starring role. We can recommend starting with the creamy burrata with Parma ham, followed by a succulent lamb fillet with gnocchi, with a delicious panna cotta as a final flourish – a combination of dishes that will take you on a gastronomic adventure back to Italy. The exceptional service from the front-of-house team makes the experience even more special!

Name: Azurite
Description: When a passionate craftsman and veteran of the Dutch culinary scene like Mario Ridder shares his most personal vision, every foodie knows it's time to pay attention. In the heart of Delft, Azurite immerses diners in a tasteful setting featuring glass, concrete and wood. Raw, natural and yet warm, with select provisions showcased as visual highlights (the upper floor is home to a wine loft holding hundreds of bottles and a vintage whisky and digestif bar). The service is equally elegant – unobtrusive yet attentive – with a sommelier who curates outstanding pairings. The stage is set; now the chef lets his expertise in classic cuisine and passion for Dutch produce do the talking.There is a choice of three set menus (vegetarian, signature and caviar) that encapsulate his distinguished career with finesse. Picture a room-temperature bluefin tuna fillet served with a simple artichoke cream, then topped with a fine sliver of aged beef, drizzled with a full-bodied olive oil and finished with a subtly vibrant kohlrabi vinaigrette. Simply exquisite! Mario Ridder bares himself bare in the kitchen, and that promises the diner an extraordinary experience.

Name: De Lepelaar
Description: Located at the edge of the picturesque village centre of Jisp, this authentic establishment has kept its spirit wonderfully intact. The restaurant is brimming with charm and character; in fine weather, the pontoon terrace right on the water is the place to be. The menu features timeless classics and Asian-inspired creations. Examples include tuna tataki brushed with yakitori sauce, nuts and seeds, and beef tournedos topped with duck liver and finished with a delicate Madeira sauce – choosing between the à la carte and the set menu is no easy task. The proprietor is on hand to provide expert wine recommendations. And if you would like to truly make a night of it, stay over in one of the guestrooms upstairs.

Name: Roux
Description: Every great sauce starts with a roux, and here it's very much a speciality. The trend-conscious cooking features playful combinations, engaging textures and international influences, and is underpinned by those robust sauces. Think medium-rare fillet of hare in a sweet-spice crust, elevated by a refined game jus and complemented by slices of raw foie gras and crunchy quince. The sommelier's expert wine pairings are delivered with infectious enthusiasm. One final tip: The long dining counter is our favourite spot in this elegant luxury brasserie.

Name: Enoteca Cavour 313
Description: Reborn in late 2024 thanks to the enthusiasm of a young Frenchwoman raised among vineyard rows and harvests, this historic establishment has found new life without losing its soul as a rustic, welcoming wine shop. Just beyond the entrance, guests pause for an easy lunch, guided by a nimble offering written on the blackboard and enriched by a daily special – irresistible, during our visit, the saffron risoni with mantis shrimp. In the evening (reservations recommended), one moves into the more intimate spaces that open beyond the visible kitchen, where the young Roman chef, trained at the ALMA school, presents seasonal dishes interpreted with technique and creativity. The wine list is excellent, harmoniously divided between Italian labels and those from across the Alps.

Name: Vinheria Percussi
Address: Rua Bianchi Bertoldi 109, São Paulo, 05422-070, Brazil
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: A family-run Italian restaurant that was a trailblazer for enogastronomy (the pairing of food and wine) in Brazil. It first opened as a wine shop in 1985 with the aim of offering good wine and food to go with it. However, with the arrival of chef Silvia Percussi and her brother Lamberto Percussi (restaurant manager and sommelier) the concept was taken to the next level. In this classic, welcoming setting, enjoy excellent cuisine including fresh pasta, which is a particular highlight. Classic dishes that you’ll want to try include the perfectly creamy tonnarelli alla carbonara, the beef ossobuco, served with a divine sauce, and the tiramisu, which is brought to your table on a platter. Why not pair these delicacies with one of the fines wines on the 300-strong wine list and in the award-winning cellar.

Name: Animus
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Housed in a corner building in the charming neighbourhood of Pinheiros, this restaurant is under the direction of young chef Giovanna Grossi, who, after gaining experience at renowned restaurants in France and Spain, decided to stake everything on her own venture. With a creative concept that champions the use of seasonal ingredients and draws inspiration from her roots, the chef turns her attention to her home state of Alagoas, inviting diners on a journey through the flavours of every corner of the region. The concept, which offers both à la carte options and a set lunch menu during the week, also features a seven-course tasting menu called Carta a Alagoas — a letter to Alagoas — using local ingredients that travel from the coastline to the arid sertão, the rugged backcountry of the state. Our recommendation? The delicate wagyu tartare served with fish roe and a crispy rice tuile, and the lamb picanha — a prized rump cut — accompanied by sheep's milk curd and the sweet, fragrant lift of roasted tangerine.

Name: Osso
Address: Rua Bandeira Paulista, 520, São Paulo, 04532-001, Brazil
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: An address that is much prized by meat-lovers in the Greater São Paulo area. Here, chef Renzo Garibaldi, who is known as the “Master Butcher” in his native Peru, has opened his first culinary venture in Brazil. His cuisine is based around meat sourced from sustainable producers, with the option of dry aged and different maturing times covering an array of different cuts. Beef is the star of the show here, available as ribeye, fillet mignon, picanha and short ribs, alongside special dry-aged pure Wagyu, Vaca Velha and Angus. Dishes such as ceviche, salad, Josper-grilled fish and the rice-based “arroz de costela” also feature on the menu in a modern, elegant space with an extensive and superb wine list.

Name: Giuseppe Grill Leblon
Address: Avenida Bartolomeu Mitre 370, Rio de Janeiro, 22431-001, Brazil
Description: As the name clearly states, all the dishes (fish and meat) at this restaurant are cooked on the charcoal grill. The menu here includes dry-aged meat options (T-bone, prime rib, bone-in tenderloin etc). Other standout dishes include the Maminha (sirloin trip-tip), the Picanha supra sumo (supra sumo rump cap), the brand’s registered trademark, as well as the slow-cooked Uruguayan lamb shoulder served with its own jus. A number of fish and seafood options are also on display, which vary according to availability on the day. The decor here features exposed stone walls, wooden chairs and leather sofas, all of which combine to add a rustic-elegant touch. Not forgetting the extensive wine cellar in the basement, featuring over 400 labels from different countries.

Name: Aprazível
Address: Rua Aprazível 62, Rio de Janeiro, 20241-270, Brazil
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking
Description: Aprazível is a must for those who love a combination of delicious cuisine and nature. This family-run restaurant is located in hills surrounding the city in the Santa Teresa district, with its narrow winding streets and breathtaking views. Here, chef Ana Castilho conjures up a menu featuring organic and biodynamic ingredients from different regions around Brazil, yet without neglecting either creativity or a delicate touch. We can highly recommend the ceviche in a tucupi sauce with plantain chips, plus the delicious free-range “galinhada caipira” chicken, served with sausage from Minas Gerais, braised cabbage, a tasty pepper jelly and “surprise” beans. The restaurant also serves an array of superb drinks, including a vast selection of 100% natural wines as a perfect pairing for this culinary experience.

Name: Mr. Lam
Address: Rua Maria Angélica 21, Rio de Janeiro, 22470-201, Brazil
Description: Mr. Lam gained renowned for his culinary prowess in restaurants in New York and London, however he has run this modern, sophisticated eatery on the edge of the Rodrigo de Freitas lagoon for almost two decades. In contrast with other gastronomic restaurants these days, the majority of dishes on the menu have been mainstays since it first opened, in so doing keeping with the tradition of offering the best Chinese cuisine. Featuring a hint of cutting-edge innovation as well as a focus on top-quality ingredients, the menu boasts memorable dishes such as the lightly battered and crispy Senhor Batista prawns, with a delicious sweet and sour sauce. The restaurant is laid out on three floors, with an attractive dining room and welcoming bar on the top floor that includes picture windows and a glass roof that provide lots of natural light and stunning views.

Name: Cala del Tanit
Address: Rua Pais De Araújo, 147, São Paulo, 04531-090, Brazil
Description: A new culinary venture courtesy of chef Oscar Bosch, who has been on the rise in São Paulo's food scene since he opened the successful Tanit in the Jardins district of the city. Located in Itaim Bibi, in one of the typical three-storey houses in this neighbourhood, Cala del Tanit boasts a terrace at the entrance and a contemporary interior with a predominance of wood, an open-view kitchen and a counter where cocktails are also prepared for guests. The menu retains the essence of Mediterranean cuisine, with Spanish dishes such as tomato gazpacho and delicious rice dishes (Paella Marinera and duck rice socarrat, with grilled octopus and aioli), although the culinary repertoire here also includes an array of pasta dishes and a few Asian touches. A space for small private events, called Sala X, is also available.

Name: Le Jardin
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking
Description: A true oasis of sophistication and serenity, enveloped by nature amid the bustle of the city. Le Jardin offers a luxurious setting inspired by a winter garden, where both the décor and the atmosphere are impossible to overlook. The concept here is rooted in classic international cuisine, with carefully crafted dishes, in a space that is ideal for dining at any hour of the day. In the morning, breakfast is served with house specialities and healthy options to choose from. As the day unfolds, a varied all-day menu is available, while evenings bring an alternative selection alongside a Kosher menu, available on prior reservation. Our suggestion? Try the lamb ossobuco — a slow-braised lamb shank — served with linguine in a truffle fonduta with watercress and a herb farofa, a toasted cassava crumb, and the suckling pig shoulder with organic carrots and a sweet-and-sour pork roti sauce — dishes that are sure to exceed your expectations!

Name: Rubaiyat Rio
Address: Rua Jardim Botânico 971, Rio de Janeiro, 22470-051, Brazil
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Great view,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Located in the Jockey Club Brasileiro within the boundaries of the city’s botanical gardens, this restaurant boasts breathtaking views where you can enjoy a meal on the terrace to a picture-postcard backdrop that features the horse-racing track as well as the statue of Christ the Redeemer. Inside, there is a large dining room with a counter that is home to the kitchen, where a large glass window provides views of the churrasco grill. Renowned for its array of premium meats and for managing its own production, the restaurant offers more than ten different cuts, although fish, seafood, rice and a variety of sides also feature on the menu. On Saturdays, don't miss the chance to try the traditional feijoada stew, made with prime cuts of meat, along with milkshakes and an incredible dessert buffet!

Name: Fogo de Chão
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: The Fogo de Chão restaurant is much more than just a place to eat as it represents Brazil’s churrasco tradition that has won admirers in different parts of the world. The origins of this method for cooking meat date back to the gauchos who discovered that skewering pieces of meat on wooden sticks and placing them around a fire transformed a simple cut of meat into a true delicacy. Served as part of “rodizio” all-you-can-eat format, the extensive selection includes beef, lamb and pork, as well as the famous prime rib and dry-aged meats (only available on the à la carte). To accompany the superb choice of meat, there’s also an extensive buffet of salads, rice dishes, carpaccios, cheeses and sausages. In total, there are five Fogo de Chão restaurants in São Paulo, all of which boast a comprehensive wine list. The new addition at the Jardins restaurant is a space dedicated to cigar-lovers.

Name: A Figueira Rubaiyat
Address: Rua Haddock Lobo 1738, São Paulo, 01414-002, Brazil
Description: The backdrop here is definitely a surprise, thanks to the majestic Bengal fig, a century-old tree that attracts admiring glances inside this restaurant. The glass roof adds a special touch, enabling guests to admire the immensity of its crown and branches to the full. In keeping with its sister property in the famous Faria Lima, the cuisine is based around the "fork to plate” concept. Both the beef and pork are produced at the group’s own farms and distributed throughout its franchises. Dishes include baby beef, Denver steak, filet mignon, master beef and “tirita de picanha”, all of which are cooked to perfection, although the menu also features a variety of fish, rice and pasta options. Enhance your dining experience with the superb array of labels available on the wine list.

Name: Fasano Trattoria
Address: Rua Iguatemi s/n, São Paulo, 01451-010, Brazil
Description: Set against a remarkable backdrop, surrounded by some of São Paulo's most imposing landmarks — including the sleek, modern Pátio Malzoni and, to its left, the Casa Bandeirista, a significant example of 17th-century Brazilian colonial architecture — this elegant trattoria emerges as a striking contrast, paying homage to southern Italy through a décor that blends wood, leather, and the colours of traditional Italian football clubs. The menu celebrates classic recipes and dishes inspired by the region of Naples and the island of Capri, including the memorable linguine alle vongole con pomodorini — linguine with clams and cherry tomatoes — the timeless spaghetti alla carbonara, and, among the main courses, the succulent polpettone al forno, a slow-baked Italian-style meatloaf. The space also boasts a delightful outdoor terrace, perfect for those wishing to enjoy their meal in a tranquil setting surrounded by a beautiful garden.

Name: Dining Room
Address: 37F, Park Hyatt Hotel, 1366 Qianjiang Road, Shangcheng, Hangzhou, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking
Description: With exquisite glass lanterns, glass screens and panoramic windows that welcome in river views, the high-ceilinged room is perfect for banquets and business dinners. The menu is dominated by Zhejiang classics, alongside some Cantonese dishes. The poetically named "Sinking Fish, Falling Geese" consists of white fish balls stuffed with bird's nest. The classic braised pork belly is thinly sliced and styled into a pagoda. Reservations recommended.

Name: Cellar to Table
Address: 1A, 1F, 20 Donghu Road, Xuhui, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
Description: With an Italian chef on board since 2024, this place exuding an appealing wine-bar vibe has a revamped menu that is unmistakably Italian. The dishes are rife with bold flavours and the extensive wine list meanders across the globe. The handmade pasta is perfectly al dente; the grilled Shandong red prawn smoky and juicy; Sichuan tomatoes and Yunnan mushrooms work magic in Italian recipes. On a balmy night, ask for a courtyard table.

Name: Trine
Address: 4F, Building 1, Surpass Court, 570 Yongjia Road, Xuhui, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
Description: In astrology, "Trine" connotes balance and harmony, in line with the chef's culinary vision. Her dishes seduce with well-matched flavours, subtle creative twists and meticulous plating – think Boston lobster ravioli or cloud brioche. Boasting juicy, pinkish flesh, the jujube wood-smoked baby pigeon goes perfectly with the red wine Sichuan peppercorn sauce. The cosy space feels inviting with its warm hues, solid wood furniture and a suspended fireplace.

Name: Gongxi Gongxi (Pudong)
Address: 2F, InterContinental Pudong Hotel, 777 Zhangyang Road, Pudong, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: With a combination of Jiangzhe motifs and modern sensibility, the space exudes understated luxury. The owner was a food supplier and is picky about freshness and quality. All dishes – à la carte, set menus or seasonal creations – centre on the freshest catch from the East China Sea and sustainable produce from Ningbo. Standouts are the deeply flavoursome swimmer crab with rice cake and the silky, umami-laden braised pomfret with fish roe.

Name: Scilla
Address: 504 Panyu Road, Changning, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Garden or park,Interesting wine list,Notable sake list
Description: Scilla is a blue-flowered Mediterranean plant, a nod to the fact that this restaurant focuses on the region's food, but with a twist – local ingredients, especially seafood, are given the Mediterranean treatment, for a unique fusion cuisine. The signature BBQ Taizhou yellow croaker with bouillabaisse and rouille boasts crispy skin, rich broth and creamy sauce. The underground wine cellar carries some rare finds from the Mediterranean coast.

Name: Sir Elly's
Address: 13F, The Peninsula Hotel, Bund 32, 32 Zhongshan Dong Yi Road, Huangpu, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Great view,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Named after the hotel's founder, the opulent interior dotted with red accents commands gorgeous river views and the upmarket vibe makes it perfect for a romantic date. The Italian chef’s globetrotting experience influences the menu, as Italian traditions undergo novel twists. His signature Chongming squab, cooked three ways, illustrates his love of local produce; for primo piatto, try his meticulously handmade ravioli or spaghetti.

Name: Cuivre
Address: 1502 Middle Huaihai Road, Xuhui, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Echoing its name, this welcoming bistro in the French Concession amusingly features copper trimming on its walls and dinnerware. The kitchen excels in reinventing bistro classics with modern techniques and subtle Asian twists. L’agneau, or rack of lamb, is nicely charred on the outside and juicy on the inside. Round off with baba Zacapa. The wine list offers vintages from French small-batch wineries, alongside affordable new world choices.

Name: Torikaze
Address: Unit 102, Block 3, M+ Blackstone Apartments, 1331 Middle Fuxing Road, Xuhui, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
Description: Japanese minimalism meets moody modernism in this joint venture between a local F&B group and yakitori master, Yoshiteru Ikegawa. The head chef, who honed his skills under the master of the Tokyo flagship, has perfected the art of chargrilling. Capons from Shaoxing are grilled over red hot bincho charcoal which seals in the juices and imparts smokiness. Set menus offer yakitori with or without alcohol and otoshi; à la carte skewers only after 8pm.

Name: Mi Shang
Address: 186 North Shaanxi Road, Jingan, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: Prada’s first standalone F&B outlet in Asia blends Milanese and Shanghainese cultures in its design and menu, hence the name ‘Mi Shang’. After years of renovation, the 1910s residence became the brand’s cultural hub and home to this restaurant and café. The Sicilian chef Riccardo La Perna reimagines Italian classics with exceptional skill and flair. His tagliolini lobster and the tiramisu with Marsala wine affogato are unmissable.

Name: The Meat
Address: 2F, Kerry Hotel Pudong, 1388 Huamu Road, Pudong, Shanghai, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Brunch,Car park,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: The gloriously blunt name chosen by this hotel restaurant says it all. The beef – mostly Australian, is served in a characteristic steakhouse vibe, sporting antlers and booth seating. On your way in, you will see the room where the beef is aged for 25 to 31 days. The signature dish is New York pressed steak: Aussie Tajima Wagyu sirloin smothered in melted Raclette cheese, served with bourbon whisky sauce.

Name: Biao Xia
Address: 3F, The Ritz-Carlton, 369 Guangji South Road, Gusu, Suzhou, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: The room inspired by classical Suzhou gardens serves refined Huai Yang and Jiangzhe cuisine. This cuisine has no secrets for chef Tian, whose father and grandfather were both Huai Yang chefs. Seasonal produce, freshwater fish and seafood are crafted into culinary gems depicted by light, umami flavours. His braised glass noodles with river shrimp, tomalley and roe are a nod to the classic shrimp trio noodles, with mild heat and acidity to cut through the richness.

Name: Fong Sense
Address: 1F, West Entrance, Marriott Nanjing South Hotel, 1519 Shuanglong Avenue, Jiangning, Nanjing, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Counter dining,Interesting wine list,Valet parking,Wheelchair access
Description: This restaurant on the shore of Baijia Lake is helmed by renowned chef Zhou who captures the quintessence of Huaiyang cuisine courtesy of modern French techniques and plating. His set menu mirrors the seasons and can include portabella consommé with crispy sesame twists and a tofu chrysanthemum that entails exquisite knife skills. The set menu comes with two or three cups of Chinese tea and wine pairing is also available.

Name: L'Arôme in the sky
Address: 50F, Golden Eagle World G Hotel, 888 Yingtian Street, Jianye, Nanjing, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Car park,Great view,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Perched atop a sky-scraping hotel tower, the lofty room affords stunning views of the city's skyline, complemented by blue fabric sculptures suspended from the white ceiling. The owner, a Nanjing native living in Paris, opened the restaurant in 2021 to serve refined French cooking with a modern twist in the form of a concise à la carte menu, six-course tasting menu and T-bone steak set. Save room for the millefeuille with caramel and raspberry jam.

Name: Xiu
Address: 1F, InterContinental, 288 Wangdun Road, Suzhou Industrial Park, Suzhou, Chinese Mainland
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Wheelchair access
Description: Xiu is the Chinese word for embroidery, an art form for which Suzhou is famous. It makes sense that silk upholstery adorns the room, a feature that exudes elegance. The menu is made up of Jiangzhe dishes and a few Sichuan and Cantonese choices. Seasonal ingredients are shipped from all over the country, astutely prepared and perfectly seasoned. The deep-fried "squirrel" mandarin fish in sweet and sour sauce looks as good as it tastes.

Name: Riva Lounge
FacilitiesAndServices: Air conditioning,Car park,Interesting wine list,Terrace,Wheelchair access
Description: Situated within the luxurious “adult only” Monte Mulini hotel, this restaurant with a beautiful terrace serves local cuisine prepared using regional produce alongside a few Asian ingredients – guests choose the number of courses they want from the menu. Careful attention is paid to wine pairing, with an impressive selection of local labels, including sparkling wines and a good choice of champagnes.
